Acadia is proud to have invested preferred equity in the JVR Energy Park alongside Wafra, supporting BayWa r.e. as they advance a project that will bring 125 MW of solar and 70 MW of battery storage to San Diego County. Once operational, JVR is expected to power about 57,000 homes and deliver lasting economic and community benefits. Congratulations to Benjamin Droz for leading Acadia's involvement, and to the BayWa r.e. team for reaching this milestone and continuing to drive clean energy development.

 The powerhouse lineup, including Danielle Decatur (Microsoft), Yinka N. text:
Creating greater resilience through AI

A key chapter in Actis’ Building Infrastructure Resilience white paper explores how AI is strengthening infrastructure resilience - all the way from origination through to operations.

AI-driven climate scenario modelling is accelerating due diligence and improving the quality of risk analysis for example. Before assets are even built, AI tools can model future climate and grid hazards to optimise siting, interconnection and design - trading modest upfront capex for meaningful reductions in long-term downtime.

Once operational, AI enhances resilience through predictive maintenance, anomaly detection, hyper-local weather forecasting and automated contract monitoring. In parallel, digital tools are improving build quality, permitting efficiency and commercial execution, while strengthening cyber and operational security.

We believe the outcome is a virtuous loop: smarter siting, stronger design, safer operations and more robust financing - all underpinned by data-driven decision making.

Access the report below.

#InfrastructureResilience #InfrastructureInvesting #SustainableInfrastructure
authorHeadline:
Actis is a leading growth market investor in sustainable infrastructure. 

Actis has been building critical infrastructure focused on essential services in growth markets for over 20 years. Today, it is one of the largest and longest-standing sustainable infrastructure investors globally.

Actis is a growth market specialist because this is where it sees the best risk adjusted returns. It has an industrialist mindset that brings deep operational expertise to every investment, and a focus on sustainability with the aim of mitigating risk, creating value and future-proofing assets.

Actis invests at scale and draws on a legacy of over 70 years. Since inception, it has raised circa US$26 billion.

In October 2024, Actis joined forces with General Atlantic, a leading global growth investor, creating a diversified, global investment platform with approximately US$108 billion in combined assets under management. Actis operates as General Atlantic’s sustainable infrastructure business. This strategic combination further enhances Actis’ focus as a leader in global sustainable infrastructure.

text:
Power plays: how insurers can invest in the energy transition

In InsuranceAUM.com's latest podcast, Actis’ Neda Vakilian and David Swift of General Atlantic joined Stewart Foley, CFA to discuss why we think growth markets energy infrastructure and scalable climate solutions are increasingly relevant for long-term insurance capital. This was a great conversation, with three powerful takeaways:

1) Core, growth market energy infrastructure creates the potential for alpha returns that so many insurance investors are seeking, thanks to the stronger growth and demand in these markets but also the mismatch between capital allocation and infrastructure supply, and demand for such infrastructure. Investors wanting equivalent returns from North American or Western European infrastructure need to take on more asset-level risk, in our view.

2) We think risk is best managed through disciplined underwriting and local execution, narrowing the gap between perceived and underwritten risk via long-term contracted cashflows and robust governance frameworks for example.

3) We believe sustainability is not an overlay but a driver of value, strengthening downside protection, enhancing exit optionality and supporting resilient, long-duration returns aligned with insurers’ liabilities.

Click the link below to listen to the conversation.

#Infrastructure #GrowthMarkets #Energy

We are proud to share news of this award which recognises what we believe to be one of the most ambitious renewable energy projects globally.

By combining a significant 3.5GW of solar PV with 4.5GWh of BESS, MTerra Solar aims to leapfrog the intermittency phase of renewables to deliver firm, baseload clean power. This will make it one of the world's largest integrated renewables and energy storage projects globally once complete.

On 14 February, the project's first phase reached a major milestone with the successful synchronisation and energisation with the Luzon grid and with 1,288MW of solar PV and 622 BESS units installed to date, just 15 months after ground-breaking.

Congratulations to our Southeast Asia energy team, the MTerra Solar team and Meralco PowerGen (MGEN) on this award.

#SustainableInfrastructure #Energy #InfrastructureInvesting

text:
Resilience is fast becoming a prerequisite for insurance - not a nice to have.
 
A key chapter in Actis’ Building Infrastructure Resilience report examines how rising climate risk is reshaping the insurance landscape for infrastructure assets.
 
Natural disasters caused over US$131 billion in losses in 2025 globally and a 60% global protection gap persists for climate-related insurance. As insured losses climb and premiums rise, the challenge is no longer just affordability, it is availability of cover. 
 
We are already seeing this shift in practice. Some Actis portfolio companies have been able to negotiate lower insurance costs by demonstrating tangible investment in resilience. Looking ahead, insurers are increasingly requiring evidence of adaptation and risk mitigation as a condition for underwriting.
 
The message ought to be clear: investing in resilience is not only about protecting asset value and operational continuity, it is becoming essential to securing insurance and safeguarding long-term returns. This is what our sustainability and operations teams are setting out to achieve.
 
#InfrastructureInvesting #InfrastructureResilience #SustainableInfrastructure

text:
Asia’s new-economy real estate: unlocking structural growth

In a recent Institutional Real Estate, Inc. (IREI). article, Brian Chinappi, Global Head of Real Estate and Data Centers at Actis, sets out why we believe Asia represents one of the most compelling global opportunities in new-economy real estate.
 
Brian highlights how structural forces - digitalisation, supply chain transformation, urbanisation and the climate transition - are driving durable demand for assets such as data centres, logistics facilities and advanced industrial platforms. In Asia, these dynamics are amplified by demographic growth, rising consumption and accelerated technological adoption.
 
Data centers exemplify this convergence. With Asia Pacific data center capacity forecast to grow at a 21% CAGR and power demand from these assets set to surge, we think success requires more than just capital, it demands operational excellence, a combination of energy and real estate expertise, and a deep knowledge of local markets.
 
We believe that investors that fit this profile will be best positioned to capture this exciting growth opportunity.
 
#InfrastructureInvesting #SustainableInfrastructure #DataCenters

text:
At Actis, we are builders and operators. That is not a slogan - it is how we aim to invest.
 
In growth markets, execution is crucial. Our experience tells us that our in-house operations capability is key to delivering projects on time and on budget, even through challenging cycles. It allows us to assess assets through an industrial lens, endeavor to identify true value, and enhance operational performance to maximise long-term yield.
 
This hands-on approach, combining investment discipline with deep operational expertise, is central to how we seek to create resilient infrastructure platforms.
 
As Barry Lynch notes in the video, we think “our ships are built for stormier seas” thanks to this approach.
 
Watch the video to see how we put this into practice.
 
#GrowthMarkets #Infrastructure #SustainableInfrastructure

Actis has been building critical infrastructure focused on essential services in growth markets for over 20 years. Today, it is one of the largest and longest-standing sustainable infrastructure investors globally.

Actis is a growth market specialist because this is where it sees the best risk adjusted returns. It has an industrialist mindset that brings deep operational expertise to every investment, and a focus on sustainability with the aim of mitigating risk, creating value and future-proofing assets.

Actis invests at scale and draws on a legacy of over 70 years. Since inception, it has raised circa US$26 billion.

In October 2024, Actis joined forces with General Atlantic, a leading global growth investor, creating a diversified, global investment platform with approximately US$108 billion in combined assets under management. Actis operates as General Atlantic’s sustainable infrastructure business. This strategic combination further enhances Actis’ focus as a leader in global sustainable infrastructure.

text:
MTerra Solar reaches a critical milestone in the Philippines
 
Actis is pleased to mark the successful initial grid synchronisation and energisation of MTerra Solar Phase 1, achieved just 15 months after ground-breaking.
 
With 1,288MWdc of installed solar capacity and 622 battery energy storage units already in place, MTerra Solar is now safely connected to the Luzon grid - a major step toward delivering firm, reliable renewable power at scale in the Philippines. Actis' investment in MTerra Solar represented the largest FDI in a single project for greenfield infrastructure in the Philippines at the time of deal completion.
 
Once fully completed, MTerra Solar is expected to become one of the world’s largest integrated solar PV and battery storage facilities. The project combines large-scale solar generation with storage to support grid stability and strengthen the Philippines’ energy security.
 
As Barry Lynch, Actis’ Head of Operations, Energy Infrastructure, noted: “The whole energy industry globally is watching this project.”
 
We are proud to partner with Meralco PowerGen (MGEN) and work with National Grid Corporation of the Philippines in delivering infrastructure of national and global significance, demonstrating that large-scale, resilient renewable energy can be built efficiently and at speed.
 
#InfrastructureInvesting #SustainableInfrastructure #Infrastructure

text:
Last week, Actis' Sherif H. Elkholy, Ph.D., spoke at the Mergermarket AVCJ Private Equity Forum Abu Dhabi 2026 on a panel looking at the expanding energy and digital infrastructure growth in the Middle East and across the world. Key takeaways from Sherif’s discussion included:
 
- The Gulf’s energy transition gathering pace, with a visible opening for the private sector to lead renewable investment in the region and the Middle East becoming an increasingly compelling destination for private capital.
 
- The pairing of renewables with battery energy storage systems is central to the next phase of the clean energy deployment. Falling battery costs are enabling renewables to deliver reliable baseload power - a trend predicted to accelerate globally.
 
- In digital infrastructure, Sherif believes a clear supply-demand imbalance is emerging. Generational AI and cloud growth are driving significant new-build requirements, with power availability the critical enabler. This creates an exciting opportunity for investors capable of building and operating energy and digital infrastructure at scale, which is key to attracting hyperscale data center tenants.
 
- Global knowledge transfer matters, and Actis’ cross-border operational expertise and platform approach provides tangible advantages in scaling complex assets.

#SustainableInfrastructure #InfrastructureInvesting #Infrastructure

text:
TERRANOVA launches first operational data center in Mexico
 
TERRANOVA, the new Latin America hyperscale data center platform created by Actis, has reached a significant milestone with the inauguration of its first operational data center in Mexico.
 
The site begins operations with a total 8.12MW of IT capacity following a swift 12-month project development and has been built to evolve in stages, ready to scale to a 20MW capacity.
 
Mauricio Giusti, Managing Director, Digital Infrastructure at Actis, commented: "This is an exciting step in TERRANOVA's next phase of digital growth. We see strong synergies between our existing data center operations in Asia and TERRANOVA’s growth in Latin America. This collaboration allows us to deliver scalable, energy-efficient, and sustainable solutions for global and regional clients."
 
#SustainableInfrastructure #InfrastructureInvesting #DigitalInfrastructure

text:
Last week, we welcomed the global sovereign wealth fund community to Abu Dhabi as ADIA and Mubadala co-hosted the IFSWF (International Forum of Sovereign Wealth Funds) Annual Meeting, held under the patronage of His Highness Sheikh Khaled bin Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, Crown Prince of Abu Dhabi and Chairman of the Abu Dhabi Executive Council.

The meeting's theme was "Building Resilience in an Era of Transformation”. Discussions reinforced the important role sovereign wealth funds play in enhancing long-term market stability and highlighted the significant opportunities that lie ahead.

ADIA was involved in the creation of the IFSWF in 2009 and currently holds a Board position, as it continues to play an active role in IFSWF's work to promote good governance, accountability and responsible investment practices.
authorHeadline:
Since 1976, the Abu Dhabi Investment Authority (ADIA) has been prudently investing funds on behalf of the Government of Abu Dhabi, with a focus on long-term value creation.

Our portfolio comprises more than two dozen asset classes and subcategories, from equities and fixed income to hedge funds, real estate, private equity and infrastructure. ADIA invests in all of the world’s major markets, in addition to having significant exposure to the developing markets that will drive global growth of the future.

Our people are as international as our investments, bringing together an array of skills and experiences to create a collaborative and stimulating workplace. ADIA combines a culturally diverse workforce, made up of more than 65 nationalities, with a global network of relationships with the world’s top investment institutions and thought leaders.

ADIA provides a dynamic work environment, where robust debate is balanced by shared values and a willingness to learn and adapt to a changing environment, and where committed people can build rewarding careers while contributing to ADIA’s continued long-term success. We place a particular emphasis on the development and training of UAE Nationals, many of whom currently, or will go on to, hold prominent roles within ADIA and in the UAE.

ADIA seeks the best global talent, across disciplines and geographies, and provides them with the resources they need to thrive and build successful careers.

Community Guidelines: ADIA reserves its right to remove content that is inappropriate, off-topic, or unprofessional.

Important Notice: ADIA does not ask individuals to provide sensitive documents or to register for training courses via LinkedIn or other social media platforms as part of the recruitment process.  If you receive any suspicious requests claiming to be from ADIA employees, we strongly advise you to exercise caution and refrain from sharing personal or sensitive information.

text:
ADIA recently joined 42 other sovereign wealth funds, asset managers and private equity funds at the 2025 One Planet Sovereign Wealth Funds (OPSWF) CEO Summit, held from 22-23 October in Paris.

ADIA was a founder member of OPSWF in 2017. It was created to support sovereign wealth funds in expanding investment opportunities in new energy solutions; accelerate efforts to integrate material financial risks and opportunities related to climate change into the management of large, long-term asset pools, and; leverage the scale and benefits of knowledge-sharing, while preserving flexibility and agility.

This year's OPSWF CEO Summit included three key announcements: 

1. Growing consensus among Sovereign Wealth Funds: OPSWF and IFSWF released joint findings showing that nearly 70 percent of sovereign wealth funds surveyed believe integration of material climate factors enhances long-term, risk-adjusted returns, reinforcing the financial case for sustainability.

2. Launch of Practical Frameworks: Members unveiled the Value Creation Framework and the Real Estate Decarbonisation Playbook, providing actionable tools to reduce risk, improve efficiency, and strengthen portfolio resilience.

3. Advancing Innovation: The Network announced new workstreams on AI-enabled investment analysis and renewable-energy platforms in emerging markets, advancing investment decision-making through data and innovation.

For more information on OPSWF, please visit its website: https://oneplanetswfs.org/

text:
As technology reshapes the investment landscape, new skills are needed to capture fast-moving opportunities in increasingly efficient markets.
 
Over recent years, we have significantly deepened our data analytics and quantitative investment capabilities, and advanced our science-driven investment approach, while deploying new tools across the organisation to analyse and derive insights from data.
 
At ADIA, we believe that success in financial markets is directly correlated with our ability to continuously learn and evolve in a dynamic, rapidly-changing environment.

authorTitle:
Since 1976, the Abu Dhabi Investment Authority (ADIA) has been prudently investing funds on behalf of the Government of Abu Dhabi, with a focus on long-term value creation.

Our portfolio comprises more than two dozen asset classes and subcategories, from equities and fixed income to hedge funds, real estate, private equity and infrastructure. ADIA invests in all of the world’s major markets, in addition to having significant exposure to the developing markets that will drive global growth of the future.

Our people are as international as our investments, bringing together an array of skills and experiences to create a collaborative and stimulating workplace. ADIA combines a culturally diverse workforce, made up of more than 65 nationalities, with a global network of relationships with the world’s top investment institutions and thought leaders.

ADIA provides a dynamic work environment, where robust debate is balanced by shared values and a willingness to learn and adapt to a changing environment, and where committed people can build rewarding careers while contributing to ADIA’s continued long-term success. We place a particular emphasis on the development and training of UAE Nationals, many of whom currently, or will go on to, hold prominent roles within ADIA and in the UAE.

ADIA seeks the best global talent, across disciplines and geographies, and provides them with the resources they need to thrive and build successful careers.

Community Guidelines: ADIA reserves its right to remove content that is inappropriate, off-topic, or unprofessional.

Important Notice: ADIA does not ask individuals to provide sensitive documents or to register for training courses via LinkedIn or other social media platforms as part of the recruitment process.  If you receive any suspicious requests claiming to be from ADIA employees, we strongly advise you to exercise caution and refrain from sharing personal or sensitive information.

text:
The global economy is becoming increasingly fragmented. Tariffs, reshoring, and rising defence expenditures are replacing the once-prevailing narrative of open markets and global supply chains, prompting a shift towards greater national self-reliance.

In a month, you can hear firsthand from Marta Perez, CIO Infrastructure, at #IIGS | Infrastructure Investor. She will join a panel discussion on one of the key themes of deglobalisation - AI infrastructure investing and its efficiency.

🕓 Panel detail: 24 March 2026 (Day 1) at 16:10 CET, Forum 1
🗓️ Visit our hospitality lounge in Hall 3 or sign up for a meeting with our investment experts at #IIGS: https://ow.ly/NsY650YcGza
🤝 Connect to participate at our evening networking event on 24 March at Berlin’s China Club: Email events@allianzgi.com if you want to join us!

Marta has also examined the question, “Is deglobalisation the answer to volatility for infrastructure investors?”, addressing the challenges and opportunities for investors in this evolving landscape. 🔗 Download the insight paper: https://ow.ly/IRQG50YcGz9

#PrivateMarkets #Infrastructure #Deglobalisation #AI #Digitalisation
Investment involves risk. For fund distributors and professional investors only.

🐎 Chinese equities: Has the rally peaked or is 2026 the real opportunity? William Russell, Head of Product Specialist Equity AP, sat down with Asset TV for a comprehensive outlook on the breadth of opportunities in China equities as we step into the Year of the Horse, examining the strengthened foundations for further growth. Easing macro headwinds, government policy support in the property sector, and accelerating AI & tech innovation are setting the stage. Plus, shifting global allocations and a recalibrated risk premium could ignite renewed interest and confidence.

💡Watch the full video here to gain insights: https://ow.ly/TjAU50YeNB7

#ChinaEquities #ChinaA #China #YearofHorse

text:
Several members of the Amber team participated in the FT Live: Central and Eastern European Forum in Vienna earlier this month. The Forum welcomed over 2,600 delegates to celebrate the CEE region on the global stage. Amber's CIO, Amanda Woods, spoke alongside finance ministers and global investors on the Rebuilding Ukraine panel. Additionally, Christian Roy, Amber's Head of CEE contributed to the Funding Resilience: Energy Security and Infrastructure Development panel alongside industry peers and representatives from a number of Three Seas Initiative Investment Fund (3SIIF)'s portfolio companies: R.Power Renewables and Enery. 

#CEE #infrastructure
authorHeadline: Amber is a specialist international investment manager, focused on investment origination, asset management and fund management. Amber currently manages or advises 9 funds (2 listed and 7 private) with over £5 billion (c.$6 billion) in funds under management.  With a presence across 12 countries, Amber manages 175 infrastructure investments representing total assets under management of £14 billion (c. $17.5billion). Amber’s core business focuses on managing infrastructure assets across the public, transport, energy, digital and demographic infrastructure sectors internationally. Amber is headquartered in London with offices in Europe, North America, Australia and New Zealand. Amber employs over 180 professionals globally.

text:
We were delighted to see Ian Cowie, Columnist at The Sunday Times and interactive investor, recognise Amber advised International Public Partnerships (INPP) as one of the “six income investment trusts for the long term.” Ian highlights INPP’s investment in Tideway London, the 15-mile super-sewer, and its role in significantly reducing pollution in the River Thames, with an estimated 12.9m tonnes prevented from entering the river since 2024. You can read the full piece here: https://lnkd.in/epww7DBd

A timely moment too, as it is great to see the former Tideway London construction site reopen as the new Bazalgette Embankment. This fantastic public space aims to reconnect Londoners with the Thames and demonstrates what thoughtful engineering and long-term investment can deliver.

Not a recommendation for investment. Capital at risk.

#infrastructure #investment #inpp #tideway

Amber-advised International Public Partnerships (INPP) announces that is has agreed to sell a minority stake of its investment in the Moray East OFTO to Daiwa Energy & Infrastructure Co. Ltd. This takes the total proceeds raised from realisations to over £130m during 2025 and c.£387m since July 2023 (equivalent to 14% of the portfolio as at 30 June 2025), with all realisations either being in line with, or at a premium to, the relevant published valuations. text:
Amber-advised International Public Partnerships (INPP) released its latest Portfolio Update covering the period 1 July – 12 December 2025. INPP’s 130+ asset portfolio continued to perform well and in line with expectations. The portfolio delivers essential services to stakeholders, maintaining availability levels that remain at or above target levels. Key highlights include:
 
- £70m+ share buybacks ( £114m since launch), generating +1.3p per share of NAV accretion
- £85m+ asset realisations, all at or above the most recently published valuations ( £345m+ since July 2023)
- c.£40m selective reinvestment, including first capital into Sizewell C, the UK’s next nuclear project using the RAB model
 
The INPP Board has increased and extended the buyback programme up to £225m through March 2027, reinforcing confidence in value and alignment with shareholders.
 
Read the full Portfolio Update here: https://lnkd.in/e-7ZmBGZ
 
Learn more about INPP here: https://lnkd.in/gQJbrbHM
 
Capital at risk. Not a recommendation for investment.

text:
Amber's Christian Roy, Head of CEE, shared his insights during a panel following the launch of Bank Gospodarstwa Krajowego and the Polish Institute of Economy’s report, “Investments as the foundation of a new model for Poland’s development.”

The discussion highlighted the central role of infrastructure in driving and sustaining Poland’s growth, and the need to facilitate the mobilisation of private capital, especially from Polish pension funds and insurance companies. Christian underlined why unlocking private investment is essential to meeting the country’s long-term infrastructure needs.

text:
Join Amber and Placecap for a webinar exploring why Central and Eastern Europe (CEE) is emerging as one of the most compelling regions for institutional infrastructure investments.

With faster growth and less competition than Western Europe as well as a pressing need for high-quality infrastructure, the CEE presents a rare opportunity to deploy capital at scale, combining returns and diversification. In this session, Amber’s Head of CEE Christian Roy will discuss the macroeconomic drivers, the infrastructure gap, and the strategic importance of “boots on the ground” in sourcing, executing, and managing investments. 

We welcome institutional investors to register here:https://lnkd.in/ehV7AUrZ to attend the webinar on Friday 28 November.

text:
We recently hosted our inaugural Portfolio Leadership and Value Creation Forum, bringing together senior leaders from across our portfolio companies for a day of discussion, insight-sharing and collaboration. 💡🤝

Held at 8 Bishopsgate in London, the event welcomed more than 30 CEOs and CFOs from 18 Ancala portfolio companies. They were joined by Ancala’s asset management team, Partners, Industry Partners and function heads across ESG, debt financing and marketing and communications, as well as Jonathan White, a Partner at KPMG LLP.

The agenda focused on creating and protecting value and combined Ancala perspectives, external expertise and success stories from across the portfolio, covering areas including debt strategy, exit planning, marketing and communications, ESG and project delivery.

text:
Ancala has appointed Heike Bergmann and Horst-Tore Land as Industry Partners, further strengthening our differentiated investment strategy and operational expertise.

Ancala's Industry Partners are former CEOs, chairs and senior executives from across key infrastructure sectors and are fully integrated members of the team. They receive a salary, carried interest and co-invest in the Ancala funds, ensuring alignment with investors and management teams. 

The Industry Partners work alongside Ancala’s investment, asset management and portfolio company teams across investment sourcing, due diligence, asset management and supporting exits. They sit on our Investment Committee and contribute directly to decision-making, execution and long-term value creation.

Spence Clunie, Managing Partner, Ancala, commented:
“Our Industry Partners are a key feature of our differentiated strategy to deliver enhanced risk-adjusted returns from critical mid-market infrastructure assets.

“Heike and Tore bring outstanding, complementary operational experience that will be applied across the entire investment cycle. Their appointments further strengthen our ability to assess opportunities, execute complex transactions and work alongside management teams to create long-term value.”

Read more: https://lnkd.in/eu_R3JMv

text:
New investment update: Ancala has acquired a majority stake in MUCH Gruppe, a specialist provider of temporary modular building, related accessories and services and broader infrastructure solutions.

Headquartered in Limburg an der Lahn, Germany, MUCH Gruppe provides its temporary space and modular solutions to defence, government and emergency response sectors. Its turnkey solutions include camps, courthouses, corporate, industrial and education sector solutions and emergency medical and crisis response accommodation.

Ancala will apply its highly proactive asset management approach to enable the management team to further expand its fleet and enhance its technical capabilities, broaden its customer base across Germany and international markets and explore complementary acquisitions.

Manuel Much, CEO, MUCH Gruppe, comments: “We recognised the importance of partnering with a leading infrastructure manager to provide us with the capital and expertise to scale further, invest in our fleet and provide even better and broader solutions to our growing customer base. Ancala’s understanding of our family business and our specialised solutions made them the natural partner for our next chapter. We are excited about the opportunities our new partnership will unlock.”

Luke Rotondella, Director, Ancala, adds: “MUCH Gruppe offers one of the most comprehensive temporary space and modular infrastructure solutions in the DACH region, combining quality, deployment speed and deep operational expertise in highly specialised environments. Bernd, Manuel and the broader team have built a best-in-class operator in a specialised market segment defined by strong tailwinds and high barriers to entry.

“The company is well-placed to support Germany’s €500bn infrastructure upgrade through its leading temporary space solutions. We are already working closely with the team to scale the business further and enhance its market-leading position.”
  
Read more: https://lnkd.in/e67c34eB

text:
New investment update: Ancala has acquired a portfolio of critical pipeline infrastructure from Hexion Inc.

The portfolio of pipeline-connected sites and storage facilities for the production, storage and transportation of critical chemical intermediates will be operated by Valentra, a new company created with associates from the former division that operated the sites at Hexion. Valentra’s assets are located in Baytown, Texas; Luling, Louisiana; and Geismar, Louisiana. 

Alexandru Mihut, former Vice President of Ascend Performance Materials' Global Performance and Specialty Chemicals Business is joining Valentra as CEO on December 8, 2025. He will oversee the transition to a standalone platform and lead Valentra going forwards. 

Alex and the Valentra team will deliver continuity of operations and best-in-class service for its customers and broader stakeholders.

Lee Mellor, Partner, Ancala, comments: “Valentra’s assets play a critical role in supporting leading global chemicals companies in manufacturing chemicals that are essential to a diverse range of end markets. The portfolio’s infrastructure qualities, well invested asset base and long-standing customer relationships make it a natural fit for Ancala’s strategy.

“We will be working closely with Alex and the Valentra team to build on the portfolio’s strong operational track record.”

Alexandru Mihut, CEO, Valentra, adds: “We are very pleased to be partnering with Ancala to build a platform for long-term growth - one that would allow us to expand our capabilities and bring even greater value to the markets we serve. I am excited to lead this next chapter and look forward to the opportunities ahead for our customers, our team and our business.”
  
Read more: https://lnkd.in/eHbsw6Yc

Ancala was awarded 𝐈𝐧𝐧𝐨𝐯𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐞 𝐒𝐦𝐚𝐫𝐭 𝐈𝐧𝐟𝐫𝐚𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐮𝐜𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞 𝐃𝐞𝐚𝐥 𝐨𝐟 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐘𝐞𝐚𝐫 at The inspiratia European Energy Transition Awards 2025 for its acquisitions of German smart metering businesses HAUSHELD AG and Solandeo GmbH, and the creation of Germany’s leading independent smart metering platform. 🏆

The Inspiratia judging panel noted that Ancala’s creation the independent smart metering platform “stands out for strengthening the foundations for Europe’s emerging smart infrastructure landscape through a sustainable data driven system.” 

The judges added: “Ancala’s investment supports Germany’s nationwide smart meter rollout, enhances grid visibility and enables more flexible energy usage for households, business and decentralised assets.”

Ancala is actively working with the smart metering platform to deliver on its existing order book and secure more orders. The platform has more than doubled contracted orders to over 500,000 since Ancala first invested in 2024.

text:
Antin Infrastructure Partners has entered into exclusive negotiations to acquire Belambra, a French leader in leisure infrastructure that owns and operates a diverse portfolio of holiday clubs. The investment is made by Antin’s Mid Cap Fund I.

Belambra is a leading French operator of all-inclusive holiday clubs. Founded in 1959, Belambra’s holiday clubs benefit from large infrastructure and common areas, and provide a full-service offering including accommodation, food, activities and sports. 

Belambra highlights many strong and appealing infrastructure features that Antin seeks in its Mid Cap strategy, including a high-quality portfolio that is difficult to replicate due to the scarcity of such prime site locations. Belambra serves clients for whom holidays are increasingly essential, with a focus on domestic destinations, playing a key role in enabling customers to unwind and recharge. Belambra benefits from a leading position in a steadily growing market. 

Full press release here: https://lnkd.in/ey-b6dr3

text:
Antin Infrastructure Partners has agreed to acquire Emsere, a global leader in clinical trial equipment rental infrastructure. This marks the eighth investment by Antin’s €2.2 billion Mid Cap Fund I.

Based in Leiden, the Netherlands, Emsere provides contract research organisations (CROs) and pharmaceutical companies with specialised medical equipment such as centrifuges, vital signs devices, cardiovascular and respiratory monitoring machines, imaging devices and cardio-pulmonary exercise testing. 

Emsere has over 13,000 pieces of equipment in its portfolio and delivers to over 100 countries through its own distribution centres and third-party relationships, making it a globally consolidated solution for all clinical trial equipment needs. Customers include six of the top 10 global pharmaceutical companies and seven of the top 10 CROs globally.
 
Full press release here: https://lnkd.in/e_F6UXjd

text:
Antin Infrastructure Partners has agreed to acquire NorthC Group Datacenters, a leading enterprise colocation data centre platform in Northwest Europe, from DWS Group.

Based in Amsterdam, NorthC operates 25 colocation data centres across major metropolitan areas in the Netherlands, Germany and Switzerland, and is developing six new sites in response to strong customer demand. 

Under the leadership of CEO Alexandra Schless, the company has grown rapidly since 2019, becoming a key partner to more than 1,600 blue-chip customers across cloud, IT services providers, carriers, public sector and financial institutions, industry, transport and the healthcare and pharmaceutical sectors.

Upon closing, this investment will mark the sixth by Antin’s €10.2 billion Flagship Fund V and further strengthens Antin’s expertise in high-growth digital infrastructure. The acquisition builds on our strong track record in this sector, following successful partnerships such as Pulsant.

Full press release here: https://lnkd.in/e9iJUdvE

text:
Antin Infrastructure Partners is thrilled to celebrate the tenth anniversary of its partnership with The Sutton Trust, the UK’s leading social mobility charity. Since 2015, Antin and Sutton Trust have worked together to help less advantaged young students access higher education to develop their full potential and enhance their career prospects.

At the anniversary event in Antin’s London office, Mark Crosbie, Antin Co-Founder and Vice-Chairman of the Board, Simon Söder, Antin Senior Partner and Head of the London office, and Nick Harrison, CEO of The Sutton Trust, reflected on the achievements made possible through this collaboration. Sutton Trust alumni also shared how this initiative has influenced their journeys, showing the real difference this partnership makes. Many thanks to Chibi Ajero, Maddy Silva, Ayllin Schoengut and Dani Y. for their compelling testimonials. 

Education opens the door to opportunities and enables students to contribute to a prosperous, healthy society. We remain committed to this vision and look forward to many more years to come of a successful partnership with Sutton Trust.

#infrastructure #socialmobility #partnership #education #community

text:
[3Q 2025 Activity Update] Antin Infrastructure Partners announced its 3Q 2025 Activity Update this morning and reported good momentum in capital deployment and value creation.
 
Alain Rauscher, Chairman and CEO, stated:
 
“Antin showed strong momentum in the third quarter, with three new investments in rapid succession in our Mid Cap and NextGen funds, while AUM continued to grow. At the same time, our diversified portfolio continues to show its resilience and create value, with all our funds performing on or ahead of plan. In an environment that remains uncertain but is showing signs of stabilisation, Antin remains focused on deploying its capital, improving operational performance in its existing portfolio and preparing exits from Flagship Fund III and Fund III-B. Our proven ability to perform well across market cycles should again serve us well, and I’m confident that Antin is well positioned to continue delivering value for its stakeholders in the near and medium term.”

The full press release is available at: https://lnkd.in/dBKEzvGk

text:
Antin Infrastructure Partners has agreed to acquire a majority stake in Swiftair S.A. Group, a leading European provider of aircraft leasing and air transportation solutions for time-critical cargo. 

Headquartered in Madrid and led by CEO Salvador Moreno, who remains a significant shareholder, Swiftair plays a vital role in the logistical supply chains of its blue-chip global clients.

The company has demonstrated strong growth through both organic expansion and market consolidation over recent years.

This investment represents the seventh by Antin’s Mid Cap Fund I and aligns with our strategy of supporting established infrastructure businesses in Europe and North America.

Francisco Cabeza and Simon Söder, respectively Partner and Senior Partner at Antin Infrastructure Partners, commented: "It is a true honour for us to partner with Salvador Moreno, an exceptional entrepreneur who has made a leader of Swiftair, to help the company reach the next level. We look forward to working with Salvador and Swiftair's team to support the company's continued expansion journey and the needs of its customers."

Read more at: https://lnkd.in/ddu5MrFJ

Antin Infrastructure Partners has signed a binding agreement to acquire Aquavista Watersides & Marinas, the UK's largest marina infrastructure provider.

Founded in 2003 and based in Nottingham, Aquavista operates 32 inland and coastal marinas, offering over 5,300 berths and marine services across the country.

Upon closing, this investment will be the sixth by Antin’s €2.2 billion Mid Cap Fund I.

We look forward to partnering with CEO Steve de Polo and the Aquavista team on their next chapter.

authorHeadline:
APG is a pension provider that provides the pensions for more than 4.8 million people on behalf of several Dutch pension funds. Not only looking for financial returns, but also at doing our bit for society. Because pensions are ultimately about people, about life, and about living together in society.

APG Asset Management is one of the largest pension investors in the world and a leading global, long-term investor. We manage in total a value of over 521 billion euros.* To manage these assets as responsibly as possible, it is our ambition to achieve attractive and sustainable investment returns for our customers, in a responsible way. So that we can ensure a good and affordable pension. For current and future generations.

*APG Group Annual Report 2023

authorHeadline:
APG is a pension provider that provides the pensions for more than 4.8 million people on behalf of several Dutch pension funds. Not only looking for financial returns, but also at doing our bit for society. Because pensions are ultimately about people, about life, and about living together in society.

APG Asset Management is one of the largest pension investors in the world and a leading global, long-term investor. We manage in total a value of over 521 billion euros.* To manage these assets as responsibly as possible, it is our ambition to achieve attractive and sustainable investment returns for our customers, in a responsible way. So that we can ensure a good and affordable pension. For current and future generations.

*APG Group Annual Report 2023

authorHeadline:
Apollo is a high-growth, global alternative asset manager. In our asset management business, we seek to provide our clients excess return at every point along the risk-reward spectrum from investment grade credit to private equity. For more than three decades, our investing expertise across our fully integrated platform has served the financial return needs of our clients and provided businesses with innovative capital solutions for growth. Through Athene, our retirement services business, we specialize in helping clients achieve financial security by providing a suite of retirement savings products and acting as a solutions provider to institutions. Our patient, creative, and knowledgeable approach to investing aligns our clients, businesses we invest in, our employees, and the communities we impact, to expand opportunity and achieve positive outcomes. To learn more, please visit www.apollo.com.
 
Nothing herein constitutes an offer to sell, or a solicitation of an offer to buy, any security or product of Apollo or any Apollo-managed fund.

text:
Every other year, we convene our entire global partnership in a dynamic market to challenge our thinking and engage in a lively discussion on our strategy. This year, that landed us in Tokyo as Japan enters a new era of corporate reforms, evolving growth strategies and demographic shifts reshaping the economic landscape.

Swipe through for a glimpse of our global partnership on the ground across Asia Pacific, from Sydney to Singapore, culminating in our time together in Tokyo.

text:
We’re pleased to announce the promotion of James Chiu to Partner, Energy. 

With 20+ years in energy and finance, James will help lead Ara Energy’s strategy and execution across delivery and distribution, power, and biofuels—acquiring, operating, and optimizing essential assets while advancing economics‑led decarbonization.
 
👏Please join us in congratulating James!
 
Read the full release: https://lnkd.in/g3BrG5Cd
authorHeadline: Founded in 2017, Ara Partners is a global private markets firm focused on decarbonizing the industrial economy. We invest in the middle market across three strategies: Private Equity, Infrastructure, and Energy. We scale commercially demonstrated decarbonization solutions, support the businesses and infrastructure that enable their adoption, and reduce emissions at the source across the conventional energy value chain. Our model combines investing, market and policy expertise, project execution and operational optimization, and rigorous carbon accounting to reduce emissions economically and unlock growth at an industrial scale. Ara operates from Houston, Boston, Dublin and Washington D.C., and, as of September 30, 2025, had approximately $6.6 billion in assets under management.

text:
We’re excited for Churchill George Yong, Partner and Co‑Head of Infrastructure at Ara Partners, to join the 2026 MIT Sloan Investment Conference as a speaker on the Infrastructure panel. 

Against the conference theme of the intersection of innovation and instability, George will share Ara’s perspective on how disciplined investing can build resilient infrastructure platforms and drive value creation amid the energy and industrial transition.

text:
Ara Partners is pleased to announce a new cross‑platform partnership with Liberty Mutual Investments. This partnership brings strategic capital to Ara’s Energy and Infrastructure platforms and allows the firms to pursue opportunities together across both mandates.

By combining Ara’s builder/operator model with LMI’s broad reach across industrial, real estate, and infrastructure ecosystems, we see a significant opportunity to unlock value, accelerate growth, and advance the next generation of energy and infrastructure.

For more details, read the full press release: https://lnkd.in/gn9HuG_Y

Our model combines investing, market and policy expertise, project execution and operational optimization, and rigorous carbon accounting to reduce emissions economically and unlock growth at an industrial scale. Ara operates from Houston, Boston, Dublin and Washington D.C., and, as of September 30, 2025, had approximately $6.6 billion in assets under management.

text:
Cement produces roughly 8% of global CO₂. What if waste glass could help change that?
 
Cement is both the backbone of global infrastructure and one of the world’s largest emitters of CO₂. 
 
Our white paper explores ground glass pozzolan (GGP) as a scalable supplementary cementitious material (SCM)—linking lower carbon intensity with supply chain resilience, performance enhancement, and circular value.
 
Download the full analysis here: https://lnkd.in/eWJnPepb
 
Through Microtec Development & Holdings, an Ara portfolio company that upcycles waste glass into high‑performance GGP, we’re helping develop domestic, reliable SCM supply to support cost and performance outcomes at scale.
 
Learn more: https://lnkd.in/gEry92jM

text:
Last week, Ara and portfolio company executive teams joined Canada's federal investment promotion agency, Invest in Canada | Investir au Canada.

The discussion gave us a clear view of where Canada is putting real weight behind strategic sectors—and how those priorities translate into concrete programs, incentives, and capital pathways for scaling companies.

Thank you to the Ara Government Affairs team for their hard work helping our portfolio navigate complex government programs and turn them into real opportunities for growth and investment.

En français:
La semaine dernière, Ara et les équipes dirigeantes de ses sociétés en portefeuille ont rencontré Investir au Canada, l’agence fédérale de promotion des investissements.

Ces échanges ont permis à Ara et à ses partenaires d'obtenir une vision claire des secteurs stratégiques prioritaires au pays, tout en précisant comment ces priorités se traduisent en programmes concrets, en mesures incitatives et en accès au capital pour les entreprises en pleine croissance. 

Un grand merci à l’équipe des affaires gouvernementales d’Ara pour son travail acharné. Leur soutien est essentiel pour aider notre portefeuille à naviguer dans les programmes publics complexes et à les transformer en véritables leviers de croissance et d'investissement.

Congratulations to the team at ArcLight portfolio company Elevate Renewables on its acquisition of Prospect Power, the largest standalone battery storage asset in the PJM Interconnection. 

We believe battery storage has the potential to be transformative, and together with Elevate, we are well positioned to meet the growing need for cost-effective grid reliability and sustainability driven by power demand.

authorHeadline: ArcLight is a leading infrastructure investor which has been investing in critical electrification infrastructure since its founding in 2001. ArcLight has owned, controlled or operated over ~65 GW of assets and 47,000 miles of electric and gas transmission and storage infrastructure representing $80 billion of enterprise value. ArcLight has a long and proven track record of value-added investing across its core investment sectors including power, hydro, solar, wind, battery storage, electric transmission and natural gas transmission and storage infrastructure to support the growing need for power, reliability, security, and sustainability. ArcLight's team employs an operationally intensive investment approach that benefits from its dedicated in-house strategic, technical, operational, and commercial specialists, as well as the firm's ~1,900-person asset management partner.

text:
Congratulations to ArcLight strategic operating partner CAMS for being awarded the 2025 Gold HIRE Vets Medallion by the U.S. Department of Labor. The HIRE Vets Medallion Program is the only federal-level employment award that honors a company’s commitment to veteran hiring, retention, and professional development. 

ArcLight is proud to have a longstanding working relationship with CAMS and its employees, who provide invaluable information technology, environmental health and safety, ESG, engineering, due diligence, and general operating services to a majority of our critical energy infrastructure portfolio assets.

#VeteranEmployment #EnergyInfrastructure #EnergyOperations

text:
As data center and AI-driven power demand continues to rise, access to reliable power is emerging as the primary bottleneck. 
 
At ArcLight, we remain focused on investing in the critical electrification infrastructure assets necessary to support AI and data center demand across the power, renewables, battery storage, transmission, natural gas transmission and storage, and digital power sectors.
 
#InfrastructureInvesting #DataCenters #Energy

text:
We are pleased to announce the appointment of Angelo Acconcia as President of ArcLight. This promotion is a continuation of the leading role Angelo has played since joining the firm from Blackstone in 2022. In his new role, he will now formally oversee ArcLight’s investment, capital raising and operational activities.
 
ArcLight Founder and Managing Partner, Dan Revers, commented: “Angelo has brought an invaluable amount of energy, investment acumen, risk management, creativity and strategic leadership to the firm. Since he joined and with his leadership, we have made substantial investments across ArcLight, which are yielding significant benefits just as the secular tailwinds of electrification and digital power are coming to define infrastructure investing. I have never been more excited about the future of the firm.”
 
Read the full announcement here: https://lnkd.in/em6Fs2Dx
 
#InfrastructureInvesting #Energy #PrivateEquity #Promotion #Leadership

ArcLight has owned, controlled or operated over ~65 GW of assets and 47,000 miles of electric and gas transmission and storage infrastructure representing $80 billion of enterprise value. ArcLight has a long and proven track record of value-added investing across its core investment sectors including power, hydro, solar, wind, battery storage, electric transmission and natural gas transmission and storage infrastructure to support the growing need for power, reliability, security, and sustainability. ArcLight's team employs an operationally intensive investment approach that benefits from its dedicated in-house strategic, technical, operational, and commercial specialists, as well as the firm's ~1,900-person asset management partner.

text:
#PEINEXUS | Ardian was at the heart of the discussions at Private Equity International NEXUS in Orlando. 
 
The conference is one of the top gatherings of investors and capital allocators in North America and provides an opportunity to hear from industry leaders on topics shaping the future of private capital. 
 
💬 Ava Mallin, Managing Director, Private Wealth Solutions, helped lead a fascinating panel discussion on how private wealth is changing the rules for GP access. This included an in-depth discussion of private wealth platforms, family offices and RIAs, changing fund structures and what will actually resonate with private wealth investors in their GP selection process. Ava was joined by representatives from Citi, Clearstead, Morgan Stanley and I Squared Capital.
 
💬 Wilfred Small, III, Co-Head of U.S. Secondaries & Primaries and Senior Managing Director, provided his insights on what is driving the demand for LP-led secondaries in the current environment. He was joined on the stage by representatives from Northleaf Capital Partners and StepStone Group, with the discussion covering evolving pricing dynamics, hotspots for capital deployment and the outlook for deal volume growth and structural innovation.
 
#PrivateEquity #PrivateMarkets #Secondaries #PrivateWealth

text:
As institutional portfolios now often allocate 20–30% to private equity, the need for liquidity and precision in portfolio management has never been greater. Buying the right asset at the right time is now critical.

Ardian’s Secondaries platform is designed to meet this growing demand. Following the close of a record-breaking $30B platform and powered by proprietary database, we deliver tailored liquidity solutions at scale across the private equity landscape.

In this video, Vladimir Colas, Executive Vice President and Co-Head of Ardian Secondaries & Primaries, shares insights on how to make the secondary market.

#Secondaries #PrivateEquity #PrivateMarkets

text:
We are proud to have received two prestigious industry awards at the Private Equity Wire® European Awards!

🏆 𝐎𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐚𝐥𝐥 𝐅𝐮𝐧𝐝𝐫𝐚𝐢𝐬𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐨𝐟 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐘𝐞𝐚𝐫 – 𝐒𝐞𝐜𝐨𝐧𝐝𝐚𝐫𝐢𝐞𝐬 for 𝘈𝘳𝘥𝘪𝘢𝘯 𝘚𝘦𝘤𝘰𝘯𝘥𝘢𝘳𝘺 𝘍𝘶𝘯𝘥 𝘐𝘟
🏆 𝐃𝐢𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐬𝐢𝐭𝐲 𝐈𝐧𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐞 𝐨𝐟 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐘𝐞𝐚𝐫 – 𝐅𝐢𝐫𝐦-𝐰𝐢𝐝𝐞 for our teams' commitment to inclusion and diversity

These awards reflect the dedication of our teams, the trust of our investors, and our ongoing commitment to delivering excellence while driving positive change across the private investment industry.
Ardian Secondary Fund IX, closed at $30 billion, is the largest secondaries fundraise globally to date, reflecting our strong relationships with investors worldwide and our ability to provide liquidity solutions across the private equity market.

A heartfelt thank you to our colleagues, partners, and clients who make these achievements possible.
 
#PrivateEquity #Secondaries #Diversity #Inclusion #Sustainability #Awards

Combining years of experience with innovation, Ardian has built one of the world’s leading Secondaries platforms: IBIP, our proprietary database.

Powered by advanced analytics and guided by human insight, IBIP captures over 5 million data points every quarter, tracking more than 10,000 portfolio companies, 1,600 funds, and 650 GPs. The platform not only strengthens our ability to deliver high-quality insights for our clients, but also enables our teams to price assets accurately and identify opportunities across sectors and geographies.

Get inside IBIP with our Ardian's Secondaries team: Grace Lloyd and Mackenzie Speak, Directors, Christopher Sealey, Senior Investment Manager and Rory Milbank, Investment Manager ↓

#Ardian #Secondaries #PrivateEquity #IBIP #DataDriven #PrivateMarkets

text:
The real estate secondaries market reached a record $20.3 billion in volume in 2025, reflecting accelerating demand for liquidity solutions across private real estate. In 2025, Ares Secondaries also drove a record 189 real estate secondaries transactions, representing $20.3 billion of net asset value closed or placed under contract and significantly outpacing the previous records set in 2024.

Our latest piece examines last year’s real estate secondaries deal volumes and the forces reshaping this rapidly scaling market, particularly as liquidity needs meet a deepening opportunity set. More here: https://lnkd.in/eDUxD6Ux
authorHeadline:
Ares Management Corporation (NYSE: ARES) is a leading global alternative investment manager offering clients complementary primary and secondary investment solutions across the credit, private equity, real estate and infrastructure asset classes. We seek to provide flexible capital to support businesses and create value for our stakeholders and within our communities. By collaborating across our investment groups, we aim to generate consistent and attractive investment returns throughout market cycles. As of December 31, 2025, Ares Management Corporation's global platform had approximately $623 billion of assets under management with approximately 4,200 employees operating across North America, South America, Europe, Asia Pacific and the Middle East.

Cybersecurity & Fraud Prevention: https://www.aresmgmt.com/fraud-awareness

text: We are pleased to announce the pricing of Ares European Direct Lending CLO II (EDL CLO II) at over €300 million. This CLO is comprised entirely of directly originated and actively managed loans issued by over 70 middle-market companies predominantly based in Western Europe. Ares believes EDL CLO II is among the first multi-currency middle-market CLOs in Europe; with strong investor demand and a resilient, diversified loan pool, EDL CLO II marks another exciting milestone for Ares’ European Direct Lending platform. More on this announcement in the full press release: https://lnkd.in/e6DPjwEN

text: We are proud to share that Julie Solomon has been named to Private Equity News’ Twenty Most Influential in Real Estate list. Since being appointed Co‑Head of the group in 2024, Julie has helped grow Ares’ real estate platform by roughly $64 billion in AUM, both organically and through significant strategic acquisitions including that of GCP International. At the start of 2026, Julie assumed the role of Head of Ares Real Estate where she helped launch Marq Logistics, Ares’ global logistics real estate platform, and now oversees nearly $114 billion in real estate AUM. Congratulations on this well‑deserved recognition, Julie! More here: https://lnkd.in/e2trmTW5

As of December 31, 2025, Ares Management Corporation's global platform had approximately $623 billion of assets under management with approximately 4,200 employees operating across North America, South America, Europe, Asia Pacific and the Middle East.

Kelling Group is a leading equipment leasing and hire business serving infrastructure markets in the UK and Ireland. Acquired by Astatine Investment Partners Fund I in 2018, the business has grown substantially on a fully organic basis through a series of initiatives, including the introduction of longer-term leasing contracts and expansion into Ireland. 
Today, Kelling is the leading provider in its markets, supplying specialist equipment across various sectors including telecoms, power, water, highways, rail and street lighting.

text:
We are delighted to welcome Francisco Thoden to the Industrial Advisory Board of Asterion Industrial Partners.

Francisco brings over 40 years of experience in the energy sector, having developed most of his career at Endesa, where he held senior responsibilities across power generation, networks, gas and regulatory affairs in several international markets. Since 2017, he has continued advising on strategic energy projects, including initiatives linked to Asterion’s portfolio. 

His appointment reinforces one of Asterion’s key differentiators: deep industrial expertise combined with a truly hands-on investment approach. At Asterion Industrial Partners, we work side by side with management teams, bringing operational insight and sector knowledge to drive long-term value creation.

We are confident that Francisco’s experience will further strengthen our Industrial Advisory Board and support the continued growth of our platforms across Europe.

Welcome to the team!
#AsterionIndustrial #AsterionTeam #infrastructure #IndustrialExpertise #energy
authorHeadline:
Asterion Industrial Partners is an independent investment management firm focused on infrastructure investments in the European mid-market. Headquartered in Madrid, with an office in London, Asterion combines transactional and operational expertise with an industrial approach and active asset management, operating through an independent and agile platform.

Asterion is committed to promoting operational transparency, responsible investment practices, best-in-class governance, and a strong corporate culture — both within the firm and across its portfolio companies.

The firm targets investments across key European geographies, including France, Italy, Portugal, Spain, and the United Kingdom, with a focus on sectors such as telecommunications infrastructure, energy, utilities, and mobility.

Founded in late 2018, Asterion has grown to manage approximately $10 billion in assets under management.

text:
We are pleased to announce that a consortium formed by Asterion Industrial Partners, EDF Invest and Swiss Life Asset Managers has agreed to sell Energy Assets Group (EAG) to Macquarie Group Asset Management.

Since our investment in 2020, EAG has delivered strong operational performance, with EBITDA almost tripling, driven by operational excellence, selective bolt-on M&A and sustained investment in last-mile energy networks.

🗣️ Guido Mitrani, Founding Partner of Asterion Industrial Partners, commented: “The value creation delivered at Energy Assets Group reflects the disciplined execution of our industrial strategy, combining operational excellence, selective bolt-on acquisitions, capital structure optimization and sustained investment, supported by a strong and experienced management team.”

We thank the EAG management team for their outstanding execution and wish the company continued success in its next phase of growth.

This transaction represents the fifth exit for Asterion, following PROXISERVE, Nabiax, Asterion Energies and Sorgenia.

More deatails : https://lnkd.in/eeS-6Qk5

text:
In Asterion Industrial Partners we plan to invest €1.5 billion of capital to ABIO (Asterion Bioenergy), reinforcing our strong conviction in #biomethane as a scalable and strategic pillar of Europe’s energy transition.

The investment includes €800m of equity (€400m from Asterion Industrial Infra Fund III and €400m from existing investors), already supporting rapid expansion across Europe. 

ABIO currently operates 6 biomethane plants, with 6 under construction, and is on track to reach 20 operational facilities by the end of 2026. The platform is active in five key markets—#Iberia, #Italy, #Germany, #Benelux and the #UK— deploying locally tailored strategies supported by specialized teams on the ground.

With a robust development #pipeline, ABIO is expected to reach ~3 TWh of biomethane capacity within the next 3–4 years, combining energy security, decarbonisation and circular economy principles.

A further step in building long-term, sustainable infrastructure at scale across Europe.

To read more about this project: 
https://lnkd.in/eipykPPn

text:
As 2025 comes to an end, we’re proud to look back on what has been a truly defining year for Asterion Industrial Partners.
 
In just 18 months, we closed Fund III at €3.4bn, exceeding our target, and reached c.$10bn in AUM only seven years after inception, securing a place among the Top 25 global infrastructure managers. A milestone that reflects the trust of our investors and the strength of our platform.
 
None of this would be possible without our people. Today, 67 professionals across #Madrid and #London make up a diverse, committed and highly experienced team. In 2025, we welcomed 11 new colleagues, including senior operating partners, who further enhance our investment and portfolio capabilities.
 
With more than €5bn invested across 20+ companies, a strong pipeline and a fantastic #team, we head into #2026 with confidence, ambition and genuine excitement, driven by our belief in the transformational power of investment to create long-term value across Europe’s essential #infrastructure.
 
From all of us at Asterion, wishing you a great start to the New Year.

#AsterionIndustrial

text:
At Asterion Industrial Partners, supporting the communities where we operate is a core part of who we are. Through our “Asterion for Local Communities” program, we continue to back initiatives that reduce inequality, promote digitalisation as a pathway to equal opportunities, and empower young people to build brighter futures. In 2025, we deepened our long-standing partnership with Asociación Norte Joven, whose mission to transform the lives of vulnerable youth through #education and vocational training #inspires us year after year.

Once again, part of the Asterion #team had the privilege of working and learning alongside Norte Joven students and Carlos Sánchez-Tembleque, head chef at TRAMO Restaurant, to prepare the menu served to more than 90 guests at our annual #Solidarity #Lunch. Seeing their enthusiasm, talent, and determination up close was truly inspiring. Jesus Olmos, Asterion Founding Partner & CEO, Felipe Turell, Co-Founder of Proyectos Conscientes and co-owner of Tramo, together with Luz Pérez Sánchez, President of Asociación Norte Joven and Mercedes Negueruela Lucena, Director at Norte Joven, expressed heartfelt thanks to the students for their dedication and spirit —and to all attendees for their commitment to the project.
 
Thank you for continuing to create opportunities that change lives —including ours.
authorHeadline:
Asterion Industrial Partners is an independent investment management firm focused on infrastructure investments in the European mid-market. Headquartered in Madrid, with an office in London, Asterion combines transactional and operational expertise with an industrial approach and active asset management, operating through an independent and agile platform.

Asterion is committed to promoting operational transparency, responsible investment practices, best-in-class governance, and a strong corporate culture — both within the firm and across its portfolio companies.

The firm targets investments across key European geographies, including France, Italy, Portugal, Spain, and the United Kingdom, with a focus on sectors such as telecommunications infrastructure, energy, utilities, and mobility.

Founded in late 2018, Asterion has grown to manage approximately $10 billion in assets under management.

text:
Asterion Industrial Partners has acquired a 50% stake in TotalEnergies’ renewable portfolio in #Greece, comprising 424 MW of wind and solar assets across operating and construction phases. The transaction, valuing the portfolio at €508 million, marks a strategic entry into the Greek power market, supported by a favorable regulatory framework and strong renewable resources.
  
TotalEnergies will retain the remaining 50% stake and continue to operate the assets, reinforcing a long-term partnership.
  
This acquisition accelerates the growth of Asterion’s pan-European renewable #platform, which—together with Revalue Energies—will approach 0.5 GW of operating capacity and exceed 3 GW under development, contributing meaningfully to Europe’s energy transition.
  
Read more here 👇👇👇 
https://lnkd.in/eaAhNuiS

#AsterionIndustrial #Infrastructure #Sustainability #EnergyTransition

text:
So proud of the Asterion team!

Yesterday, our colleagues proudly represented Asterion Industrial Partners at #LaCarreradelasEmpresas, turning teamwork into action on the streets of Madrid.

Running together as a #team — supporting one another, pushing forward towards a shared goal, and celebrating every step — our people reflected the same spirit that defines how we work every day at Asterion. 

Surrounded by more than 30,000 participants from 1,614 companies, our team demonstrated that success is never an individual effort. It is built through #trust, #commitment, and the sum of many #talents moving in the same direction.

#AsterionIndustrial #Infrastructure

text:
Asterion Industrial Partners is delighted to celebrate the continued growth of Clubö, our parking platform in #Spain. The company has recently completed the acquisition of the Villanueva 2 car park, a prime asset located next to Madrid’s Puerta de Alcalá and Plaza de Colón. With this addition, Clubö strengthens its position as a leading operator in #Madrid, where it already manages three projects in premium locations. 

Since entering the #parking sector in 2022 , Clubö has accelerated its growth, adding several assets in recent months. Today, the platform manages 22 car parks across 15 Spanish cities, with approximately 9,500 spaces, serving over 11,500 customers under the leadership of CEO Jero Olleros.

We are proud of Clubö’s strong momentum and look forward to continuing to support its expansion across Spain.

#AsterionIndustrial #Infrastructures

AustralianSuper is pleased to share its FY25 Modern Slavery Statement outlining our approach to identifying and managing modern slavery risks.

We recognise the complex risks modern slavery presents across our operations and investment portfolio.

We are supportive of efforts to strengthen modern slavery legislation that increase transparency on the reporting of modern slavery risks in company operations and value chains.

Learn more: https://ausup.me/3MPchIY

AustralianSuper welcomes the proposed changes to the Low-Income Superannuation Tax Offset (LISTO) that have been introduced into Parliament. 

We’ve been advocating for these changes for a long time and believe that if enacted, they will make a meaningful difference to the retirement savings of more than a million low-income workers, including approximately 365,000 AustralianSuper members. Learn more about AustralianSuper’s advocacy for LISTO reforms here: https://ausup.me/3MIYEek

Read the PDS and TMD at https://ausup.me/4aQLLXR

text:
AustralianSuper has joined Assemble in celebrating a construction milestone at another major apartment development.

The topping-out celebration marked the completion of the structural framework at 342 Victoria St, Brunswick.

Due to open later this year, the 284-apartment project will be the third Australian housing development delivered with investment from AustalianSuper.

AustralianSuper has committed more than $700 million to housing projects through Assemble, with 3,000 homes to be delivered by 2030, including 370 that are already complete.

Read the PDS and TMD at https://ausup.me/3OQaZ0W

AustralianSuper is Australia’s biggest active investor in the Australian share market*. 

The Annual General Meeting (AGM) season provides a key opportunity to exercise our ownership rights on behalf of AustralianSuper members by voting on our shareholdings.

Voting presents an opportunity to express our views on company matters that can impact investment value for members.

Read more about how we exercise our ownership rights during the 2025 Australian Annual General Meeting season here - https://ausup.me/4aAg8Sl

*Frontier Advisors, January 2025

We are pleased to announce a number of promotions across Basalt Infrastructure Partners, effective January 2026.

These promotions reflect the continued growth and evolution of the Basalt platform and recognise the experience, performance and increasing responsibilities of colleagues across the firm. They further strengthen the depth, continuity and long-term stability of our team.

Congratulations to:
Selina Sugiarto – Principal (London)
Greg Barancik – Principal (New York)
Anna Tachau – Principal (New York)
Jonathan Steele, CFA – Principal (New York)

Daniel Bateson – Vice President (London)

Flavia Fabbiano – Senior Associate (London)

We are proud of the depth of talent across Basalt and our long-standing commitment to developing and retaining high-quality professionals, supported by clear, long-term career pathways as the platform continues to grow.

text:
Basalt is delighted to announce that funds advised by Basalt have acquired Go Lime Inc. ("Go Lime") and substantially all the assets of the Crown Crest Group of Companies ("Simply Green"). 

Go Lime and Simply Green both lease water heaters, HVAC units and water treatment systems to residential homeowners under long term leases, across Ontario. 

David Greenblatt, Managing Partner at Basalt commented "This dual transaction represents Basalt's first infrastructure investment in Canada. Go Lime is a leading water heater and home equipment provider in Ontario and we believe the combined platform is well positioned to unlock further growth in an attractive sector with strong infrastructure characteristics. We look forward to partnering with the Go Lime team to support the company's next stage of growth."

text:
Last week, we held our 4th Annual Sustainability Forum, bringing together sustainability leads from across 17 of our portfolio companies from Europe and North America. 

Hosted by Basalt’s Responsible Investing and Sustainability Manager, Katie Ahmed, it was a chance to spend the day discussing market trends, how we are investing responsibly, and what we could be doing better. It is always a fantastic opportunity to share knowledge and best practice, covering topics including climate risk and resilience, value creation opportunities, health and safety, and emerging sectors and technologies. 

Thank you to all those who attended and shared case studies (Simon Richards, Steve Williams, Colm Connolly, and Jos Schmitz), as well as to our excellent presenters, Pedro A. (Basalt Infrastructure Partners), Rose Newton, ACA, CFA (Harrison Street Asset Management) and Sarah Jones (Anthesis Group).

text:
We are excited to announce that funds advised by Basalt have agreed to acquire JR Richards & Sons ("JR Richards"). JR Richards is a leading waste management platform in regional New South Wales, Australia, marking Basalt's first investment in the Australian market. 

John Hanna, Managing Partner at Basalt said, “Basalt is delighted to enter the Australian infrastructure market through the acquisition of JR Richards, a leading and trusted regional waste management platform. With strategically located facilities, an extensive fleet and long‑term municipal partnerships, JR Richards is well placed to benefit from favorable regulatory momentum encouraging higher resource recovery and organics diversion. We look forward to working closely with the company’s experienced management team and employees to support the next phase of growth.”

In addition, we are delighted to welcome Malcolm Brown who has joined our bench of Operating Partners, with a focus on Transport. text:
We are delighted to announce that funds advised by Basalt have agreed to acquire Chemco Ireland Limited (“Chemco”). Chemco is the leading provider of essential specialist chemical logistics and distribution services, primarily supporting the semiconductor and pharmaceutical industries in Ireland, with a significant long-term contractual underpin.
 
John Hanna, Managing Partner at Basalt said ”Chemco is the leading provider of specialist logistics services to the growing semiconductor and pharmaceutical sectors in Ireland, and its highly specialised and long term contracted services are a critical component to its customers. Basalt is excited to partner with management to support the Company’s growth.”

With a strong track record of delivering reliable, cost‑effective, and sustainable energy infrastructure, Corix is well positioned for continued growth as cities and residents pursue greater density and energy efficiency.

authorHeadline:
British Columbia Investment Management Corporation (BCI) offers an exceptional opportunity to work at a world-class organization. With $295 billion of gross assets under management, BCI provides investment management services to British Columbia public sector and institutional clients. As an active in-house asset manager investing globally across public and private asset classes and strategies, BCI depends on talent with specialized expertise to fulfill client needs, helping them build meaningful futures.

BCI’s teams come to work each day knowing their performance has purpose, and that our achievements are built on collective expertise, in an environment where we can bring innovative ideas to the table to perform at the highest level for our clients.

Our employees share BCI's core values of clients first, performance-focused, world-class, accountability, transparency, and integrity. Being recognized as one of Canada's Top 100 Employers, Canada's Top Family-Friendly Employers, Canada's Top Employers for Young People, and BC's Top Employers exemplifies our ongoing commitment to be a progressive workplace of choice; one that attracts, retains, and promotes talent so that we can continue to work towards meeting our clients' financial goals.

text:
The AI investment landscape resembles a gold rush. But is the math adding up? 

Sai Devabhaktuni, Senior Managing Director, Private Equity, explores a critical question in our latest Market Viewpoint: whether current AI valuations justify both the magnitude and timing of future cash flows. 

Key considerations: 

🔹 AI's higher floor doesn't eliminate valuation risk in a stretched market 
🔹 Sustainable competitive advantages matter more than prototype capabilities 
🔹 Success requires combining strategic patience with decisive action 
 
This environment demands greater scrutiny of profitability paths and business model durability. At BCI Private Equity, we don't chase every opportunity — we pursue disciplined value creation through digital transformation. 

Read the full analysis at the link in the comments. 

#MarketViewpoint #PrivateEquity #DigitalTransformation #AI #InvestingThatMatters

text:
Seven years in a row as one of BC's Top Employers!  

Our roots are here in British Columbia, and that shapes everything we do: helping to fund pension benefits for public sector workers, such as teachers and first responders, supporting cultural, sports, and development organizations, and contributing to communities in BC and beyond.  

Every dollar we invest is generating possibilities for our clients and their members. That same commitment shows up in how we support our team through strong parental leave support, flexible work arrangements, tuition support, and career development across our global offices.  

Learn more about what makes BCI one of BC’s Top Employers at the link in the comments. 

#InvestingThatMatters #PerformanceWithPurpose #TopEmployerBC
