text:
We’re pleased to announce that Jon Salon has officially stepped into his new role as Executive Vice President and Global Head of Private Equity. 

Jon joins BCI’s executive leadership team with a clear mandate: to advance BCI Private Equity’s high‑conviction, globally diversified investment strategy that creates sustainable, long‑term value for pension fund and institutional clients. He brings more than three decades of private equity investment and executive management experience to the role. 

With Jon's seasoned leadership and expertise, we're excited about the continued growth and impact of our $36 billion+ Private Equity program!  

Read our conversation with Jon about his vision for BCI Private Equity at the link in the comments. 

#PrivateEquity #InstitutionalInvesting #InvestingThatMatters

As an active in-house asset manager investing globally across public and private asset classes and strategies, BCI depends on talent with specialized expertise to fulfill client needs, helping them build meaningful futures.

BCI’s teams come to work each day knowing their performance has purpose, and that our achievements are built on collective expertise, in an environment where we can bring innovative ideas to the table to perform at the highest level for our clients.

Our employees share BCI's core values of clients first, performance-focused, world-class, accountability, transparency, and integrity. Being recognized as one of Canada's Top 100 Employers, Canada's Top Family-Friendly Employers, Canada's Top Employers for Young People, and BC's Top Employers exemplifies our ongoing commitment to be a progressive workplace of choice; one that attracts, retains, and promotes talent so that we can continue to work towards meeting our clients' financial goals.

text:
Natural capital isn't new territory for BCI – with 20+ years of experience in forestry and agriculture, our investment teams know how to identify exceptional opportunities.

In a recent interview with IPE Real Assets, Sameer Jinnah, Managing Director of Infrastructure & Renewable Resources, explains our disciplined, cycle-led approach: we invest when market dynamics align to create durable value, then focus on building platforms and hold for the long term.

Today, these sectors represent ~20% of our $32-billion infrastructure portfolio, delivering solid returns while diversifying across global markets from Canada to Brazil.
 
Read the full article at the link in the comments.
 
#InfrastructureInvesting #NaturalCapital #InvestingThatMatters

text:
We’re pleased to welcome Jeff Andry, CFA to BCP as a Director based in our New Orleans office.

Jeff joins BCP at an exciting time of growth across our infrastructure portfolio and adds meaningful depth to our regulatory, strategic, and transactional capabilities. Most recently, he served as Chief Strategy and Regulatory Officer at DEMCO, a Louisiana electric cooperative, where he oversaw enterprise strategy and execution as well as all regulatory interactions and legal matters.

Earlier in his career, Jeff held roles at NextEra Energy Resources and Cleco Corporate Holdings affiliates, leading a broad range of transactions across the electric power industry.

We’re excited to have Jeff on board as we continue to grow our portfolio.
authorHeadline:
Bernhard Capital Partners (BCP) is a private equity management firm founded in 2013 with a focus on services and infrastructure. Since inception, BCP has raised and deployed capital across five funds spanning multiple strategies, with more than $5.5 billion in assets under management. BCP creates long-term, sustainable value by leveraging deep experience in acquiring, operating, and scaling services and infrastructure businesses. The firm’s disciplined approach and operational expertise have positioned it as a leader in its sector.
 
During a recent 10-year review of 2014 vintage funds, PitchBook recognized BCP Fund I (2014 vintage) as one of the top-performing Global Real Asset Funds, ranking it among the top three out of more than 130 funds globally and the top two domestically.
 
Headquartered in the Southeastern United States, BCP maintains offices in Baton Rouge, LA; New Orleans, LA; and Nashville, TN.

text:
Today BCP announced it has closed on the previously announced sale of The Gray Casualty & Surety Company to Palomar Holdings, Inc.
 
With the backing of BCP, Gray Surety has grown substantially as a surety carrier specializing in contract bonds for midsized and emerging contractors across the U.S., operating 13 regional offices from coast to coast. 
 
“We’re pleased to complete the Gray Surety sale, marking another important milestone in BCP’s disciplined investment and value-creation strategy,” said Jeffrey Koonce, Partner at BCP. “Since our investment in 2021, Gray Surety has delivered significant growth, expanded its national presence, and deepened its reputation as a trusted surety partner to contractors across the country. We are proud of the company’s continued success and grateful to the Gray Surety team for their exceptional execution.”

Read more: https://lnkd.in/eMaHkcaq

text:
In a recent conversation with PE Hub, Mark Spender, Partner and Chief Investment Officer at BCP, shared his perspective on the power infrastructure services market following BCP’s recently announced exit of United Utility.

Sustained demand for grid reliability, modernization, and resilience—driven by aging infrastructure, data centers, and industrial growth—continues to support strong outcomes for scaled, well-integrated service platforms like United Utility. Mark highlighted how the company's growth, integration, and disciplined execution of a value creation plan under BCP’s ownership positioned the business for a successful transition to its next phase.

Read more in PE Hub Wire: https://lnkd.in/eZUcypVE

text:
BCP announced today that it has agreed to sell United Utility, a leading provider of electric utility infrastructure services, to Sandbrook Capital, in partnership with funds managed by Blackstone Credit & Insurance. 

Under BCP’s ownership, United Utility has grown into one of the leading and most trusted service providers to the power grid, supporting major utilities as they invest at record levels to improve the reliability and resilience of critical infrastructure nationwide.

“Since our initial investment, United Utility has grown from an acquired collection of regional businesses into a scaled national platform and a trusted partner to many of the leading utilities in North America,” said Mark Spender, Partner at BCP. “We are proud of what we and management have accomplished, as it reflects BCP’s disciplined strategy of acquiring and integrating high-quality regional providers to support utilities’ evolving needs and long-term grid investment priorities. Sandbrook is a highly aligned new owner with deep sector expertise, and we are confident that under Colt’s leadership the company is well positioned for its next phase of growth.”

Read more: https://lnkd.in/eWt3DbTu

text:
BCP portfolio company Brailsford & Dunlavey, Inc. (B&D) announced today it has merged with Project Management Advisors, Inc. (PMA) — uniting two nationally recognized firms to form a leading institutional program management platform. 

Together, the firms combine complementary strengths, broaden geographic reach, and deepen expertise across higher education, healthcare, life sciences, technology, K-12 schools, civic projects, sports venues, and innovative energy infrastructure solutions.

“B&D and PMA each bring exceptional talent and a disciplined, client-focused approach to delivering complex projects,” said BCP Partner Mark Spender. “By merging, they are creating a unified institutional program management platform with the integrated capabilities and scale to support social and institutional infrastructure clients and large asset owners at every stage of the real estate lifecycle. We are proud to support the combined firm and look forward to the impact it will deliver across the country.” 

Read more: https://lnkd.in/e7ZnN64C

text:
We are deeply saddened to share the news of the unexpected passing of our founder, Jim Bernhard. Jim wasn’t just a visionary businessman — he was a force of nature whose belief that “the best has no equal” shaped everything he built, from The Shaw Group to Bernhard Capital Partners, and inspired generations of leaders. 

Jim transformed industries, communities, and countless lives throughout the course of his life. Many of us are who we are today because of his leadership, the high standards he set, and his commitment to helping others reach their full potential. His legacy lives on in those he mentored, the companies he built, and the values that continue to define our firm today.

Our thoughts and prayers are with Dana and the entire Bernhard family as we honor the extraordinary legacy Jim leaves behind.

A full statement is available here: https://lnkd.in/gEUSxNF5

text:
BCP announced today an agreement to sell The Gray Casualty & Surety Company to Palomar Holdings, Inc., with the transaction expected to close in the first half of 2026. 

In 2021, BCP made a significant investment in Gray Surety, a Treasury-listed surety carrier specializing in contract bonds for midsized and emerging contractors across the U.S. Led by an experienced management team, Gray Surety is licensed in all 50 states and operates through 13 regional offices.

“Over the past four years, we’ve been proud to partner and work with Gray Surety and its leadership team to help position the company for long-term success,” said Jeffrey Koonce, Partner at BCP. “Together, we’ve seen the company achieve significant growth, expand its national footprint, and strengthen its reputation as a trusted surety partner to contractors across the country. This transaction is a reflection of that success and the exceptional foundation built by the Gray team.”

Read more: https://lnkd.in/gadGWKnG

text:
At BCP, discipline and specialization define our approach. We invest exclusively in essential infrastructure—the systems and services that power communities and drive economic growth.

As the U.S. undergoes a once-in-a-generation infrastructure buildout, we believe our proven blueprint and operational expertise make BCP a trusted partner of choice.

Hear more from partners Mark Spender and Jeff Jenkins on our focused strategy to create lasting value in essential infrastructure.

authorHeadline:
BlackRock is a global asset manager and technology provider dedicated to helping more and more people experience financial well-being.  

We help millions of people invest to build savings that serve them throughout their lives. We always start with our clients’ needs and look to offer them more quality choices for how and where to invest their money. 
 
Our global investments platform offers our clients access to the world’s markets while making investing easier and more affordable. And with offices in more than 40 countries, our global expertise helps them navigate changing markets to stay ahead of the curve. 

Follow us for global insights shaping the economy, conversations about financial well-being and more information on culture and careers at BlackRock.

text:
A great discussion today at the Professional Planner Licensee Summit, where Jason Collins, BlackRock’s Head of Australasia, joined a panel of industry peers to share insights on key trends shaping the financial advice and licensee landscape. From regulatory shifts and the influx of private capital and M&A activity, to advancements in technology and the evolution of private markets - these forces are reshaping the industry.

A big thank you to Conexus for hosting us!

authorHeadline: Blackstone is the world’s largest alternative asset manager. Blackstone seeks to deliver compelling returns for institutional and individual investors by strengthening the companies in which the firm invests. Blackstone’s $1.3 trillion in assets under management include global investment strategies focused on real estate, private equity, credit, infrastructure, life sciences, growth equity, secondaries and hedge funds. Further information is available at www.blackstone.com. Follow @blackstone on LinkedIn, X (Twitter), and Instagram.

text:
Lights, Camera, Blackstone! 🎬 We’re thrilled to announce the opening of Manhattan’s first purpose-built film and TV facility alongside Vornado Realty Trust and Hudson Pacific Properties.

Sunset Studios’ Pier 94 Studio is the result of a strong public-private partnership that drives valuable investments for our partners and the community. A huge thanks to New York City Economic Development Corporation and New York State for their commitment to fostering the film and TV industry in New York. We can’t wait for the first production. 🍿

Blackstone’s $1.3 trillion in assets under management include global investment strategies focused on real estate, private equity, credit, infrastructure, life sciences, growth equity, secondaries and hedge funds. Further information is available at www.blackstone.com. Follow @blackstone on LinkedIn, X (Twitter), and Instagram.

authorHeadline:
We are a leading global investment firm that invests on behalf of institutions and individuals around the world, with the goal of helping them to create long-term, sustainable wealth. Our origin as an owner of high-quality businesses allows us to leverage our unique and deep operational expertise to grow the businesses we own on behalf of our clients. We put our own capital to work alongside our partners’ in nearly every transaction, ensuring strong alignment of interests. 

To learn more about our global businesses spanning transition, renewable power, infrastructure, real estate, private equity and credit, please visit www.brookfield.com.

When Chemelex — a market-leading manufacturer of heat-control systems — came up for sale for the third time in 12 years, many investors passed. We didn’t.

Instead, we saw a resilient industrial business providing essential products and services that keep critical infrastructure running across the real economy — from industrial facilities to the New York City subway and the Eiffel Tower.

Explore how we carved out and are transforming Chemelex: https://brkfld.co/exwbm59r

authorHeadline:
Carlyle (NASDAQ: CG) is a global investment firm with deep industry expertise that deploys private capital across three business segments: Global Private Equity, Global Credit, and Carlyle AlpInvest. With $477 billion of assets under management as of December 31, 2025, Carlyle’s purpose is to invest wisely and create value on behalf of its investors, portfolio companies, and the communities in which we live and invest. Carlyle employs more than 2,500 people in 27 offices across four continents.

text:
We are pleased to share that Carlyle has been named “Best Alternatives Manager of the Year” at the Financial Times and MandateWire’s Asset Management Excellence Awards Middle East 2026.
 
The Middle East is an important and strategic region for Carlyle, and one where we have been present since 2007. This award recognises our long-standing commitment to the region, the recent momentum across our global platform, and our deep and longstanding partnerships across the Middle East. Our momentum was evident in 2025 with:
 
➡️ Significant participation from Middle East investors across the firm’s strategies and co-investments, including major flagship fundraises in secondaries, U.S. real estate, and private credit
➡️ New structures and partnerships with banks and private wealth platforms to expand access to alternatives across the region
➡️ Investment in local talent development through bespoke training and education programs organized for key partners with participation from sovereign wealth funds, pension institutions, banks, and family offices
 
We are grateful to our partners and investors across the region for their ongoing support.
 
This award was announced in February 2026 by the Financial Times & MandateWire. No compensation was paid by Carlyle for award. No award should be relied upon as a predictor of future results.

text:
Greg Zeluck, Partner & Co-Head of Asia Buyout at Carlyle, recently joined Manisha Girotra, CEO of Moelis India, at the The Times Group ET NOW Global Business Summit in New Delhi for a fireside chat to discuss Carlyle’s 25+ years of investing in India and the evolution of the country’s private equity market.

He shared his view that India today is a vibrant market for control buyouts, touched on PE investing themes in the country, and highlighted key factors he believes are powering the transformation of India’s PE market, including:
 
➡️ A shift from minority deals to partnerships and control buyouts—driven by a mindset shift among founders and promoters as they plan for succession or look to scale their businesses further
➡️ Deeper liquidity and robust capital markets supporting multiple deal exit pathways across IPOs, secondary block trades, and M&A
➡️ A rapidly expanding pool of C-suite talent across sectors, enabling a more evolved value-creation playbook
 
📺 Watch the full conversation here: http://spr.ly/6049h5VHl
📄 Read more on The Economic Times here: http://spr.ly/6040h5VHm

text:
AI is no longer theoretical – it’s becoming embedded in how businesses operate.
 
On the latest episode of Credit Exchange with Lisa Lee, Mark Jenkins, Co-President and Head of Global Credit & Insurance at Carlyle, shares his perspective on where AI goes next: implementation. “We have this great tool out there called AI, and we haven’t 100% figured out how to implement it yet.”
 
Mark believes we’re still in the early innings – and that the next phase won’t take years, but months, as corporates integrate AI into workflows. At Carlyle, AI is already helping our investment teams operate more efficiently and analyze large volumes of data in ways previously impossible.
 
But technology is an enabler, not a decision-maker. As Mark notes: “It’s not going to make the decision for you.”
 
He also shares his outlook on the macro environment over the next 12–36 months.
 
🎧 Listen to the full episode here: http://spr.ly/6045hRvr7
📺 Watch the full episode here: http://spr.ly/6046hRvrC

authorHeadline:
Based in Boston and New York City, Charlesbank is a leading middle-market private investment firm with more than $20 billion in assets under management as of 9/30/2025. Drawing on nearly three decades of experience and sector insights, the firm takes a thematic approach to investing across its target sectors: business and consumer services, healthcare, industrials and technology & technology infrastructure. Charlesbank partners with talented management teams to help businesses unlock value and accelerate growth, with a focus on long-term value creation. The firm provides flexible capital through its complementary private equity and credit strategies, which collaborate closely to harness the firm’s collective capabilities, resources and network.

Charlesbank partners with talented management teams to help businesses unlock value and accelerate growth, with a focus on long-term value creation. The firm provides flexible capital through its complementary private equity and credit strategies, which collaborate closely to harness the firm’s collective capabilities, resources and network.

Disclaimer: The information on this page is for informational purposes only. Nothing on or within this page constitutes a solicitation, offer, recommendation or representation of suitability or endorsement of any security or investment or any product of Charlesbank or Charlesbank-managed funds. Charlesbank is not responsible for any content posted by third parties to this page.

Our work in the West Adams neighborhood of Los Angeles continues with the recent completion of The Read, a 75-unit, six-story apartment building with 9,000 square feet of ground floor retail space.

The property offers one- and two-bedroom residences with wood flooring, in-unit washers and dryers, quartz countertops, large windows, and contemporary fixtures. Amenities include a rooftop lounge with BBQ grills and a fully equipped fitness center.

The Read follows the recently completed Coro, a nearby 168-unit apartment project.

text:
We're pleased to announce that iconic Atlanta steakhouse Chops Lobster Bar from Buckhead Life Restaurant Group has signed a 13,000-square-foot restaurant lease at Centennial Yards Atlanta, the $5 billion, 50-acre mixed-use redevelopment transforming Downtown Atlanta.

Recognized by numerous publications as one of the top 10 steakhouses in the country, Chops Lobster Bar has been known for its exceptional food and best-in-class hospitality for more than 35 years.

"We are excited to bring the innovative team behind Chops Lobster Bar to Centennial Yards,” said Shaul Kuba, Co-Founder and Principal, CIM Group. “Their commitment to exceptional dining and hospitality is a perfect complement to our vision for the Entertainment District and our goal of creating a vibrant destination in Downtown Atlanta."

This location is the brand's second outpost in Georgia and is anticipated to open in late 2026 in the Entertainment District at Centennial Yards.

Read the press release here: https://lnkd.in/gfAJ83bW

text:
Global power demand is rising, driven by electrification, AI, and a desire for energy independence. Renewable energy is leading the way.
 
In our latest article, we examine how wind and solar are now the fastest and most cost‑effective technologies to build new power capacity, and why this makes energy transition infrastructure the fastest‑growing segment in global infrastructure investment. 
 
The appeal is clear: 
♦ Cash flows with a long-term and resilient profile 
♦ Low correlation to equities 
♦ Portfolio diversification
 
In a volatile world, resilience is increasingly central to risk‑adjusted returns.
 
Read our latest article ‘Why Investors Are Backing Energy Transition Infrastructure’ to understand more: https://lnkd.in/eExjpQ4M
authorHeadline:
Founded in 2012, Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners P/S (CIP) today is a global leader in energy infrastructure investments, specialising in developing and constructing large, complex projects that shape the future of energy. The funds managed by CIP focuses on investments in offshore and onshore wind, solar PV, biomass and energy-from-waste, transmission and distribution, reserve capacity and storage and Power-to-X. 

CIP manages 13 funds and has to date raised over EUR 35bn for investments in energy and associated infrastructure from ~200 international institutional investors. 

With a diverse team of over 2,300 professionals engaged in projects, CIP is present in more than 30 countries globally.

text:
Next month, Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners will be in Berlin for the industry’s largest gathering: Infrastructure Investor Global Summit.
 
Throughout the week, members of the CIP delegation will be contributing to discussions across the programme. Key CIP moments include:

♦ Wednesday, 25 March at 10:20 CET – CIP’s fireside chat in the Plenary Hall between CIP Managing Partner Jakob Baruël Poulsen, and Michael Liebreich, host of the Cleaning Up podcast and Senior Contributor to BloombergNEF

♦ Wednesday, 25 March at 14:50 CET – CIP’s Spotlight Session in the Networking Area with CIP Green Credit Fund Partner Jakob Groot
 
We look forward to insightful discussions, new connections, and exploring fresh perspectives with industry peers in Berlin. 

If you are interested in connecting with someone from the CIP team, please reach out to ir@cip.com to book a meeting. Our team will be in touch to confirm.

text:
Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners welcomes yesterday’s UK AR7 auction results for onshore renewable technologies, with strong outcomes for our CI Flagship Fund V funded platforms Elgin Energy and Bute Energy.

Together, the teams secured key contracts, including:
♦ 94MW of onshore wind capacity via Bute Energy’s Twyn Hywel project
♦ CfDs for eight solar projects via Elgin Energy, representing over 380MW of new solar capacity

This milestone reflects the quality, scale, and maturity of our development pipelines in the UK. 

Congratulations to all our development and investment colleagues involved. We look forward to seeing these projects continue to drive the UK’s energy transition forward.

text:
Last week, our Senior Partners Jakob Baruël Poulsen, Torsten Lodberg Smed, Christina Grumstrup Soerensen, and Christian Skakkebæk visited Chile and Mexico to explore the impressive project portfolio that our Growth Market Fund II (GMF II) is developing across the region.
 
A highlight was a visit to CIP’s first construction project in Latin America, the Arena BESS project in the Atacama Desert in Chile. Arena is a 220MW / 1,100MWh battery project, and currently the country’s largest stand‑alone BESS project. As Arena BESS moves toward COD, it is set to become a flagship energy storage asset for both Chile and Latin America, reinforcing CIP’s mission to build new energy infrastructure globally.
 
The delegation also visited our first Mexican development projects, La Esperanza and La Alegría, both hybrid solar‑battery projects now approaching FID. Mexico faces a growing energy supply gap and has set ambitious renewable targets, making it a market full of potential.
 
Our developments across Mexico and Chile present an exciting opportunity for CIP, with our next battery project in Chile, Patache, already well underway. We remain committed to delivering projects across Latin America.

Read more about GMF II here: https://lnkd.in/dgsBPb4N

CIP manages 13 funds and has to date raised over EUR 35bn for investments in energy and associated infrastructure from ~200 international institutional investors. 

With a diverse team of over 2,300 professionals engaged in projects, CIP is present in more than 30 countries globally.

text:
This week, Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners welcomed more than 25 new colleagues from across our global offices to Copenhagen for our first employee onboarding session of the year. 
  
CIP’s onboarding programmes are delivered by our senior leadership. Over two days, we introduce new hires to the CIP platform, the unique ways we work, and outline how we create value for investors.

This is just one of the ways we ensure new colleagues have a strong start at CIP. We support our people at every stage of their career and always look to invest in their growth and success. 
 
We can’t wait to welcome the next CIP cohort!
 
Learn more about careers at CIP here: https://lnkd.in/ddmNdNjv

text:
Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners, through our flagship fund CI V, has acquired Ørsted’s European onshore renewables business, adding 800+ MW of operating and in‑construction assets and a multi‑gigawatt development pipeline to the project portfolio.
 
Adding a fully integrated, multi-technology onshore business to CI V, this milestone acquisition further strengthens CI V's position as a leading fund within energy infrastructure and onshore renewables.
 
Mads Skovgaard-Andersen, CIO and Partner in CIP, says: “With this significant acquisition across multiple markets and technologies, we further strengthen our presence in Europe. The combined onshore wind, solar, and BESS portfolio complements our existing project portfolio and give us the scale to further accelerate the deployment of renewable energy and strengthen Europe’s energy independence, while delivering strong, risk-adjusted returns to our investors.”  

Nischal Agarwal, Partner in CIP, says: “This is a great opportunity for CI V to acquire a sizeable portfolio and pipeline of projects across technologies in Europe’s most attractive markets with very strong demand growth. Along with a trusted team with a track record of delivering and operating projects, we are well positioned to further develop, manage, and realise the full value potential of the platform.”
 
The transaction includes a diversified portfolio of wind, solar, and storage projects in Ireland, the UK, Germany, and Spain, alongside the addition of a highly experienced team with a proven track record in delivering onshore renewable projects.
 
Learn more about the transaction here: https://lnkd.in/egaDmbAN

text:
Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners was pleased to join President Lee Jae-myung at The Blue House, Seoul, for a foreign investment roundtable.
 
Our delegation, led by Thomas Wibe Poulsen, shared CIP’s perspective on the vital role of offshore wind in accelerating Korea’s energy transition and strengthening a resilient energy economy.
 
At the event, we highlighted CIP’s commitment to supporting the government’s renewable energy ambitions, our strong partnerships with Korean business, and the continued momentum in our major Korean offshore wind projects across Shinan, Taean, and Ulsan. 
 
Korea is a nation with immense potential, and we are excited to continue working closely with the government and local industry to accelerate the country’s energy transition.
 
A sincere thank you to the Korean government and our partners for the opportunity to engage in this important dialogue. We look forward to deepening our collaboration and contributing to Korea’s renewable energy future.
 
Read more about how we build value: https://lnkd.in/djvFTcMA

text:
CPP Investments holds $114 billion Canadian assets. 

Explore how long-term capital is helping scale Canadian leadership in natural resources, infrastructure, and systematic investing in Mapping Canadian Capital, an ongoing series from CPP Investments Insights Institute. 
Each profile highlights how discipline, partnership, and scale can strengthen Canada’s economic foundations while building globally competitive platforms. 

Connor, Clark & Lunn Investment Management (CC&L) (Toronto) 
Pioneering systematic investing and expanding Canada’s presence in global quantitative asset management. 

Canadian Natural Resources Limited (CNRL) (Calgary) 
Sustaining resource leadership through operational scale, capital discipline, and long-term investment. 

407 ETR (Ontario) 
Paving the way for growth through one of North America’s most significant privately financed infrastructure assets. 

Read more here: https://lnkd.in/gypkt5rN 

#CPPInvestments #InvestingInCanada #InsightsInstitute #MappingCanadianCapital

text:
We’re pleased to announce the appointment of David Colla as Senior Managing Director and Global Head of Credit Investments at CPP Investments.

David will lead our global Credit Investments business, which serves corporate, real estate, and consumer issuers, managing exposure across term loans, investment-grade and high-yield bonds, mezzanine lending, and structured products.

Since joining CPP Investments in 2010, he has held several senior roles, most recently leading our Capital Solutions Group within Credit Investments. His long track record with the organization strongly positions the team to continue delivering disciplined, long-term value for the CPP Fund. 

Following a distinguished tenure at CPP Investments, leading the Credit Investments department since 2021, Andrew Edgell will take up a new role as Senior Advisor. We are pleased to keep benefitting from Andrew’s valuable experience.

https://lnkd.in/gU2_3msq

text:
Physical climate risk is not a single-actor problem.  

At our recent Insights Institute event, Chief Sustainability Officer Richard Manley noted that physical risk cannot be solved by governments alone, nor by insurers, investors, or markets in isolation. 
 
Convened in Toronto, this invitation-only discussion brought
together Canadian investors, banks, insurers, reinsurers, and data and modelling experts to examine what it will take to underwrite and price physical risk with confidence. A central theme emerged: pricing physical risk requires system-level coordination—across public policy, the real economy, and financial markets—with incentives aligned to reward adaptation and resilience. 
 
Watch the full discussion here: https://lnkd.in/egQQFDhh

text:
CPP Investments delivered a 10-year annualized net return of 8.4%, supporting the long-term sustainability of the Canada Pension Plan. Since beginning to invest the Fund in 1999, CPP Investments has generated $543.4 billion in cumulative net income. 
 
“With that longer-term imperative in mind, the Chief Actuary’s latest report provides an independent expert affirmation that the Canada Pension Plan remains financially sustainable until at least the end of the current century,” said John Graham, President & Chief Executive Officer, CPP Investments. “The report highlights that investment performance of the base CPP over the three-year period since December 31, 2021, was more than $75 billion higher than expected, resulting in a CPP Fund that is substantially larger than projected in the previous actuarial report. This independent confirmation supports the soundness of our long-term investment approach to help support a stable retirement foundation for CPP contributors and beneficiaries for generations to come.” 
 
Explore highlights from the quarter in our latest results presentation: https://lnkd.in/eRF2KMmm

text:
CPP Investments net assets totalled $780.7 billion at the end of Q3 fiscal 2026, an increase of $3.2 billion for the quarter. 

The Fund delivered a 0.5% net return in the quarter and a 10-year annualized net return of 8.4%, reflecting the strength of a long-term investment approach built to perform across market cycles. 
 
“We experienced a volatile quarter amid slowing growth and escalating geopolitical tensions,” said John Graham, President & Chief Executive Officer, CPP Investments. “The Fund remained resilient, and we stayed focused and disciplined as a patient investor. To meet obligations to current and future CPP beneficiaries we must hold to our long-horizon perspective and let fundamentals drive our conviction and decisions.” 

Learn more about our third quarter results: https://lnkd.in/eUYsW-qh

text:
We’re pleased to announce that CPP Investments has agreed to acquire a 50% ownership interest in Inkia Energy, Peru’s largest private power generation company.

Inkia operates a diversified 2.6GW electricity generation platform and plays a critical role in supporting Peru’s energy system and economic growth. This investment reflects our long-term approach to partnering in high-quality, essential infrastructure assets that can deliver attractive risk-adjusted returns for the CPP Fund.

text:
CPP Investments holds $114 billion in Canadian assets. Learn the stories behind our investments in Radical Ventures, Wolf Midstream and Northleaf Capital Partners in Mapping Canadian Capital, an ongoing series from CPP Investments Insights Institute.  
 
Each story demonstrates how scale, partnership, and patience can translate Canadian strengths into globally relevant platforms. 
🔹Radical Ventures (Toronto) 
Backing frontier AI and deep-tech companies with global ambition.  
🔹Wolf Midstream (Calgary) 
Building critical energy infrastructure through long-term partnerships and disciplined execution.  
🔹Northleaf Capital Partners (Toronto) 
Connecting global institutional capital to Canada’s mid-market across private equity, credit, and infrastructure.  
 
Read more here: https://lnkd.in/gypkt5rN

text:
Our President and CEO John Graham joined the Greater Vancouver Board of Trade Economic Outlook Forum last week.  

In his keynote, John reflected on CPP Investments’ mission – to help ensure financial security in retirement to over 22 million Canadians – and the importance of a long-term approach. 

“Our job isn’t to respond to every market swing,” he said. “It’s to build a resilient portfolio that can support the CPP across very different economic environments.”

text:
When it comes to data centres in Europe, "we are at the beginning of the S curve - the development of the whole industry", says Xavier Martin.

In a recent interview with Institutional Investing in Infrastructure (i3) editor Kali Arevalo, Cube Partner Xavier Martin discusses the exciting opportunities in Europe’s data centre landscape, the varying market dynamics across regions, and how infrastructure managers and investors are navigating the new challenges that arise in this relatively new subsector of infrastructure.

Watch the full interview here: https://lnkd.in/eZRdF93r
authorHeadline:
Cube Infrastructure Managers is an independent mid-market infrastructure investor joining deep sector knowledge with operational and financial expertise. 

Since inception in 2007, Cube has leveraged its operational and financial capabilities to pioneer and transform essential and local infrastructure assets, in sectors such as energy transition, telecoms, transport and environment. The firm delivers essential infrastructure assets to local communities and authorities, driving significant growth across its portfolio. 

Cube has raised an aggregate €4.2 billion and manages four funds. Cube Infrastructure Fund, Cube Infrastructure Fund II and Cube Infrastructure Fund III are focusing on investments in regulated, brownfield infrastructure. The Connecting Europe Broadband Fund is a fund dedicated to greenfield investments in broadband infrastructure projects. 

Since its creation, Cube has successfully made more than 40 investments across Europe and will continue to invest in profitable and responsible investment opportunities with strong growth potential.

text:
2025 was an exciting year for us here at Cube! 

From strengthening our leadership team with the hiring of two new partners to completing our largest co-investment capital fundraise to date, the last 12 months have brought a range of significant achievements.

Through all of these milestones, we have remained focused on investing in essential infrastructure that benefits local communities. We are excited for 2026 and all of the opportunities it will bring.

Swipe through to see Cube’s five highlights of 2025 ⬇️

text:
A powerful milestone for SEPUR, France’s leading pure-play municipal waste collection operator: its fleet will be 33% electric by mid-next year.

By then, 640 electric vehicles will be on the road, driving the transition to cleaner, greener infrastructure. Cube is proud to support this journey toward a more sustainable future.

Since its creation, Cube has successfully made more than 40 investments across Europe and will continue to invest in profitable and responsible investment opportunities with strong growth potential.

text:
“Infrastructure is the foundation of thriving communities.”

Since the creation of Cube IM in 2007, this philosophy has guided us as we continue to pioneer essential local infrastructure across Europe, creating value for local communities and our investors.

In our new corporate video, we look at what defines and differentiates Cube as a firm, our strategy and our role in enabling today's communities and local authorities to meet tomorrow's needs.

From being an early entrant into now-mainstream sectors such as fiber and public transport, to identifying and investing in new high-growth sectors within European infrastructure, Cube transforms companies that deliver strong financial performance and positive environmental and social outcomes.

Watch the video to hear from our team members!

text:
Great to see Cube's Head of ESG Erwann Duquesne sharing the firm's insights at the 2025 Luxembourg Sustainable Finance Initiative.

Erwann was a speaker in the EU Sustainable Finance Regulatory Framework masterclass, where he explained how Cube IM ensures compliance with ESG regulations through longstanding practices that are already deeply embedded in its investment process.

Thank you to LSFI for this event. The Cube team are excited to keep contributing to discussions around sustainable finance, particularly on leveraging ESG in infrastructure strategies.

text:
CVC Secondary Partners today announced the completion of a landmark $1.1 billion managed fund secondary transaction in a new strategic partnership with M&G Investments.

Under the terms of the partnership, funds advised by CVC have committed to acquire a portfolio of private equity interests - primarily in mature North American mid-market buyout funds. Building on M&G and CVC’s history of collaboration, the transaction delivers immediate exposure to a diversified portfolio of high-quality US private equity funds.

Louise Boothby, a Managing Partner at CVC Secondary Partners, said: “We are pleased to be partnering with M&G again in this landmark transaction. This represents an exciting expansion of our long-standing relationship with M&G and importantly provides our investors with exposure to a seasoned and diversified portfolio, managed by some of the highest quality private equity managers.”

text:
CVC DIF, the infrastructure strategy of CVC, is pleased to announce that it has signed an agreement to sell American Roads to John Laing Group.

American Roads is a transportation infrastructure platform that owns and operates a portfolio of toll road infrastructure in the United States, serving approximately seven million trips annually. The portfolio includes three bridges in Alabama, as well as the concession-lease for the American-side of the Detroit–Windsor Tunnel, a strategically important cross-border tunnel connecting Detroit, Michigan, USA with Windsor, Canada. 

Andrew Freeman, Partner and Head of Divestments at CVC DIF: “American Roads is a strong illustration of CVC DIF’s active ownership and long-term approach to infrastructure investing. Through close partnership with management, disciplined operational optimisation, and thoughtful capital structuring, we have developed a resilient and well-governed platform of essential transport assets. We are pleased to have agreed this transaction with John Laing, a highly experienced infrastructure investor, and believe American Roads is very well positioned to continue delivering critical connectivity in its next phase of ownership.”

text:
Today CVC Capital Partners plc announces its Full-Year 2025 Activity Update. 
 
Rob Lucas, CEO at CVC: “2025 saw record realisations at attractive returns underpinning our confidence in future fundraising, including Fund X. More than half of our FPAUM now comprises Credit, Secondaries and Infrastructure, which delivered 12% growth in 2025. Private Wealth aggregate value increased almost 5x, and our partnership with AIG and announced acquisition of Marathon position us strongly for accelerated growth, including in the Insurance channel. We are highly confident in our ability to build on this momentum in 2026.”
 
CVC Capital Partners plc will publish its Full-Year 2025 Results on 11 March.
 
View our key business updates here: https://lnkd.in/ehUDwSUb

authorHeadline:
CVC DIF (formerly DIF Capital Partners) is a leading global mid-market infrastructure equity fund manager.

Founded in 2005 and headquartered in Amsterdam, the Netherlands, CVC DIF has c. €18 billion of infrastructure assets under management in energy transition, transport, utilities and digitalisation.

With over 240 people in 12 offices, CVC DIF offers a unique market approach, combining a global presence with the benefits of strong local networks and sector-focused investment capabilities. 

CVC DIF forms the infrastructure strategy of leading global private markets manager CVC. This partnership allows CVC DIF to benefit from CVC’s global platform, with 30 offices across five continents.

text:
We are excited to announce Christian Velasco, Managing Director of DigitalBridge, will be leading a virtual teach-in to explore the decarbonization imperative across global transport and the three key opportunity areas: electrified fleet and equipment platforms, clean charging infrastructure networks and next-generation transport electrification.
 
As the sector continues advancing towards becoming net-zero through these key focus areas, next-gen mobility infrastructure is continuing to evolve and become increasingly integrated with AI infrastructure supporting modern transport systems. While institutional adoption of next-gen transportation is still in its early stages, we believe it offers long-term structural growth drivers alongside a meaningful decarbonization pathway.
 
Webinar details below
Wednesday, 11 March
2:00 – 3:00 PM GMT
 
Please contact clients@digitalbridge.com if you wish to register for the event. Note that this webinar is for institutional investors only.

#digitalinfrastructure Christian V. #decarbonization #mobiiltyinfrastructure #aiinfrastructure

text:
Power is the new currency of AI.
 
At Metro Connect 2026, DigitalBridge CEO Marc Ganzi made the case that winning in AI infrastructure won't be decided at the application layer, it will be decided by who can deliver power.
 
The numbers tell the story. The US grid is leasing around 12 gigawatts per year while adding only four. Developers approaching utilities today are being told to expect connection dates between 2030 and 2032, well beyond the life cycle of the GPU generations those data centers are being built to serve.
 
"If you're a developer of digital infrastructure, you are now in the power business."
 
Marc also identified water as the next frontier and urged the industry to start thinking in 20-year investment cycles rather than five-year models. The opportunity is enormous, but only for those building with the right time horizon.
 
Read the full recap from Metro Connect 2026 here: https://lnkd.in/epKjWvym
 
#DigitalBridge #AIInfrastructure #MetroConnect2026 #DataCenters #DigitalInfrastructure #EnergyTransition Capacity - a techoraco brand

text:
What if the answer to the data center power crisis was water?

In his latest piece for InterGlobix Magazine, DigitalBridge Senior Advisor Christian Belady puts real numbers behind a counterintuitive thesis: going waterless may actually cost the industry more—in capacity, in dollars, and in community goodwill.

For a 500MW data center, the difference between evaporative and non-evaporative cooling isn't just an engineering choice. It's 52MW of additional IT capacity, over $42 million in annual energy savings, and a model for genuine water replenishment that leaves communities better off than before.

At a moment when interconnection and generation constraints are among the biggest challenges in our industry, Christian makes the case that water—used wisely and responsibly—is one of the most powerful tools we have.

Read the full analysis in Interglobix Magazine. https://lnkd.in/g5igMgUa

#DataCenters #DigitalInfrastructure #Sustainability #WaterStewardship #EnergyEfficiency #Interconnection #Colocation #PUE #CloudInfrastructure #DigitalBridge

Wilson Chung, Managing Director, Investment Management at DigitalBridge Investment Management, will join global industry leaders at the Infralogic Investors Forum Australia 2026 on Tuesday, 3 March 2026 at 9:30 AM Australian Eastern Daylight Time (AEDT).

The session, Global spotlight: Surviving and thriving as infrastructure investors, will feature Hans-Martin Aerts of APG Asset Management, Jan Brand of Ontario Teachers'​ Pension Plan, and Hemant Daga of Neo Wealth and Asset Management, moderated by James Abbott of Corrs Chambers Westgarth.

text:
Christian Belady, Senior Advisor at DigitalBridge, shares a timely perspective on the evolving relationship between data centers and the communities they serve in his article, “PIMBY: Please In My Backyard Again” in the latest edition of InterGlobix Magazine.
 
As AI and cloud demand accelerates, community resistance is becoming the rule rather than the exception. Christian challenges the industry to move beyond mitigation and toward transformation — reimagining data centers as net contributors that lower residential electricity rates, create water abundance, and integrate infrastructure into nature-positive community destinations. It’s a call to think bigger about how digital infrastructure can align long-term growth with local trust and shared prosperity.
 
Read the full article and join the conversation on how we build the next generation of community-centered digital infrastructure.
 
#DigitalInfrastructure #DataCenters #AI #Sustainability #EnergyTransition #CommunityEngagement

text:
🌊 We are pleased to expand our long-standing partnership with #WWF and announce that we are now supporting a new marine conservation project in the German Baltic Sea near Stralsund and Rügen. 🌊

🌱 Building on our marine protection work in Belize since 2021, the project focuses on restoring local seagrass meadows. These key ecosystems provide habitat for marine species, protect coasts from erosion and storms, and absorb CO₂ at high efficiency, up to 35 times faster than tropical rainforests. These ecosystems are under severe pressure from pollution, nutrient inputs, construction, fishing, and the impacts of the climate crisis. 

🌱 The project applies drones, cameras, sonar, and AI to map existing seagrass beds and identify optimal sites for replanting, with the goal of restoring around 100 hectares. 

🌎 This work strengthens our commitment to effective environmental action and to protecting essential marine ecosystems. 

Click here for more information: http://dws.co/6043AQhNJ

#CSR #MarineProtection #SpeciesProtection #ClimateChange

Congratulations to Fidra Energy on a well‑deserved win at the 2025 PFI Awards.

Thorpe Marsh being recognized as European Battery Storage Deal of the Year is a testament to the scale, ambition and collaborative effort behind what is now the largest standalone project‑financed battery storage project globally.

Great to see partners including EIG, the National Wealth Fund, Santander UK and the wider lender group together in London to mark this milestone.
authorHeadline:
EIG is a leading institutional investor in the global energy and infrastructure sectors with $24.3 billion under management as of September 30, 2025. EIG specializes in private investments in energy and energy-related infrastructure on a global basis. During its 43-year history, EIG has committed over $51.7 billion to the energy sector through 421 projects or companies in 44 countries on six continents. EIG’s clients include many of the leading pension plans, insurance companies, endowments, foundations and sovereign wealth funds in the U.S., Asia and Europe. EIG is headquartered in Washington, D.C. with offices in Houston, London, Sydney, Rio de Janeiro, Hong Kong and Seoul.
For additional information, please visit EIG’s website at www.eigpartners.com.

EIG appreciates the support of many loyal clients, business partners and friends, but we are limited in how we can discuss such matters in a social media forum. SEC rules for investment advisers prohibit our use of endorsements to promote or market our services. Accepting recommendations and endorsements on LinkedIn is viewed by the SEC as a violation of law, and, accordingly, we do not accept them as a matter of policy. text:
We're delighted to share that Jim Reinhart has joined the Firm as Operating Partner. Jim brings more than 35 years of experience leading high growth, operationally intensive companies across the real assets, financial services, and technology sectors, having held multiple CEO and other C-suite positions throughout his career. Jim will spearhead value creation initiatives across portfolio companies, partnering with portfolio company leadership teams and Ember's investment team. 

#valuecreation #operatingpartner #privateequity #portfoliooperations #realassets
authorHeadline: Ember is a private equity platform delivering capital solutions to middle market businesses and assets that contribute to reduced carbon intensity and enhanced resource efficiency. Ember targets experienced management teams seeking to create scalable platforms utilizing proven technologies and processes. text:
We're delighted to share that Jim Reinhart has joined the Firm as Operating Partner. Jim brings more than 35 years of experience leading high growth, operationally intensive companies across the real assets, financial services, and technology sectors, having held multiple CEO and other C-suite positions throughout his career. Jim will spearhead value creation initiatives across portfolio companies, partnering with portfolio company leadership teams and Ember's investment team. 

#valuecreation #operatingpartner #privateequity #portfoliooperations #realassets

Thanks to all those who joined Ember's Annual General Meeting this year. We are always humbled to be surrounded by this group of limited partners, management teams, and thought leaders, all focused on building more efficient and effective infrastructure solutions for our communities and industries.

We were honored to be joined by Rohit Aggarwala, NYC Commissioner for the Department of Environmental Planning and Professor at Columbia SIPA, in addition to Catherine Coleman Flowers, Founder of The Center for Rural Enterprise and Environmental Justice. 

H2O Innovation | Ground/Water Treatment & Technology, LLC | OnSyte Performance | Porous Technologies, LLC | SunShare | Caban | ReGenerate Energy, LLC | Advanced Recycling Technologies (ART) | Earthwise Environmental Solutions

text:
Ember's Impact Report 2024: Solutions for a Changing World is now live!
 
🔹 This year, we spotlight infrastructure resilience and adaptation. With ~90% of tracked climate finance directed toward mitigation, resilience & adaptation is a critical but under-prioritized investment theme.
 
🔹 We discuss how distributed infrastructure, grid hardening, emergency response systems, and other solutions help prevent, prepare for, and recover effectively from disruptive events, and showcase how Ember portfolio companies including Caban, OnSyte Performance, Porous Technologies, LLC, H2O Innovation and Advanced Recycling Technologies (ART) are contributing to delivering more resilient infrastructure solutions.
 
🔹 Overall, our portfolio companies continue to achieve meaningful results. In 2024 alone, they delivered:
 
💧 46.6 billion gallons of wastewater treated
 ⚡ 898,000 MWh of renewable energy generated
 🌱 249,000 tons of CO₂e emissions avoided
 
#ImpactInvesting #ClimateResilience #SustainableInfrastructure #ClimateAdaptation #SustainableInvesting #ImpactMeasurement #IMM

Discover more in the full report: https://lnkd.in/eUDgM5c9
 
Please see the full report for the methodologies used to calculate the metrics above and for important disclaimers.

text:
We are excited to share that Ember has earned the highest possible rating—Platinum—through BlueMark’s independent impact verification process, Fund ID. This achievement highlights our commitment to investing in sustainable infrastructure that delivers both strong financial returns and measurable, meaningful impact.

The dedication of our investment team, along with the collaboration of our portfolio companies, contributed to Ember's outperformance across all four pillars:

▪ 𝗜𝗺𝗽𝗮𝗰𝘁 𝗦𝘁𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗲𝗴𝘆: 𝟳𝟴% (vs. benchmark avg. 63%)
▪ 𝗜𝗺𝗽𝗮𝗰𝘁 𝗠𝗮𝗻𝗮𝗴𝗲𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁: 𝟳𝟳% (vs. benchmark avg. 68%)
▪ 𝗜𝗺𝗽𝗮𝗰𝘁 𝗥𝗲𝗽𝗼𝗿𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴: 𝟲𝟯% (vs. benchmark avg. 50%)
▪ 𝗜𝗺𝗽𝗮𝗰𝘁 𝗚𝗼𝘃𝗲𝗿𝗻𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲: 𝟴𝟲% (vs. benchmark avg. 67%)

We’re proud of this recognition and remain committed to driving investments with profit and purpose.

#infrastructure #sustainability #IMM #ImpactInvesting #ESG

text:
Last week, as a keynote speaker at the MIT Sloan Investment Conference, ECP Founder and Executive Chairman Doug Kimmelman shared perspectives on building long-term success in business and the leap to entrepreneurship in finance.
 
The discussion touched on the evolving energy landscape, rising demand growth, and pinpointing the need for a diversified mix of technologies in supporting a reliable grid.
 
He closed with a simple reflection on careers: rather than chasing passion alone, focus on doing meaningful work and developing deep interest in an industry, where passion often follows.
authorHeadline:
ECP (Energy Capital Partners) is an investor in critical infrastructure focused on electrification, decarbonization, reliability and sustainability. Founded in 2005, ECP’s investment team has an early mover advantage in the sector as we have been successfully investing through multiple energy transitions over the last 30 years, including ECP’s senior partners whom have been investing in critical electrification and decarbonization real asset infrastructure since the mid 1990s and are experienced owners of ESG-related businesses. With 100 employees and over 825+ years of cumulative energy experience, ECP is built on cutting edge strategies and opportunities linked to decarbonization and clean energy.
Follow us on SoundCloud: https://soundcloud.com/energy-capital-partners

text:
This week, ECP hosted a US power and environment infrastructure seminar in Seoul, Korea, where our team shared perspectives on the US power macro environment, load growth outlook, and key market dynamics across gas, renewables, and environmental infrastructure.
 
It was a great opportunity to engage with a diverse group of institutional investors and exchange thoughtful views on drivers shaping infrastructure investing today.

text:
Triple Oak Power works closely with community partners to develop wind and solar projects on working ranches and farms, providing landowners with a reliable, long-term income stream and directing investment into local economies while continuing traditional agricultural operations.

Stories like this highlight how clean energy projects can deliver lasting value for families and communities across the U.S. In this clip, Triple Oak’s CEO & Cofounder Jesse Gronner speaks with Bill Elkins of Rocking Chair Ranch about the project’s impact on his ranch and community, and how these partnerships represent a win-win: strengthening rural economies while supporting the expansion of America’s energy infrastructure at a time of rapidly growing demand for power.

text:
Atlantica Sustainable Infrastructure Ltd, a global sustainable infrastructure company with a diversified portfolio of renewable energy, storage, and transmission assets, continues to execute on its growth strategy across core markets.

In North America, Atlantica completed the acquisition of a Canadian renewable energy platform, adding 236 MW of operating wind assets backed by long-term PPAs, alongside a 0.6 GW development pipeline of renewables and storage projects. The company also successfully closed project financing for the Winnifred project, further supporting continued growth in the region.

In Spain, construction of the Caparacena solar PV project in Granada has been successfully completed, adding 22 MW of capacity and further strengthening Atlantica’s growing solar footprint in the region, with commercial operations expected in March 2026.

In Chile, Atlantica’s Chile PV 3 BESS expansion reached commercial operations, bringing an additional 60 MW of battery energy storage online to enhance grid reliability in the country’s energy-intensive northern region.

Together, these milestones reflect Atlantica’s continued progress across solar, storage, wind, and platform expansion, with a diversified pipeline spanning multiple geographies.

 In this clip, ECP Partner Andrew Gilbert explains why digital infrastructure developers partner with ECP: an existing power asset footprint, deep knowledge of local markets and interconnection processes, and in-house manufacturing that helps de-risk supply chains.

text:
We are pleased to announce that we have agreed to sell Cornerstone Generation, a ~2.6 GW natural gas power generation portfolio, to Talen Energy.
 
Our acquisition of Cornerstone was built around a clear thesis: efficient, baseload natural gas generation in PJM - particularly Ohio - is critical to meeting accelerating power demand driven by data centers and broader electrification.
 
We continue to believe this will remain an exciting narrative in PJM and are excited to enhance our exposure to Ohio’s growth by remaining a shareholder in Talen.

Read the full announcement here: https://lnkd.in/g4qM9XvV

text:
Today marks the closing of a major milestone for Calpine and the culmination of a seven-year partnership.

When ECP acquired Calpine in 2018, we believed flexible, lower-emission generation would remain essential to a reliable grid. Over the past seven years, in close partnership with Calpine’s leadership and employees, the team executed against that thesis amid a rapidly evolving energy landscape: strengthening operations, expanding geothermal and renewable capacity, investing in storage, and working to consistently provide reliability across some of the nation’s fastest-growing power markets.

Those efforts have positioned Calpine to combine with Constellation and help form a scaled, diversified generation platform with nearly 60 GW of capacity across a broad mix of resources.

We are grateful to the Calpine management team and employees for their partnership and performance. Through disciplined stewardship and long-term vision, together we are proud to have helped enable the creation of a national leader positioned to support growing electricity demand with dependable power. 

For full release, click here: https://lnkd.in/gmvy339x

text:
This holiday season, ECP is proud to continue a meaningful tradition of giving back.

We’re entering our third year partnering with Turning Point Community Services (TPCS), an emergency short-term shelter in Irvington, NJ that supports women and families as they work toward stability and independence.

Through our Holiday Wishes program, ECP employees help provide essential items, winter clothing, and thoughtful gifts to families during a critical time of year. 

Founded in 2002, TPCS helps women and families break the cycles of abuse and homelessness by providing housing, support, and services that enable independence and stability within 6-9 months.

Learn more about TPCS and their important work: https://tpcsinc.org/

Unser Team vor Ort: Sandro Gschwend und Marc M.

Sind Sie ebenfalls in Berlin? Dann lassen Sie uns treffen – wir freuen uns auf den Austausch.

Our team on site: Sandro Gschwend and Marc M. 

Are you in Berlin as well? Then let’s meet – we look forward to the exchange.

EIP welcomed back MBA and master’s students from the London Business School to our headquarters in Zurich earlier this month. In an interactive session with members of our Investments team, we discussed the role of long-term capital in building resilient energy systems and shared a behind-the-scenes look at our approach to infrastructure investing at EIP. 

Thank you to the students for spending the morning with us and for the smart questions, fresh perspectives and enthusiasm you brought along.

text:
After 15 years of declining electricity consumption, Europe stands at an inflection point. Power demand is growing again — driven by factors like digitalization, industrial electrification, adoption of electric vehicles and hotter temperatures. Yet the continent's energy infrastructure is straining under the weight of chronic underinvestment and grids that predate the energy transition by decades.

Our Five Trends Shaping Energy Infrastructure in 2026 illustrate why energy infrastructure investment continues to become more important year after year.

👉 Explore all five trends here: https://lnkd.in/dKc2bVmi

Die SBB CFF FFS setzt auf 100 % erneuerbare Energie für den Bahnbetrieb.
Beat Deuber, Mitglied der Geschäftsleitung Infrastruktur, SBB, erklärt, wie Investitionen in Wasserkraftwerke, die Integration von Photovoltaik und die enge Zusammenarbeit mit Swissgrid AG – dem nationalen Übertragungsnetzbetreiber und einem EIP-Portfolio-Unternehmen – die Versorgung sichern.

Mit jährlich 3 Milliarden Franken für Unterhalt und Erneuerung des Netzes sowie innovativen Lösungen zur Netznutzung bleibt die SBB Vorreiterin in Sachen Effizienz und Versorgungssicherheit – und gestaltet die Energietransformation aktiv mit.

text:
A lot of infrastructure doesn’t need reinvention. It needs better ownership.

EQT's Chairperson of EQT Real Assets, Lennart Blecher, explains why infrastructure investing is as much about operational capability as it is about capital. At least when it's done well, that is.

Across energy, transport, digital, and social infrastructure, these assets provide essential services – and improving them takes teams on the ground, sector expertise, and long-term focus.

It’s a reminder that infrastructure returns aren’t driven by timing the cycle, but by steadily improving how critical systems actually work.

Have you seen a similar effect in your industry?

text:
How often do you stop to ponder the power of invisible infrastructure? 

An Instagram post uploaded on a train, a Zoom call that doesn’t glitch, an AI query answered in seconds – each one depends on data moving near the speed of light and systems that stay stable when demand surges. 

The same is true in our physical lives. Hot water, home heating, every kilowatt-hour used in a home has to be delivered, measured and managed through layers of infrastructure that most people never notice.

This is the world we invest in through EQT’s Active Core Infrastructure strategy. 

What most of us picture when we think about infrastructure – bridges, roads and railways – still attract the bulk of investor attention and capital, but we see opportunities in the distributed networks that sit behind modern life: meters that track heat and hot water in rental apartments, small cells that provide wireless coverage in dense cities, or the ground beneath mobile towers. It is infrastructure that is essential yet often unseen.

Read more about our infrastructure investing strategy at the link in the comments.

text:
The cloud drove the first wave of data centers. AI is changing the rules entirely.

In less than a decade, demand for data centers has shifted from cloud-driven growth to an AI-led buildout at a completely different scale. By 2030, global data-center capacity needs are expected to triple – with AI accounting for the majority of that demand.
But the constraint isn’t interest or capital. It’s power.

Hyperscalers are forecast to spend more than $500bn on data centers in 2026 alone, while grid capacity, transmission and permitting are struggling to keep pace. Where the compute gets built – and how it’s powered – is becoming the defining question.

This carousel breaks down what’s changing, why energy now sits at the center of digital infrastructure, and how platforms like EdgeConneX are adapting as AI reshapes the physical internet.

Full case study in the comments.

Save this if you work in infrastructure, energy or AI

text:
In life sciences, Europe is a tale of two halves. 

Its laboratories and scientists are world-leading, but the region falls short in reaping the rewards of its home-grown innovation. Financial support from European investors pales in comparison to the U.S., leading many start-ups to look across the Atlantic to access deeper pools of capital. 

“There’s a lot less capital available in Europe than in the U.S.,” René Kuijten, head of EQT Life Sciences, tells ThinQ by EQT. “We need more funding for the stars.”

The good news: European leaders recognize the chasm between scientific prowess and investment and are taking steps to address it. The region’s edge in fundamental research provides a strong foundation on which to build. 

Link to the full story in the comments.

text:
Everyone agrees that the world needs more data centers – but where should we build them?

McKinsey forecasts that around $7tn of new data-center investment will be needed by 2030 to keep up with AI demand. But scaling to meet this demand isn’t as simple as finding land and plugging in servers. 

Power limits, local policy, community pushback, and geopolitics are shaping where capacity can (and can’t) be built. Already, construction has been paused or disrupted in major hubs, from Amsterdam to Dublin. AI has the potential to offer unparalleled productivity and economic benefits – but if the infrastructure stalls, so does progress.

What do you think? Is the next wave built where power is cheapest – or where approvals are fastest?

text:
Global diversification is a hot topic among investors who have benefitted from the growth of U.S. markets in recent years, explains Peter Aliprantis, EQT’s Head of Americas Private Wealth.

In pretty much every conversation I’ve had with wealth advisors over the last few months, the discussion has turned to the pros and cons of growing allocations in Europe and Asia.

Public markets in the U.S. are close to all-time highs, so investors are rightly asking: what next? Big names on Wall Street have expressed concern that a correction is on the horizon, citing geopolitical instability and AI overhype.

Meanwhile, in U.S. private markets, companies are staying private for longer and competition for deals is as intense as ever. Seven of the world’s ten largest private markets firms are U.S.-based and their allocations are weighted heavily towards the U.S. 

But when domestic opportunities become constrained, it’s wise to look overseas, and many U.S. firms are now turning their attention to Europe and Asia. 

For EQT, however, the global diversification mindset has been ingrained in our DNA from the very beginning.

Read Peter’s entire post on diversifying global investments at the link in comments.

text:
Europe has an investment problem.

European households save more than their U.S. counterparts – yet far less of that capital ends up backing companies, innovation or growth.

The consequences are structural. Between 2009 and 2023, net household wealth grew 55% in the euro area, versus 151% in the U.S. Fewer deep capital pools mean shallower markets, with fewer companies able to scale at home.

That gap shows up in private markets too: PE and VC assets amount to ~8% of Europe’s GDP, compared with ~17% in the U.S. No wonder so many European success stories ultimately move across the Atlantic.

European policymakers are now trying to change the region's risk tolerance. Initiatives like the Savings and Investments Union and reforms to ELTIFs aim to reduce friction, modernize rules, and make investing feel less like speculation – and more like long-term participation in growth.

Of course, aligning rules across 27 countries won't be easy. But if Europe wants stronger companies, deeper markets, and better long-term outcomes, mobilizing its savings base could be pivotal.

Read the full piece via the link in the comments.

text:
For years, the story has been that Europe is falling behind the US and China in tech.

But that’s not the full picture.

Europe has a once-in-a-generation window of opportunity. The raw materials are already here: world-class talent, deep research, and a growing base of ambitious startups.

In our new report, Seizing Europe’s Strategic Tech Opportunity, we show how Europe is already producing a large and expanding cohort of companies across the most strategic tech sectors of the next decade, including AI, robotics and energy. The pipeline includes hundreds of emerging future unicorns, and by 2030 the number of scale-ups is expected to grow by more than 2.5x to around 1,900.

The opportunity is real. The question is whether we move fast enough to seize it.

Find the link to our report in the comments.

text:
Sueann Yeo, EQT’s Head of APAC Private Wealth, participated in a panel at the Asia Private Equity Forum 2026, organized by the Hong Kong Venture Capital and Private Equity Association.

The forum is one of the region’s leading private markets gatherings, bringing together nearly 800 professional investors, GPs, and industry professionals. Moderated by Brad McCarthy (Carlyle), the panel explored a new era in private markets driven by the growing influence of private wealth capital.

Sueann joined panelists from HSBC, iDirect and UBS to discuss how private wealth could reshape fund structures, investor access and long-term partnerships between asset managers and distribution platforms. Key themes included the evolution of evergreen versus closed-ended structures, investor profile and risk considerations, as well as the building blocks of successful GP–wealth partnerships — from alignment and education to scalability and trust.

Looking ahead, the discussion also touched on structural growth in alternatives, product innovation and the inflection points likely to define the next phase of private wealth, underscoring both the momentum in the space and the importance of collaboration.

text:
Ted Frith, Managing Director, Business Development, UK at Equitix, will be joining the panel at the Infrastructure Deep Dive Webinar hosted by The Good Economy Partnership Limited - Scaling-Up Local Investing for Place-Based Impact - on 5 March.

Ted will be speaking alongside Sam Monger (Head of PBI & Strategic Advisory, The Good Economy Partnership Limited), Nadeem Hussain (co-Chief Investment Officer and Head of Private Markets, LGPS Central), Simon Povey (co-Head of Europe, Capital Formation Group, Office for Investment) and Shelley De Souza (Director, Banking and Investments, National Wealth Fund) to discuss how infrastructure can support LGPS local investment strategies and unlock long-term, place-based impact.

As a long-term investor in critical infrastructure, we see how well-structured investments can support local growth, strengthen essential services and contribute to net zero, while also providing commercial robustness and positive community outcomes.

The session will explore infrastructure as a strategic asset class for LGPS, covering opportunity across sectors, investment structures, and governance considerations - followed by a live Q&A with the panel.

#lgps #netzero #infrastructure #placebasedimpactinvesting

text:
🔔 Equitix is pleased to welcome a new financing partnership supporting the continued growth of Andion CH4 Renewables across Europe, a portfolio company backed by the Equitix Next Generation Fund.
 
Andion, a biogas developer and operator, has reached financial close on a c.€70 million facility provided by Private Credit at Goldman Sachs Alternatives, enabling the advancement of its near-term development pipeline in Italy and the Nordic region. As part of the transaction, Andion’s existing shareholders, led by the Equitix Next Generation Fund, have also made a material equity contribution.
 
➡️ With policy tailwinds including REPowerEU and Fit for 55 driving momentum in the European biomethane sector, engineered biogas infrastructure is playing an increasingly important role in supporting energy security, circular waste management and net-zero ambitions. This milestone reflects Andion’s strong platform and Equitix’s long-standing commitment to backing high-quality, sustainable infrastructure. It also reinforces our position as Europe's leading small to medium mid-market infrastructure investor, supporting scalable platforms that advance the circular economy and deliver resilient, long-term value.
 
🔗 Read more in the full press release here: https://lnkd.in/ejZwjPmT

text:
Equitix’s Director of Investment Stewardship and Sustainability, Joe Robinson, speaks to LAPF Investments about how Equitix uses data and AI to drive its active climate stewardship strategy.
 
📣 At the heart of this strategy is a commitment to creating a lasting legacy - building and stewarding essential infrastructure that delivers value not just for today’s communities, but for generations to come.
 
From embedding climate considerations throughout the investment lifecycle to partnering with the UK Space Agency on AI-enabled climate risk analysis, the article highlights how technology, engagement and governance come together to support sustainable infrastructure for the future.
 
➡️ Read the full article to learn more about how Equitix is using data-led insights to inform investment decisions and climate stewardship here: https://lnkd.in/exj53hBr 

#responsibleinvestment #infrastructureinvesting #climaterisk #sustainableinfrastructure #climatestewardship

📢 Equitix is proud to sponsor the Infrastructure Investor Global Summit, Berlin 2026.

As Europe’s leading small to mid-market infrastructure investor, we’ll join other leading global businesses helping shape the next phase of sustainable, long-term infrastructure investment. From the energy transition and digitalisation to transport, social infrastructure and sustainability, infrastructure delivers enduring value globally.

We look forward to engaging with the global infrastructure community in Berlin.
 
🔔 Attending the Summit? Reach out to our team for a 1:1 meeting or enquire about joining our dinner ahead of the main event (places extremely limited): IR@equitix.com
 
#IIglobalsummit #infrastructureinvestment #europeaninfrastructure #midmarket #infrastructuretolife

⚡ In December 2025, Equitix signed an agreement to acquire a substantial minority stake in Viridor Group, the UK’s leading operator of Energy-from-Waste facilities, from KKR, with a path to acquire up to 50%.

➡️ This week, Equitix hosts its explainer webinar with live Q&A featuring representatives from the project team; Aisha Hamid, CFA, Investment Director; Dr Egan Archer, Head of Renewable Power Joe Robinson, Head of Stewardship & Sustainability and moderated by COO Paul Winters. Together they’ll explore Viridor’s market leading position in the circular economy, how it creates long-term value and why this is such a strategic investment.

📩 If you would like access to the webinar or wish to submit questions in advance, please contact IR@equitix.com.

#energytransition #infrastructureinvesting #sustainability #carboncapture

text:
This week, Equitix had the pleasure of welcoming 23 students to our 16th Uptree event.
 
💡 This biannual event provided attendees with the opportunity to explore the infrastructure sector, gain insight into the breadth of career paths it offers, and ask their own questions about the industry’s role in supporting essential services and the transition to a more sustainable future.
 
Their engagement and curiosity were evident throughout, and it was encouraging to see such strong interest from the next generation of talent.
 
🌟 We’re proud of our long-standing partnership with Uptree, which reflects our commitment to widening access to careers for young people from all walks of life and supporting them as they begin to shape their professional journeys.
 
By offering direct exposure to real‑world projects and human perspectives, we aim to help students build their confidence, broaden their understanding, and make informed decisions about their future.
 
Huge thanks to our team members who took time out to join the day and share their story to our guests, and to our terrific HR team for another successful event.

Orlagh McCauley-Bové
David Conlon
Joe Robinson
Abhimanyu Verma
