text:
At Equitix, we are focused on supporting the LGPS (Local Government Pension Scheme) investment community with practical insight and expertise as funds navigate an increasingly complex investment landscape. As part of this commitment, Dr Egan Archer, Managing Director and Head of Energy at Equitix, will be speaking on the clean energy panel at this year’s LAPF (Local Authority Pension Fund) Strategic Investment Forum, organised by DG Publishing, at Fairmont Windsor Park. 

Egan will be joined by Anish Butani (Managing Director, Head of Infrastructure at bfinance), Barney Coles (Senior Managing Director, Co-Head of Clean Energy at Capital Dynamics) and Nick Dixon (Head of Pensions at Avon Pension Fund) to discuss how the energy transition is progressing, the challenges that remain, and what this means for infrastructure investment strategies. 

🔔 With net zero targets accelerating across the UK and Europe, this conversation is especially timely for LGPS funds looking to balance impact, resilience and long-term value creation. 

We look forward to being part of an engaging discussion on pool innovation and strategic asset allocation and to contributing to the wider dialogue shaping the future of infrastructure investing. 

🔗 To arrange a meeting with our team attending the forum - Ted Frith and Zainab L. - please contact IR@equitix.com. 

#infrastructuretolife #LAPF2026 #energytransition #cleanenergy #responsibleinvestment

text:
Last week, our team attended the Menai Suspension Bridge 200 Year Plaque Unveiling, marking 200 years of this iconic landmark and its vital connection between Anglesey and the mainland since its completion in 1826 by Thomas Telford. This event celebrates both the bridge’s legacy and the generations of engineers who have helped preserve it. 

➡️ As part of our A55 project portfolio, the bridge holds special significance. Recent safety and refurbishment works, alongside ongoing Phase Two activity delivered by UK Highways A55 Ltd and their contractors, are helping to ensure its long-term safety and performance. 

The event featured speeches from the Presidents of the Institution of Civil Engineers (ICE), the Chartered Institution of Highways & Transportation (CIHT), the Institution of Structural Engineers and the Chartered Institute of Logistics and Transport, alongside representatives from the Welsh Government. We’re also pleased to acknowledge Peter McDonald, Kash Rahuf CDir, Tom Cunningham, Jack Scott, and Kerry Evans CEng FIHE, FCIHT, FWES and many more! 

We are proud to be celebrating this milestone with industry peers and local stakeholders and honouring 200 years of engineering excellence. 🌟

authorHeadline: Fengate is a leading investment firm specializing in real assets, with a focus on infrastructure, private equity and real estate. With 250 team members across Canada and the U.S., Fengate leverages 50 years of entrepreneurial experience to deliver excellent investment results on behalf of its clients. The firm has been recognized as one of Canada’s Best Managed Companies since 2007 and as part of Waterstone Human Capital’s Canada’s Most Admired Corporate Cultures™. Fengate has been certified as a Great Place to Work® and was named one of Canada’s Best Workplaces™ and one of Canada’s Best Workplaces for Women, for Inclusion, for Mental Wellness, for Today’s Youth, for Professional Development and more. text:
We’re pleased to share the latest episode of Molecule to Market, featuring eMAX Health Systems Founder and CEO, Julian Casciano. Fengate Private Equity completed a strategic investment in eMAX Health in January 2025, and this episode offers a look at the vision and strategy behind the company's growth.

Julian shares what it truly takes to build in pharma services over the long term, from navigating early challenges and making deliberate pivots to scaling a trusted, technology-enabled market access partner for leading pharmaceutical companies. The episode also offers perspective on the private equity partnership process through a founder’s lens, underscoring the importance of early alignment, trust, and a shared long-term vision.

For those in pharma services, market access, or anyone interested in building a founder-led business for the long haul, this is well worth the listen.

authorHeadline:
Founded in 2014, Generate builds, finances, owns and operates sustainable infrastructure to deliver affordable and reliable resource solutions for companies, governments, and communities. 

Generate is the one-stop shop offering technology and project developers pioneering the Infrastructure Revolution the tailored funding and support they need to get projects built. Generate partners with over 40 technology and project developers and owns and operates more than 2,000 assets globally, across the clean energy, transportation and waste and water sectors.  With an Infrastructure-as-a-Service model, Generate serves over 2,000 customers, companies, communities, school districts and universities. Together, we are rebuilding the world. 

Generate is a Public Benefit Corporation. 

We build, own, operate and finance assets in:
- Sustainable Energy (microgrids, solar, energy storage, lighting, HVAC systems, fuel cells, geothermal, biomass & biogas, building automation & sensors, heat pumps, smart meters)
- Sustainable Mobility (electric vehicles, hydrogen vehicles, autonomous vehicles, charging depots & infrastructure, fleets)
- Sustainable Water, Waste & Agriculture (anaerobic digesters, wastewater treatment, desalination, food waste management, recycling/reuse, precision agriculture, farm automation, irrigation, sensors & meters).

To join the Infrastructure Revolution, visit generatecapital.com.

We’re proud to announce a C$60 million financing led by Fiera Infrastructure for Generate Upcycle's North American anaerobic digestion portfolio.

This is Generate’s first cross-currency, cross-border financing and the first food-waste RNG financing in our portfolio, reflecting both the scale and the maturity of Upcycle’s RNG platform.

Together with Fiera, we’re proving that creative capital and sustainable infrastructure can deliver measurable environmental and economic impact.

We’re excited to share that Generate has been named IJGlobal ESG Investor of the Year for 2025!

Generate was selected by an independent panel of judges for our leadership in advancing critical infrastructure by combining flexible capital solutions with deep operational expertise and market insight. 

A judge said: “Generate sets a new benchmark for ESG-driven equity deployment, combining flexible capital solutions with deep operational expertise across sectors like RNG, community solar, and industrial decarbonisation….With a $14 billion capital base and a clear commitment to circular economy and community impact, Generate exemplifies innovation, scale, and measurable ESG success.”

text:
We’re excited to announce our latest partnership with KeyState — bringing Generate’s total financing in 2025 to more than $1.4 billion. 

Generate Capital has closed an $85 million community solar tax equity fund with KeyState’s SOLCAP platform, supporting eight projects across New York and Illinois totaling 38 MWdc. These projects are on track to come online between Q4 2025 and Q1 2026.

This milestone marks Generate’s 10th community solar tax equity fund and 5th with KeyState since 2021, underscoring our role as one of the most active and trusted tax equity providers in the distributed clean energy sector.

Since entering the market in 2017, we’ve built one of the industry’s most experienced community solar platforms, owning and operating more than 430 MW of projects that deliver affordable, reliable power to households, businesses, and communities across the country.

Read the full release here: https://lnkd.in/e-BDASfy

text:
In his latest Expert View, CEO David Crane reflects on what it will take for the clean energy sector to deliver durable, long-term impact. 
The piece highlights several important themes shaping the next phase of the energy transition:
- The need for greater operational discipline and financial rigor
-  How scale and sector expertise can help attract institutional capital and     manage risk
- The importance of purpose-built financing structures to bridge early innovation and mature projects
- The role of consolidation in strengthening the sector when pursued intentionally
As David notes, moments of volatility can also be moments of clarity. By focusing on fundamentals and building resilient platforms, the sector can move beyond short-term cycles and create lasting value for communities, investors, and the planet.
https://lnkd.in/eSpWfExG
#EnergyTransition #CleanEnergy #Infrastructure  #Resilience

Established in 1981 to manage Singapore's foreign reserves, we strive to achieve good long-term real returns on assets under our management to preserve and enhance the value of Singapore's reserves. 

We have investments in over 40 countries and are headquartered in Singapore, with 11 offices in key financial cities worldwide.

text:
GIC is proud to lead the $30 billion Series G in Anthropic, an AI market-leader focused on enterprise AI, safety-focused LLMs, and coding applications, alongside Coatue Management. 

“Anthropic’s thoughtful approach to AI development is changing the way enterprises operate,” said Chris Emanuel, Head of the Technology Investment Group at GIC. “Our partnership and continued investment reflects our conviction in their visionary leadership team and technical depth as they expand access to advanced AI tools.”   

In just one year, the number of Anthropic customers spending over $100,000 annually on Claude has grown 7x, exemplifying their ability to leverage strategic partnerships and build high-impact use cases that win enterprise customers.

This new investment builds upon GIC’s significant investment in #Anthropic’s $13 billion Series F, and will support the company’s continued leadership in research, innovation and infrastructure expansion as they continue to grow their customer base.

text:
We were delighted to host more than 150 C‑suite and senior executives from over 100 leading financial institutions, corporates, asset managers, and funds to celebrate the expansion and relocation of our Japan office.

At the event, we were honoured to have Timothy Geithner, Chairman of Warburg Pincus LLC , and Kenichiro Yoshida, Executive Chairman of Sony Group Corporation, join us at the event to share their insights. Both spoke about the importance of adaptability in today’s geopolitical environment and highlighted Japan’s unique strengths. These include market stability, advanced technologies, and world‑class companies.

Japan has been an important market for GIC since the 1980s and we remain deeply committed to investing there for the long term. The long‑term nature of our capital aligns well with Japan’s corporate culture and we look forward to continuing to grow alongside our partners there in the years ahead.

#GIC #GICSG #investing

authorHeadline:
Global Infrastructure Partners (GIP) is a leading infrastructure investor that specializes in investing in, owning and operating some of the largest and most complex assets across the energy, transport, digital infrastructure and water and waste management sectors. With decarbonization central to our investment thesis, we are well positioned to support the global energy transition. Headquartered in New York, GIP has offices in Brisbane, Dallas, Delhi, Hong Kong, London, Melbourne, Mumbai, Singapore, Stamford and Sydney.

GIP has approximately $103 billion in assets under management. Our portfolio companies have combined annual revenues of approximately $75 billion and employ over 115,000 people. We believe that our focus on real infrastructure assets, combined with our deep proprietary origination network and comprehensive operational expertise, enables us to be responsible stewards of our investors' capital and to create positive economic impact for communities. For more information, visit www.global-infra.com.

text:
A year ago, Global Infrastructure Partners (GIP) and BlackRock announced that they were joining forces with Microsoft, MGX, and NVIDIA to launch the Artificial Intelligence Infrastructure Partnership (AIP). Since then, the partnership has expanded to include xAI, Kuwait Investment Authority, and Temasek. Together with our energy partners GE Vernova and NextEra Energy, Inc., and our technology partner Cisco, the partnership aims to mobilize significant private capital into the data centers and energy infrastructure needed to meet growing demand for AI and cloud workloads amid a digital transformation of the global economy.
 
Today, AIP is announcing its first investment. AIP, GIP, and MGX are acquiring 100% of the equity in Aligned Data Centers, which designs, builds and operates cutting-edge data campuses and data centers for hyperscalers, neocloud, and enterprise innovators. 
 
Today’s transaction brings together AIP’s ability to form long-term capital at scale with MGX’s global investment focus on AI and advanced technologies and GIP’s expertise as a leading infrastructure investor to fuel expansion of next-generation cloud and AI infrastructure.
 
Read more on our website: https://1blk.co/46TSgII

text:
XRG today announced it has closed its acquisition of an 11.7 percent equity stake in Phase 1 (Trains 1-3) of the Rio Grande LNG project in Brownsville, Texas, United States.
 
The investment is a strategic joint venture between Global Infrastructure Partners (GIP), a part of BlackRock and XRG, building upon the close partnership.
 
Rio Grande LNG is one of the most ambitious LNG export infrastructure projects in the US with approximately 48 million tonnes per annum (mtpa) of potential liquefaction capacity currently under construction or in development, underscoring the role of the US as a major energy exporter.
 
Construction of Trains 1-3 continues to progress smoothly, while Final Investment Decision (FID) for Train 4 was achieved in early September. In addition, XRG’s parent company ADNOC Group entered into a 20-year LNG offtake agreement for 1.9 mtpa from Rio Grande LNG Train 4.

text:
Global Infrastructure Partners (GIP) Chairman and CEO Bayo Ogunlesi was pleased to join Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis of Greece and Philipp Hildebrand at the Athens International Investor Summit (AIIS): Investing in Innovation and Infrastructure as Drivers of Growth. The Summit convened senior international investors, policymakers, and industry leaders, including more than 40 global institutions representing over $20 trillion in assets. 

BlackRock Vice Chairman Philipp Hildebrand said: “Greece’s economic transformation is testimony to the consistency and determination of the government and the Greek people.  As global infrastructure needs to accelerate, mobilizing private capital is essential — not just to meet today’s demands, but to shape a resilient and competitive future. This is a shared challenge across Europe, and one that calls for deeper collaboration between public institutions and private investors.  BlackRock is proud to support this progress, working alongside governments to help deliver lasting impact.”

text:
Global Infrastructure Partners (GIP), a part of BlackRock, is excited to announce that it has agreed to invest in Eni CCUS, a leading global carbon capture, utilization, and storage (CCUS) platform.

Eni CCUS currently includes the Liverpool Bay and Bacton projects in the UK and the L10 project in the Netherlands – cornerstone assets aimed at decarbonizing industrial clusters.

GIP views the energy transition as a generational investment opportunity and CCUS as a critical component of that transition. By permanently storing or repurposing captured CO2, it enables industries to meet climate targets, while maintaining energy security and industrial competitiveness.

We look forward to partnering with Eni to accelerate the development of these projects and to expand the Eni CCUS portfolio.

https://bit.ly/416kooI

text:
Global Infrastructure Partners (GIP), BlackRock, Microsoft and MGX welcome NVIDIA and xAI to the AI Infrastructure Partnership to drive investment in data centers and enabling infrastructure. 

Additionally, GE Vernova and NextEra Energy, Inc. have agreed to collaborate with AIP to accelerate the scaling of critical and diverse energy solutions for AI data centers. 

AIP has attracted significant capital and partner interest since its inception in September 2024, highlighting the growing demand for AI-ready data centers and power solutions. 

By investing in next-generation AI data centers and energy infrastructure, AIP is not just expanding capacity - it is shaping the future of AI-driven economic growth.

authorHeadline: Harbert Management Corporation (“HMC”) is an investment management firm focusing on alternative assets, with approximately $8.0 billion in Regulatory Assets Under Management as of January 31, 2026; asset classes include: Power, U.S. Real Estate, European Real Estate, Seniors Housing, Seniors Housing Development, Growth Equity and Credit Solutions.  HMC is headquartered in Birmingham, AL, USA and has offices in Atlanta, Charlotte, Dallas, Nashville, New York, Richmond, San Francisco, London, Luxembourg, Madrid and Paris. HMC and its affiliates make significant co-investments on the same terms as other investors in all their sponsored funds helping ensure a sharp focus on returns and strict controls around fund administration, legal, compliance and reporting.

text:
The recent acquisition of two senior housing communities in Stamford, CT marks an important milestone for HSAM’s senior housing platform.

Completed between 2018 and 2019, the communities comprise more than 200 units across independent living, assisted living, and memory care and are operating at near‑full occupancy—underscoring the durability of well‑located, amenity‑rich assets supported by strong demographics and stabilized performance.

Full Press Release here: https://lnkd.in/gu_6YwrQ 

#HSAM #SeniorHousing #RealAssets

text:
We believe the European alternative real estate market is well supported by a resilient macroeconomic backdrop, which provides a compelling setup for alternative real estate investment. 

Inflation across the Eurozone has returned to target, interest rates have fallen, and labor markets remain resilient despite muted GDP growth. Alongside this environment, capital markets dislocation, limited new supply, improving financing conditions and durable demographic tailwinds are converging to support an attractive entry point for alternative sector strategies across Europe. We believe living‑focused assets and other alternative sectors like self storage are particularly well positioned to deliver resilient income growth, capital preservation, and portfolio diversification through the next phase of the cycle.

#HSAM #RealAssets #Alternatives #Europe #2026Outlook

text:
The execution of two long-term hyperscale pre-leases at our PowerHouse Arcola campus in Loudoun County, Virginia, marks an important milestone for our digital investment platform.

Building on this momentum, HSAM has now executed more than 4 million square feet of hyperscale leasing across 18 leases and eight campuses, underscoring our ability to deliver large‑scale, power‑secured data center capacity in the world’s most supply‑constrained markets.

Northern Virginia remains the most important data center market globally, and PowerHouse Arcola reflects our continued conviction in markets driven by strong fundamentals, sustained demand, and long-term value creation.

text:
Carolyn Arida, Partner - Infrastructure, will be presenting at the Campus Energy Conference in Washington, DC, where she’ll spotlight our innovative public-private partnership with Appalachian State University. This campus initiative includes 156 faculty housing units and a next-generation district energy system powered by ninety 800-foot geo-exchange wells, wind turbines, and over 500 kW of solar energy delivering net-zero performance and exemplifying sustainable, low-carbon solutions. Commissioned in the summer of 2025, this project offers a blueprint for the future of campus infrastructure. Be sure to connect with Carolyn at the event and discover how these actionable strategies can help shape energy initiatives!

Register for event here: https://hubs.li/Q0432MZq0

text:
With Canada’s 15–19 year old domestic population projected to rise between 2.5% and 5.7% by 2034 and university enrollment growing by nearly 2,000 students on average over the last five years, demand remains deeply resilient. Provisioning rates remain low at just 15% nationally, and even with 20,000–30,000 new beds expected over five years, demand will continue to outpace supply.

These strong fundamentals and the rising adoption of purpose-built student housing should make this sector a high-conviction investment opportunity for 2026.

Sources: Harrison Street Research (2025), Statistics Canada, (November 2025) Organization for Economic Co-operation and Development (2024), Bonard Student Housing Market in Canada, (June 2025), Bonard (2025).

text:
We recently hosted Chicago Scholars at our Chicago headquarters to learn more about their impactful work supporting first generation and under resourced students as they navigate the path from high school to college and into their careers.

Through our five year partnership, HSAM has proudly hosted Chicago Scholars interns across our Impact, Brand Strategy, and People Operations teams, providing hands on experience, mentorship, and exposure to careers in real assets.

HSAM remains deeply committed to investing in the next generation of leaders by partnering with organizations like Chicago Scholars that expand access, foster connection, and create meaningful pathways to professional growth.

#HSAM #ChicagoScholars #DevelopingLeaders #Mentorship #CommunityImpact #RealAssetsCareers

Headquartered in Chicago, Toronto and London with over 600 employees in offices across North America, Europe, Asia and the Middle East, the firm offers innovative solutions across a variety of closed-end, open-end and specialized vehicles on behalf of 1,200+ institutional and over 300+ Registered Investment Advisors.1

*The information contained on this site is for informational purposes only and should not be considered as investment advice or as a recommendation of any particular strategy or investment product. This profile should not be considered as a solicitation for services.

**Unless otherwise indicated all information included on this page is as of September 30, 2025. Assets under management ("AUM") reflects AUM for the Firm’s investment advisory and asset management clients, and is inclusive of the Firm’s regulatory AUM reported in its Form ADV.

text:
Canada’s senior housing sector continues to gain momentum, fueled by robust demographic trends—most notably, the 80-plus age group, which is projected to grow at a 4.9% annually through 2034. In mid-2025, national occupancy reached 91.5% and is expected to surpass 92% as demand continues to outstrip new supply. This supply constraint is not expected to abate in the near-term, with construction starts accounting for less than 1% of total inventory and 34% of existing senior housing assets being over 25 years old. Harrison Street believes current market conditions set the stage for continued rent growth and resilient sector fundamentals.

Investor confidence is strong, highlighted by approximately C$6.5 billion in senior housing transactions announced in 2025 alone. These powerful long-term demographic drivers, coupled with tightening market conditions—high occupancy rates and limited new development—solidify senior housing as one of Harrison Street’s highest conviction ideas in Canada for 2026.

#HSAM #SeniorHousing #Canada #Alternatives

H.I.G. WhiteHorse provides financing to Nasta Pet Food, a French-based, family-owned international group specializing in ultra-premium pet nutrition.

“We are delighted to support Nasta with a financing solution tailored to its strategic ambitions. This transaction highlights H.I.G. WhiteHorse Europe’s ability to deliver flexible, scalable capital to help sponsor-less businesses execute transformative cross-border acquisitions,” Pascal Meysson, Head of H.I.G. WhiteHorse Europe.

#HIGCapital #PrivateEquity
authorHeadline:
H.I.G. Capital (“H.I.G.”) is a leading global private investment firm with $74 billion in equity capital under management.* Since its founding more than 30 years ago, H.I.G. and its family of funds, spanning private equity, real estate, growth equity, and credit, have invested in and managed over 400 companies worldwide. Today, the firm oversees a portfolio of companies generating more than $53 billion in combined annual sales.

H.I.G. specializes in providing capital to small and mid-sized companies with attractive growth potential. Its investments typically focus on management-led buyouts and recapitalizations of well-managed, profitable manufacturing and service businesses. The firm also has deep expertise in operational turnarounds and financial restructurings.

H.I.G.’s proven investment strategy is built around three pillars: (1) a flexible and responsive approach that emphasizes speed and adaptability, (2) a large, skilled team with a collaborative, “can-do” mindset, and (3) a consistent and long-standing focus on small and mid-sized businesses, typically valued between $50 million and $500 million, with a clear understanding of their unique opportunities and challenges.*

Headquartered in Miami, H.I.G. has U.S. offices in Atlanta, Boston, Chicago, Los Angeles, New York, San Francisco, and Stamford, as well as affiliate offices in Europe (Hamburg, London, Luxembourg, Madrid, Milan, Paris), Latin America (Bogotá, Rio de Janeiro, São Paulo), the Middle East (Dubai), and Asia (Hong Kong).

*Based on total capital raised by H.I.G. Capital and its affiliates.

text:
H.I.G. Realty Increases to Over £1bn its Prime Living Portfolio in Central London.

This acquisition marks another significant step in the expansion of H.I.G.’s London Living Platform, a highly curated portfolio of prime central London assets developed through a joint venture with HUB, a leading U.K. residential developer.

“This asset is a strong addition to our London Living Platform and showcases our focus on investing in strategic locations with excellent connectivity. The London residential market remains severely undersupplied and needs high-quality, institutional-grade units with modern, community-oriented amenities.” - Riccardo Dallolio, Managing Director and Head of H.I.G. Realty in Europe

Learn more: https://bit.ly/4aqOsPA

text:
H.I.G. Capital has completed the acquisition of CargoTuff, a leading provider of load securement solutions. CargoTuff specializes in dunnage bags, lashing, and strapping systems that keep cargo secure across rail, ocean, truck, and intermodal shipping.

“CargoTuff’s consumable and recyclable products provide critical protection for in-transit shipping within their customers’ supply chains. We look forward to supporting CargoTuff as it continues to invest in growth while maintaining its industry leading quality and service levels.” - Matt Kever, Managing Director at H.I.G.

Learn more: https://bit.ly/4tdSP9b

Headquartered in Miami, H.I.G. has U.S. offices in Atlanta, Boston, Chicago, Los Angeles, New York, San Francisco, and Stamford, as well as affiliate offices in Europe (Hamburg, London, Luxembourg, Madrid, Milan, Paris), Latin America (Bogotá, Rio de Janeiro, São Paulo), the Middle East (Dubai), and Asia (Hong Kong).

*Based on total capital raised by H.I.G. Capital and its affiliates.

text:
H.I.G. Bayside Capital Europe has completed the refinancing of Amerplast Group, a pan-European leader in sustainable flexible packaging solutions. Headquartered in Finland and the United Kingdom, Amerplast is a market-leading European manufacturer of flexible packaging serving the food & beverage, bakery, hygiene, retail, and industrial sectors.

“Amerplast is a high-quality, innovative, and resilient business with strong market positions in attractive end markets and a clear sustainability-led value proposition. The Company has demonstrated consistent EBITDA growth, supported by a disciplined commercial model and a highly engaged management team. Our capital solution refinances the existing debt structure and positions Amerplast for its next phase of expansion. We look forward to partnering with David, Mark, Matt, and the wider Amerplast and Chiltern Capital teams.” - Mathilde Malezieux, Managing Director at Bayside

Learn more: https://lnkd.in/eRcaKkzb

text:
Europe’s economic and geopolitical transformation is creating compelling opportunities in the lower mid-market, particularly in carve-outs and complex situations where operational execution is critical. Within this evolving landscape, H.I.G. recently closed more than €1.6bn for its latest European lower mid-market fund, reflecting strong investor conviction in the region and an operating-led approach.
 
Wolfgang Biedermann and Jordan Peer Griffin recently spoke with Maria Armental at The Wall Street Journal Pro Private Equity about European market dynamics and the strategy behind the fund.

Read the full article here: https://bit.ly/45hhu2y

text:
In the latest episode of CAIA Association’s Capital Decanted podcast, Gautam Bhandari, Co-Founder and CIO at I Squared Capital, joins hosts John L Bowman, CFA and Aaron Filbeck, CAIA, CFA, CFP®, CIPM, FDP to discuss the evolution of infrastructure and how the definition of the asset class continues to expand, operating at the intersection of geopolitics, policy and investor returns.

Gautam underscores a fundamental reality: every nation seeks to industrialize — and industrialization requires infrastructure. As public budgets face increasing constraints, private capital has an essential role to play in building and scaling critical infrastructure across energy, utilities and digital networks to meet growing global demand. 

Listen to the full episode here: https://lnkd.in/e3JYhFEV

text:
I Squared Capital is proud to join the #MadeForGermany initiative, supporting efforts to strengthen Germany’s competitiveness and long-term growth. 

As part of this cross-industry platform, backed by the German government, we are contributing to a collective investment commitment by German industry of more than €800 billion by 2028 across critical sectors including infrastructure, digitalization, industrial capacity, and R&D.

After decades of underinvestment, Germany is entering a renewed phase of infrastructure momentum, supported by the government’s €500 billion infrastructure fund and significant private capital. We see meaningful opportunities to partner with regional businesses, policymakers, and investors to help deliver the next generation of essential infrastructure and support sustainable economic growth across Europe.

I Squared has been active in the DACH region since 2019 and strengthened its local presence with the opening of an office in Munich in 2024. Yves Meyer-Bulow, who leads the Munich office, will represent I Squared within the initiative. We look forward to working alongside fellow participants to help future-proof Germany’s infrastructure.

text:
I Squared Capital is pleased to announce a new strategic partnership with CPP Investments | Investissements RPC in Inkia Energy, Peru’s leading private power generation company. Under the terms of the transaction, CPP will acquire a 50% ownership interest in Inkia, valuing the business at a total enterprise value of US$3.4 billion.
 
I Squared has been invested in Inkia since 2017, working closely with management to transform the company into a scaled, focused and strategically important generation platform supporting Peru’s evolving energy needs. Together with CPP, we see significant opportunity to continue investing in the platform, advancing its growth strategy and supporting Peru’s energy transition. 

We look forward to continuing our close collaboration with Inkia’s management team in this next chapter of growth.

 Read the full press release here: https://lnkd.in/gxVchv9c

text:
In the latest Infralogic 'Crossroads' podcast, I Squared Capital Chairman and Managing Partner Sadek Wahba joins host Jonathan Carmody to discuss how infrastructure investing is being reshaped by new capital sources, emerging technologies, and shifting public-private models. 
 
As the sector enters a global “Supercycle” driven by decarbonization, digitization, and supply-chain restructuring, Sadek highlights the growing democratization of infrastructure investing, with managers tapping the wealth channel to unlock mid-market opportunities. He also reflects on the evolving U.S. industrial policy, including the government’s more active role in strategic sectors, and what this means for cost of capital and private investment in national-priority infrastructure. 
 
Listen to the full conversation here: https://lnkd.in/e9t3hHib

text:
We recently spent time at Green; a Swiss-based digital infrastructure platform operating hyperscale and enterprise data centres - to gain a deeper, first‑hand view of how the infrastructure operates and the role it plays within the local ecosystem.
 
We’re pleased to be invested in high‑quality Swiss data centre infrastructure, supported by our local presence in Zurich and long‑standing relationships in the Swiss market.
 
Assets like this play an important role in supporting Switzerland’s digital economy. Engaging directly with infrastructure assets helps build understanding of how they operate on the ground and what makes them distinctive within their market.

authorHeadline:
IFM Investors is a global asset manager, founded and owned by pension funds, with capabilities in infrastructure equity and debt, private equity, private credit, real estate and listed equities. 

Our purpose is to invest, protect and grow the long-term retirement savings of working people. We believe healthy returns depend on healthy economic, environmental and social systems - and these are evolving on a scale never experienced before. 

To find opportunity, build value and meet the needs of future generations, you need scale, skill and expertise. That’s what IFM Investors has built up over 30 years.
 
With assets under management of approximately $243.3 AUD billion (as at 30 September 2025), we prioritise the interests of over 812 institutional investors worldwide. 

IFM operates from 16 offices across Australia, Europe, North America and Asia. 

For more information, visit ifminvestors.com.

text:
Tightening across public and private credit markets has set the tone for a more selective environment this year. We believe investors are increasingly prioritising disciplined deployment and alignment with managers who can identify stronger relative value.

At the same time, improving profitability through 2025 means many borrowers are now more willing to invest and expand, supporting healthier deal flow.

And with Australia’s economic outlook remaining positive, we expect to see growing international interest in the broader Asia Pacific region, where investors may find more attractive compensation for risk.

Visit https://ow.ly/QKU150YmlVi to learn more.

text:
At this year's Pensions UK Investment Conference, IFM Investors is pleased to be supporting one of the sector’s most important annual gatherings - a valuable opportunity to meet with clients and partners across the UK pensions landscape and to engage on the issues shaping long‑term investment. 

As part of the programme, Gregg McClymont, Executive Director, Public Affairs, Europe, will chair a headline plenary session featuring Caroline Lucas, former Leader of the Green Party, and Michael Gove, former Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs.

The discussion will bring two contrasting viewpoints together to explore how environmental investment strategies are evolving, the role of stewardship, and what these shifts mean for schemes navigating long‑term climate and policy transitions.

We look forward to connecting with clients, partners and industry colleagues throughout the conference.

For more information, visit: https://lnkd.in/e-e8tTnp

text:
We’re excited to announce that Igneo Infrastructure Partners has entered into an agreement to acquire Strait Link, a leading Australian end‑to‑end logistics business and the largest integrated freight provider across the Bass Strait.
 
With strategic port terminals in Burnie and Melbourne, logistics depots across Tasmania, and a c.45% share of Bass Strait trade, Strait Link is a critical piece of Tasmania’s supply chain infrastructure and an exceptional addition to our growing regional portfolio.
 
As Danny Latham, Partner and Head of Igneo Australia & New Zealand, shared:
“Strait Link is a quality addition to our regional portfolio and aligns perfectly with our strategy to invest in core‑plus mid‑market assets. We look forward to partnering with the Strait Link team to deliver reliable, efficient services and long‑term value for our investors.”
 
The transaction is expected to settle in the first half of 2026 (subject to regulatory approvals) and we couldn’t be more excited for what’s ahead.

Press release here: https://lnkd.in/g5M_fMWA 

*For professional/institutional investors only.

text:
AI is advancing at record speed and behind the breakthroughs lies the infrastructure that makes it possible. 

At a Rotman-hosted event in Toronto, our Chief Investment Officer, Rossitsa Stoyanova, joined former Finance Minister Bill Morneau, Senior Advisor at OpenAI, Dev Saxena, and Managing Director, Infrastructure, Brookfield, Lief Williams, CFA, to discuss how strategic investments can help build the digital backbone required to strengthen Canada’s productivity and competitiveness in the age of AI. 

Canada’s Minister of AI and Digital Innovation, Evan Solomon, was also in attendance, reflecting the broader public engagement around this technology and its evolving role in economic growth.

authorHeadline: The Investment Management Corporation of Ontario (IMCO) manages $86 billion of assets on behalf of its clients. IMCO’s mandate is to provide broader public sector institutions with investment management services, including portfolio construction advice, better access to a diverse range of asset classes and sophisticated risk management capabilities. IMCO is an independent organization, operating at arm’s length from government and guided by a highly experienced and professional Board of Directors.

text:
Today is Pension Awareness Day - a timely reminder of what pensions are really about: people's futures.

As an investment manager for pension plans, the IMCO team thinks about retirement security every day. Pension investing is built on a long-term horizon that requires a disciplined approach, thoughtful risk management and maintaining resilience through market cycles. The outcome matters: helping our clients' beneficiaries retire with greater confidence.

Learn more from the Financial Services Regulatory Authority of Ontario (FSRA): https://lnkd.in/d773fzmN

text:
IMCO’s 2026 Student Internship Program opportunities are now live!

We’re hiring across a range of corporate and back-office teams that support IMCO’s work as a long-term institutional investor.

Undergraduate and master’s students, as well as recent graduates who are looking to gain hands-on experience and build practical skills in a professional, purpose-driven environment, are encouraged to apply. 

Opportunities are available across Client Relationship Management, Communications, Compliance, Enterprise Risk, Finance, IT, Investment Operations and Legal. 

Our four-month (May-August 2026) paid internship program offers:

▪️ Meaningful, project-based work alongside experienced professionals
▪️ Opportunities for learning, development, and career exploration
▪️ Exposure to IMCO’s collaborative and purpose-driven culture

Explore the opportunities, apply today or share with someone who’s ready for a rewarding summer internship experience. Postings close January 20, 2026. https://lnkd.in/g-qD2B_Q 

 #IMCOInternship #ProfessionalDevelopment

authorHeadline: InfraBridge is a leading infrastructure investment manager dedicated to investing in mid-market businesses in transportation and logistics, digital infrastructure and energy transition, among other key sectors. The firm’s investment strategy applies private equity rigor with an active approach to asset management which seeks to maximize value and drive returns on behalf of investors. Formerly AMP Capital’s international infrastructure equity business, InfraBridge is now a division of DigitalBridge Group, Inc. (NYSE: DBRG), a leading global digital infrastructure firm managing a portfolio of digital infrastructure assets on behalf of its investors.  InfraBridge is headquartered in London, United Kingdom.

text:
InfraBridge today announced a new agreement under which Aena will acquire a stake in our interests in Leeds Bradford Airport (LBA) and Newcastle International Airport (NIAL).
 
This transaction reflects the strong performance, the operational and commercial improvements achieved under InfraBridge’s stewardship, and the long-term growth prospects of both airports. It also deepens our partnership with Aena—a relationship that began with our joint ownership of London Luton Airport and continues to grow across UK airports.
 
We look forward to building on this partnership with Aena and our continued management of LBA and NIAL.

text:
Geopolitics has moved from background risk to a central consideration in infrastructure investment decisions, now often outweighing traditional macro concerns.
 
In our latest paper, we examine how the changing global order is reshaping the risk‑return landscape for infrastructure investors. We also highlight insights from our GeoAlpha framework, which hard‑wires geopolitical dimensions into country risk analysis, to show how an integrated approach can support more informed country selection, portfolio construction and opportunity identification.
 
Below is an extract of the paper. To read the full version, please click here: https://lnkd.in/epdcF26J

#geopolitics #infrastructure #investment #geopoliticalrisk
authorHeadline:
InfraRed is a leading international mid-market infrastructure asset manager. Over the past 25 years, InfraRed has established itself as a highly successful developer, particularly in early-stage projects, and an active steward of essential infrastructure.

InfraRed manages US$13bn of equity capital [1] for investors around the globe in listed and private funds across both core and value-add strategies.

InfraRed combines a global reach, operating worldwide from offices in London, Madrid, New York, Sydney and Seoul, with deep sector expertise from a team of more than 160 people.

InfraRed is part of SLC Management, the institutional alternatives and traditional asset management business of Sun Life, and benefits from its scale and global platform.

For more information, please visit www.ircp.com.

[1] Uses 5-year average FX as at 31st December 2024 of GBP/USD of 1.2818; EUR/USD 1.1092. EUM is USD 13.186m.

text:
Over many years, we have recognised and celebrated innovative and impactful initiatives from across our portfolio that deliver tangible benefits, helping to share best practice.
 
As we open the submission process for the 2026 Creating Better Futures Awards, we’ll be showcasing a selection of initiatives from the 2025 edition over the coming months.
 
The first initiative comes from Queen Alexandra Hospital in the UK, which introduced virtual reality (VR) headsets to support children undergoing medical procedures. This approach not only improves the experience for children and their parents, but also reduces the need for sedatives and anti-anxiety medication – generating cost savings for the NHS.
 
Find out more about the Creating Better Futures Awards: https://lnkd.in/e-8BwywC
 
#virtualreality #healthcare #CreatingBetterFutures

text:
As the festive season draws to a close, we were delighted to have helped spread a bit of joy during the Christmas period at one of our UK assets, the Royal Alexandra Children's Hospital in Brighton.

Thanks to the tremendous generosity of everyone involved and the funds raised at an InfraRed Capital Partners Christmas event, much-loved characters – including Father Christmas, Spiderman, and the witches from Wicked – visited children receiving treatment or awaiting surgery. They handed out presents and spread festive cheer, brightening the day for both the children and their families.

A heartfelt thank you to all who contributed and to our public sector partners University Hospitals Sussex NHS Foundation Trust for organising and helping make this special occasion possible.

text:
We are pleased to announce a majority investment in NXN Datacenters (“NxN”), a next-generation data centre platform in Spain formed in 2023.
 
The platform was designed to serve the growing storage and computing demand in Spain, with a particular focus on regional enterprise customers. 

Construction of its first facility, located in Valencia, has commenced and is scheduled to open in 2027. Once operational, the data centre will deliver high-quality, AI-ready, energy-efficient colocation solutions for public and private clients across the region.
 
Pilar Banegas Muñoz, Partner at InfraRed, commented: “NxN represents an exceptional opportunity to establish a data centre platform within Spain’s fast-growing digital landscape. We will draw on our experience developing nexspace, our DACH data centre business, as we support the impressive NxN leadership in building this investment.”
 
For more information, please click here: https://lnkd.in/edXbdRYm 
 
#datacentres #infrastructure #investment

authorHeadline:
Founded in 2008, InfraVia is a leading independent private equity firm specializing in real assets - infrastructure, critical metals, real estate - and technology investments.
InfraVia is a conviction-driven investor focusing on resilient businesses partnering with management teams, entrepreneurs, or industrials to develop their businesses and drive long-term value creation through active, hands-on asset management.
InfraVia manages EUR 20 billion of capital* and invested in 60+ companies across Europe.

text:
📢 Portfolio Update | Infrastructure | nexfibre

InfraVia, Liberty Global and Telefónica have announced an agreement to acquire Netomnia, the UK’s second-largest alternative fibre provider. The acquisition will be completed through their joint venture nexfibre, and will unlock £3.5 billion of investment in the UK market. 

This transaction will: 
✅ Create a scaled and financially robust alternative wholesale fibre platform
✅ Strengthen the UK’s digital infrastructure, delivering greater choice and quality for consumers and businesses
It reflects strong, long-term confidence in the UK as an attractive investment market.

#FiberNetwork #InfraVia 
Bruno Candès, Zeynep Lucchini Gilera, Pierre-Adrien Achard, Charles Brière, Hannah Z., Arnaud Delarue, Cynthia Delaume

🔗Read the full press release here : https://lnkd.in/eGdXU-mG

InfraVia is a conviction-driven investor focusing on resilient businesses partnering with management teams, entrepreneurs, or industrials to develop their businesses and drive long-term value creation through active, hands-on asset management.
InfraVia manages EUR 20 billion of capital* and invested in 60+ companies across Europe.

text:
📢 Portfolio Update | Infrastructure | Heygaz Biomethane, S.L.

Heygaz, the European biomethane platform, has successfully secured a platform financing with a first tranche enabling the development of 15 projects. 

➡️ It attracted significant interest from a diversified group of lenders, including infrastructure institutional debt investors and commercial banks.
➡️ This financing marks a major milestone and paves the way towards Heygaz’s target of 1.5 TWh of biomethane capacity by 2030.

InfraVia set up in Heygaz in 2023 to capture the growing demand from various industries and transportation sectors in Europe for carbon-neutral gas, positioning it as a critical tool for decarbonization. 🌍🌳 

Since then, Heygaz has scaled rapidly, with a footprint expanding to five countries, operating nine plants (with four more under construction), and accelerating both greenfield development and acquisitions for conversion to biomethane.

#EnergyTransition, #biomethane, #InfraVia, #Sustainability, #Heygaz,
#biomethane, #renewablegas, #EUdecarbonization
Athanasios (Thanasis) Zoulovits, Nicolas Ritter, Pauline Fiastre, Augustin Schneider-Maunoury, Alice Ardiet

🔗Read the full press release here : https://lnkd.in/e5w_6mtM

text:
📢 Portfolio Update | Infrastructure | Prosolia Energy Group

Prosolia, the leading Independent Power Producer (IPP) specialized in providing decarbonization solutions to large industrial companies, secures €380 million in debt financing to fund both the acquisition of an operational portfolio of solar projects and the construction of its global project pipeline, including photovoltaic, wind, and battery storage assets. ☀️⚡

➡️ The financing package includes three significant transactions spanning across seven jurisdictions 
➡️ It attracted significant interest from top-tier lenders, reflecting strong confidence in Prosolia’s capabilities in delivering its ambitious roadmap as an international IPP.

#EnergyTransition #SolarPower #InfraVia #Sustainability #Prosolia
Gabriel Gauthier, Reda Benkarim, Miguel Escalada Lopez-Ibor, Sophie REY-LECOCQ, Sebastien Kammerman, Dingyi YU

text:
📢 This week, InfraVia hosted its ESG Forum, bringing together a community of leaders from across our infrastructure and growth portfolios.

🔹 Over 70 participants including ca. 30 portfolio companies joined the event 
🔹 The day was filled with a dynamic mix of roundtables, plenary sessions, and hands-on workshops 
 
🙏 Special thanks to:

Christopher Guerin ◉, Former CEO of Nexans, with an inspiring talk on his responsible management model focused on Economy, Environment and Engagement, combining long-term vision with simplification at scale.

All our portfolio company speakers, including Liam Dunne ( COO, Mater Private) and Eric Mieras (CIO, One Click), who shared their approaches to decarbonization; and isabelle HERAULT (Deputy CEO Univet) and Laurette Kristina Charlier (Senior VP People, Datadome), who discussed talent retention strategies.

💡 We all left the event with new perspectives, new connections, and concrete, practical takeaways.

👏 A big thank you to everyone who contributed to the day!

Laëtitia Feraud, Isoline Degert, Pauline Pigott, Hien Nguyen

text:
On January 28, Bruno Candès will join the opening panel of TMT Finance’s Annual Conference, taking part in the Digital Infra Leaders discussion on "How consolidation and capital rotation are reshaping the sector’s value equation". 
 
The discussion follows a particularly active year for InfraVia in digital infrastructure, marked by the exit from Green, the leading data centre platform in Switzerland, and the acquisition of Opcore, the hyperscale data centre platform developed in partnership with iliad.
 
We are also honoured to be shortlisted by TMT Finance for:
 🏆 Investment Firm of the Year
 🏆 Investment Leader of the Year
 🏆 Data Centre Deal of the Year
 
Thank you to our teams, partners, and the TMT Finance community. We look forward to the discussion!

#DigitalInfrastructure #TMTFinance #DataCentres #InfraVia 

More info : https://invt.io/1lxbs73vdo

text:
📢 Portfolio Update | Infrastructure | Prosolia Energy Group

Prosolia, the Independent Power Producer (IPP) specialized in providing decarbonization solutions to large industrial companies, has completed the acquisition of a 229 MW portfolio of operational solar assets in Spain. ☀️ 

➡️ This transaction adds 229 MW across 5 parks located in Andalucía and Castilla y León, supported by long-term PPAs with leading industrial clients.

➡️ Following this transaction, Prosolia expects to reach close to 1 GW of operational assets by year-end.

➡️ Since the acquisition of Prosolia in October 2024, InfraVia has been actively supporting the group’s development, accelerating its international growth through projects that combine different renewable technologies and energy storage systems (BESS). 

#EnergyTransition #SolarPower #InfraVia #Sustainability #Prosolia
Gabriel Gauthier, Reda Benkarim, Miguel Escalada Lopez-Ibor, Sophie REY-LECOCQ, Sebastien Kammerman, Dingyi YU

text:
Markets have continued to fixate on AI-related headlines since our last newsletter in December. While there’s plenty of noise about “demand risk”, we remain confident in CDC’s outlook - and this edition steps back to focus on some of the underlying global demand signals for data centre capacity we’re seeing.

Other highlights from the latest Infratil newsletter include:
- CDC is expanding operating capacity to meet customer demand
- Longroad Energy is progressing development and construction across its renewables pipeline
- Contact Energy Ltd is advancing investment across battery, solar and geothermal
- Infratil received a BBB+ credit rating (stable outlook) from S&P Global Ratings
- Wellington Airport is paving the way to more destinations and improved connectivity
- Anytime Radiology has been established as a standalone teleradiology provider, combining RHCNZ Medical Imaging and Qscan Group teams and assets

The full newsletter is available below.
authorHeadline: Launched in 1994, Infratil Limited is a New Zealand headquartered, global infrastructure investment company (NZX: IFT, ASX: IFT). Our purpose is to invest wisely in ideas that matter and, in doing so, create long-term value for our shareholders. We invest in renewables, digital infrastructure, healthcare and airports, with operations in New Zealand, Australia, Europe, Asia and the United States. With group assets currently in excess of NZ$10 billion, we target returns to shareholders of 11-15% p.a. over the long-term.

Well done to everyone involved. Read the full update from One New Zealand below.

text:
There’s something pretty special about hearing the impact of support in people’s own words. In this video, members of the New Zealand Youth Choir share what it’s meant to be part of NZYC - and what Infratil’s backing has enabled along the way.

The NZYC has been showcasing New Zealand’s choral talent since 1979: around 50 singers aged 18–25, are selected on a three-year audition cycle, performing at home and on stages around the world.

Infratil has proudly been the NZYC’s Principal Sponsor since 2012, and it was a real thrill to see the choir crowned Choir of the World at the 2025 Llangollen International Musical Eisteddfod in Wales.

To everyone in the NZYC - thank you for the talent, commitment, and heart you bring.

#IdeasThatMatter
#Community

text:
Summer in New Zealand is when we lean hardest on our networks - beach days, road trips, catching up with whānau, and sharing the moments as you go. It’s great to see One New Zealand heading into the holiday break even stronger, with 258 mobile upgrades completed in 2025 and extra support in place over the peak period, including mobile cellsites on wheels and teams rostered 24/7 to monitor performance.

And if you’re straying off the beaten track this summer, or simply visiting a beach or campsite without traditional mobile coverage, One NZ is currently the only provider to offer satellite mobile coverage.

#IdeasThatMatter
#Connectivity

text:
Earlier this week, Auckland Radiology Group officially opened its new flagship clinic at 99 Remuera Road, welcoming more than 90 healthcare professionals and stakeholders for the launch.

The new facility showcases leading diagnostic capability, including Auckland’s most sensitive PET-CT scanner and the Southern Hemisphere’s most advanced analogue SPECT-CT scanner, helping accelerate access to expert imaging and support better patient outcomes.

Congratulations to the Auckland Radiology and RHCNZ Medical Imaging teams on this important milestone.

#IdeasThatMatter
#Healthcare

text:
We are proud to see Longroad Energy demonstrating how large-scale solar can actively support local ecosystems, not just supply the grid. At a recent Solar Wildlife & Ecosystem Research Meeting in Arizona, Longroad shared research from one of its solar sites, including a successful western burrowing owl translocation and evidence that the microclimates created by solar panels can accelerate biocrust growth in desert soils.

These projects highlight the depth of Longroad’s environmental focus and the value of partnering with local conservation groups and research institutions to deliver better outcomes for nature.

#IdeasThatMatter
#RenewableEnergy
#Sustainability

text:
Kao Data is making strong progress on KLON-03, its new 17.6MW data centre on the Harlow campus, which is on track for completion at the end of 2026. 

Purpose-built for GPU-accelerated AI and advanced computing, KLON-03 will offer hybrid cooling for both liquid- and air-cooled systems, rack densities of up to 130kW, and be powered by 100% renewable energy.

As one of the UK’s largest allocations of liquid-cooled AI infrastructure, KLON-03 further strengthens Harlow’s role as a key hub for AI and high-performance computing.

#IdeasThatMatter
#DigitalInfrastructure

It covers CDC’s demand outlook (including neoclouds and AI), Longroad Energy’s accelerating earnings, our progress on portfolio refinements and funding capacity, the performance of our New Zealand businesses, and an update on Gurīn Energy and Australia’s new National AI Plan.

With group assets currently in excess of NZ$10 billion, we target returns to shareholders of 11-15% p.a. over the long-term.

text:
Kao Data has announced a new partnership with Downing Renewable Developments, who will develop, build and operate a 40MW solar PV farm to supply Kao Data’s Harlow data centre campus with solar-generated electricity. 

The Solar Farm is anticipated to be one of the first ground-mounted solar PV systems supplying a UK colocation data centre campus, supporting Kao Data’s target to become fully net zero by 2030 and helping address key challenges around energy availability, power pricing and grid connection delays highlighted in the announcement.

#IdeasThatMatter
#DataCentres
#Renewables

authorHeadline:
At J.P. Morgan Asset Management, we believe investors deserve an expert global partner they can trust to step up and deliver strong outcomes. From the largest institutional investors around the world to financial advisors around the corner, our clients rely on the power of perspective to help build stronger portfolios and solve their toughest challenges. 

Our perspective draws on knowledge earned over a century of investing through every market cycle. Today, with market intelligence trusted the world over, we offer deep, actionable insights. From our vantage point, we strive to see correlations others miss and seize opportunities that our global, connected view can deliver. Through our fully ESG-integrated investment platform, enhanced investment stewardship, and range of ESG-focused strategies, we invest for sustainable return in a sustainable world. 

Our clients trust us to manage $2.5 trillion in assets to secure what matters most to the individuals, families, companies, and communities they serve. To make the most of retirements. To help protect endowments. To strengthen returns. To enjoy hard-earned success.

To any challenge our clients face, we say, Let’s Solve It. 

This content is exclusively for institutional/wholesale/professional clients and qualified investors only as defined by local laws and regulations.

text: Recently, President Trump announced tariffs on the U.S.’s major trading partners. The magnitude of the tariffs exceeded market expectations, triggering sharp losses across global equity markets. Today, April 7th, after market close, Dr. David Kelly, Chief Global Strategist, and Gabriela Santos, Chief Market Strategist for the Americas, will discuss the recent tariff announcement and its implications for markets and the economy. Register now for the discussion: http://spr.ly/6043FWSJD

text:
At KKR, we focus on the sustainability issues most material to a company's business model, stakeholders, and operating context. When integrated into core strategy, sustainability performance can mitigate risk, improve efficiency, and position companies for long-term growth. 

At the 2025 Private Equity Sustainability Practicum at NYU Stern Center for Sustainable Business, leaders from three of our portfolio companies shared how they are operationalizing sustainability to build resilience and long-term value:

🌍 ContourGlobal's Global Head of Sustainability, Linda Wrong, discussed why decarbonization has become a core business priority. 

🔄 Charter Next Generation's SVP of Technical Sustainability & Innovation, John Garnett, outlined how circularity is reshaping competitiveness in packaging. 

👥 Potter Global Technologies' Chief Human Resources Officer, Tom Hodge, emphasized that investing in employees and driving strong business results go hand-in-hand. 

Learn more: https://go.kkr.com/4sb6wEr
authorHeadline:
KKR is a leading global investment firm that offers alternative asset management as well as capital markets and insurance solutions. KKR aims to generate attractive investment returns by following a patient and disciplined investment approach, employing world-class people, and supporting growth in its portfolio companies and communities. 

KKR sponsors investment funds that invest in private equity, credit and real assets and has strategic partners that manage hedge funds. KKR’s insurance subsidiaries offer retirement, life and reinsurance products under the management of Global Atlantic Financial Group. References to KKR’s investments may include the activities of its sponsored funds and insurance subsidiaries.

text:
We're almost 2 months into 2026, and our view for this year remains constructive, even amid a complex market environment with persistently higher inflation and interest rates, elevated valuations, tighter spreads, and positive stock-bond correlations around the world.
 
This moment calls for high-grading portfolios for the long-term: staying invested, while prioritizing quality, diversification across assets and regions, and capital efficiency.
 
For data-backed insights on constructing portfolios that compound over time, check out our Q1 Global Wealth Investment Playbook: https://go.kkr.com/4qPp1gH

At KKR, employee ownership is a cornerstone of our approach to value creation. We believe when people think like owners, they build like owners. 

We’re proud to have announced the sale of Novaria last November. With this partnership, we supported an employee ownership program which gave all 1,600 employees a stake in Novaria’s success. With the completion of the sale, all employee-owners received cash payouts in recognition of their contributions and impact. 

Watch the full video and learn more about our approach to employee ownership: https://go.kkr.com/4c342U2

text:
Keep an eye on India.

After a recent trip to various cities across India, Henry McVey and the Global Macro team see a country rife with long-term opportunity across our Private Equity, Infrastructure, Climate, and Credit franchises, given our deep local expertise and favorable macro forces.

text:
India is evolving, and we are evolving right alongside it.
 
In a recent interview with The Economic Times, Henry Kravis reflected on more than two decades of investing in the country. His perspective was clear: the quality, ambition, and execution capability of Indian entrepreneurs today stand alongside the best globally.
 
What began as a private equity platform has expanded into infrastructure and private credit, where we provide companies with flexible, long-term capital solutions aligned with their needs. Over the past 20 years, we’ve invested more than $13 billion in India, including approximately $9 billion in the last five years alone. As India’s economy expands and its capital markets deepen, we expect our pace of deployment to increase meaningfully over the next decade.
 
Across strategies, our focus remains consistent: partner with strong businesses, think across the capital structure, and provide capital that supports durable growth.
 
Read the full interview in The Economic Times: https://go.kkr.com/46VcuBb

KKR’s insurance subsidiaries offer retirement, life and reinsurance products under the management of Global Atlantic Financial Group. References to KKR’s investments may include the activities of its sponsored funds and insurance subsidiaries. 

For additional information about KKR (NYSE: KKR), please visit www.kkr.com. For additional information about Global Atlantic Financial Group, please visit www.globalatlantic.com.

KKR will never request personal information, account details, payments and transfers over digital chat applications, social media, email or through SMS: https://www.kkr.com/security-and-fraud-awareness

text:
In a recent interview with The Deal by With Intelligence‘s ‘Behind the Buyouts’ podcast, Raghu Velamati, Senior Managing Director in Macquarie Capital’s Software & Services coverage team, discusses the evolving edtech landscape and the state of the education M&A market today. 

Raghu explores key trends across the sector – from the expanding role of AI in supporting personalised learning outcomes to growing investor appetite in core sectors like workforce training and re-skilling, for-profit universities and healthcare education. 

Listen to the episode and read our latest outlook here: https://macq.co/6041htVx9

text:
Core to the Group since its origins in 1969, Macquarie Capital is a global adviser and investor and a market leader in ANZ. Its unique set of capabilities allow it to advise clients on transformative opportunities, invest its own capital, finance and develop projects, and support the growth of businesses in 22 markets around the world. 

Macquarie Capital uses deep sector expertise to help listed companies like Lynas Rare Earths Ltd raise capital, expand their business and capture new investment opportunities. And its Venture Capital team invests in early-stage companies like Forwood Safety, partnering with founders to help build global businesses that make a real-world impact. 

Learn more about what we shared at our 2026 Operational Briefing: macq.co/OB26

text:
Built on the strong foundations of Munich Re and ERGO, our size, expertise, and reliability make us an ideal partner also for private and institutional investors.
 
MEAG’s total assets under management amount to approximately €368 billion. This includes €63 billion from private and external institutional investors who trust in our extensive market know-how.
 
Our portfolio reflects broad diversification across liquid and illiquid asset classes and underlines a resilient, long-term investment approach with a clear focus on our stable foundation of fixed income and participations.
 
#MEAG #AssetManagement #Investment
authorHeadline:
Decades of experience, over 130 investment managers and assets under management of over EUR 300 billion make us one of the most significant institutional players in the world of finance. Headquartered in Munich, and with our global offices in New York and Hong Kong, we offer institutional as well as private investors access to our investment expertise in all major asset classes and solutions geared to growth trends. Our versatile know-how in the field of public markets (including equities, bonds, currencies) and alternative assets (real estate, infrastructure, renewable energies and forestry & agriculture) together with our dedicated ESG focus complement each other in tailor-made solutions for our clients. The transfer of competence and knowledge with Munich Re supports us in seizing opportunities and identify risks prematurely. The incessantly dialogue between the interdisciplinary teams leads to holistic assessments of market movements and trends, which form the basis of our long-term performance success. Our core principle and mission is to focus on our clients' interests and objectives in order to achieve their financial goals together.

text:
We look forward to the Infrastructure Investor Global Summit 2026!
 
Taking place at Station Berlin, the summit will bring together the global infrastructure investment community from 24–27 March.
MEAG will contribute to the programme on 26 March with three panel discussions:
 
1️⃣ Forum 1 | 2:30 – 3:10 pm
Assessing dealflow in the private infrastructure market
Işıl Tanrıverdi Versmissen, Head of Infrastructure Debt Transactions
 
2️⃣Forum 2 | 2:30 – 3:10 pm
The role of renewables in sustainable infrastructure
Severin Buchwald, Head of Alternatives ESG Integration
 
3️⃣Forum 2 | 3:10 – 3:50 pm
Unlocking value through sustainability
Sandra Rother, Head of Infrastructure Debt Asset Management
 
We look forward to the discussions and to meeting you on site.
 
#MEAG #AssetManagement

text:
MEAG and Culmia establish affordable housing platform in Spain
 
MEAG has entered into a strategic partnership with Culmia to create Spain’s largest operational affordable housing platform.
The platform will launch with 1,782 homes and is expected to grow beyond 2,500 units, representing an investment volume of approximately €400 million.
The portfolio is structured through long-term concessions and surface rights with CPI-linked rents, providing a stable regulatory framework.
 
With this transaction, MEAG positions itself as a first mover in Spain’s affordable housing sector using infrastructure capital.
 
👉 More information: https://lnkd.in/eE7-narN 
 
#MEAG #AssetManagement #InfrastructureCapital

text:
MEAG has started the heavy refurbishment of 4, Rue de Penthièvre in #Paris’ 8th Arrondissement, transforming the 1970s building into a modern, sustainable seven-story office space.

The project places a strong emphasis on #sustainability and flexibility, featuring a new building envelope with highly energy-efficient façade systems and state-of-the-art technical equipment. The existing concrete structure will be preserved to reduce embodied carbon, with the aim of achieving top certifications such as BREEAM and HQE “Excellent.”
Highlights include a green roof terrace with views of the Eiffel Tower, an enhanced entrance area, redesigned outdoor spaces, and end-of-trip facilities supporting eco-friendly mobility with bike parking and showers.

“Our ambition is to combine sustainability with long-term value creation,” says Project Director Gianna Mechnig. “By preserving the existing structure and integrating high-performance systems, we are significantly reducing the building’s environmental footprint while preparing it for the future.”

Project Director Cedric Cyrill Nieser adds: “We are creating flexible, future-proof office space that meets the evolving needs of modern occupiers – in terms of sustainability, functionality and user experience.”

#MEAG #AssetManagement #OfficeRefurbishment #FutureWorkspaces

Copyright: Baumschlager Eberle Architekten

Our versatile know-how in the field of public markets (including equities, bonds, currencies) and alternative assets (real estate, infrastructure, renewable energies and forestry & agriculture) together with our dedicated ESG focus complement each other in tailor-made solutions for our clients. The transfer of competence and knowledge with Munich Re supports us in seizing opportunities and identify risks prematurely. The incessantly dialogue between the interdisciplinary teams leads to holistic assessments of market movements and trends, which form the basis of our long-term performance success. Our core principle and mission is to focus on our clients' interests and objectives in order to achieve their financial goals together.

text:
MEAG at the EUREX Derivatives Forum 2026 in Frankfurt!
 
From 24 to 26 February, the EUREX Derivatives Forum will bring together market participants to discuss current developments in trading, clearing and market infrastructure.
On 25 February, MEAG will be part of two panels:
 
🔹 3:05 – 3:50 pm | Beyond 2026: the European Clearing Landscape
Mark Erler, Senior Trader Fixed Income at MEAG, will discuss structural changes in European clearing markets, including developments beyond EMIR 3.0, liquidity and execution strategies.
 
🔹 4:25 – 5:10 pm | Technology, Automation and the Future of Buy-Side Derivatives Trading
Elke Wenzler, Head of Trading at MEAG, will speak about how automation, data and connectivity are reshaping trading and where human judgment remains essential.
 
We look forward to the discussions and to meeting you in Frankfurt.

#WSED #Sustainability #EnergyEfficiency #RenewableEnergy
authorHeadline: Meridiam was founded in 2005 by Thierry Déau, with the belief that the alignment of interests between the public and private sector can provide critical solutions to the collective needs of communities. Meridiam is an independent investment Benefit Corporation and an asset manager. The firm specializes in the development, financing, and long-term management of sustainable public infrastructure in three core sectors - sustainable mobility, critical public services and innovative low carbon solutions - and invests in sustainable SMEs in Europe. With offices in Addis Ababa, Amman, Dakar, Istanbul, Johannesburg, Libreville, Luxembourg, Paris, Vienna and Washington, Meridiam manages over EUR23 billion and more than 130 projects to date. Meridiam is certified ISO 9001: 2015, ISO 37001 Anti-Corruption certification by AFNOR and applies a unique methodology in relation to ESG and impact based on United Nations’ Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

text:
Listen to #Meridiam CEO Americas Nicolas Rubio join Jonathan Davies on P3 Bulletin’s “3 P’s in a Pod” #podcast to discuss the growing opportunities in the #US #P3 market and some of Meridiam’s landmark US projects. 
 
Nicolas highlights how the value of P3s extends well beyond financing to drive efficiency and innovation in project delivery. Drawing on lessons from Meridiam’s SR 400 and Long Beach Courthouse projects, he underscores the importance of strong partnerships, bipartisan consensus, and physical and social resilience to deliver infrastructure that stands the test of time.
 
Listen to the full episode here: https://lnkd.in/ekjUeZwx
 
#forpeopleandtheplanet #publicprivatepartnerships #sustainableinfrastructure

text:
#Meridiam closed its 𝐌𝐞𝐫𝐢𝐝𝐢𝐚𝐦 𝐈𝐧𝐟𝐫𝐚𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐮𝐜𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞 𝐄𝐮𝐫𝐨𝐩𝐞 𝐂𝐨𝐫𝐞 𝐅𝐮𝐧𝐝, a €2.2 billion vehicle designed to support the #longterm ownership and ongoing management of its #landmark #European #infrastructure assets. 
This closing marks a significant strategic milestone, strengthening the firm's ability to steward essential, high performing assets over extended horizons - often reaching up to 50 years.

The fund attracted strong demand from global institutional investors, with nearly 70% of commitments coming from new LPs. The remaining are existing LPs who chose to roll over and reaffirm their confidence in Meridiam’s long term strategy for an additional 20 years.

In parallel, Meridiam (as financial delegate) and Private Corner have launched 𝐌𝐞𝐫𝐢𝐝𝐢𝐚𝐦 𝐆𝐥𝐨𝐛𝐚𝐥 𝐈𝐧𝐟𝐫𝐚𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐮𝐜𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞 𝐒𝐭𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐞𝐠𝐢𝐞𝐬, Meridiam’s first fully #retail dedicated #evergreen fund of funds. This new product provides individual investors with access to a diversified, operational, and yield-generating infrastructure portfolio seeded with cornerstone assets from the 𝐌𝐞𝐫𝐢𝐝𝐢𝐚𝐦 𝐈𝐧𝐟𝐫𝐚𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐮𝐜𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞 𝐄𝐮𝐫𝐨𝐩𝐞 𝐂𝐨𝐫𝐞 𝐅𝐮𝐧𝐝.

Learn more from the press release: https://lnkd.in/eaU9jRSQ

The firm specializes in the development, financing, and long-term management of sustainable public infrastructure in three core sectors - sustainable mobility, critical public services and innovative low carbon solutions - and invests in sustainable SMEs in Europe. With offices in Addis Ababa, Amman, Dakar, Istanbul, Johannesburg, Libreville, Luxembourg, Paris, Vienna and Washington, Meridiam manages over EUR23 billion and more than 130 projects to date. Meridiam is certified ISO 9001: 2015, ISO 37001 Anti-Corruption certification by AFNOR and applies a unique methodology in relation to ESG and impact based on United Nations’ Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

text:
#Meridiam closed its 𝐌𝐞𝐫𝐢𝐝𝐢𝐚𝐦 𝐈𝐧𝐟𝐫𝐚𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐮𝐜𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞 𝐄𝐮𝐫𝐨𝐩𝐞 𝐂𝐨𝐫𝐞 𝐅𝐮𝐧𝐝, a €2.2 billion vehicle designed to support the #longterm ownership and ongoing management of its #landmark #European #infrastructure assets. 
This closing marks a significant strategic milestone, strengthening the firm's ability to steward essential, high performing assets over extended horizons - often reaching up to 50 years.

The fund attracted strong demand from global institutional investors, with nearly 70% of commitments coming from new LPs. The remaining are existing LPs who chose to roll over and reaffirm their confidence in Meridiam’s long term strategy for an additional 20 years.

In parallel, Meridiam (as financial delegate) and Private Corner have launched 𝐌𝐞𝐫𝐢𝐝𝐢𝐚𝐦 𝐆𝐥𝐨𝐛𝐚𝐥 𝐈𝐧𝐟𝐫𝐚𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐮𝐜𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞 𝐒𝐭𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐞𝐠𝐢𝐞𝐬, Meridiam’s first fully #retail dedicated #evergreen fund of funds. This new product provides individual investors with access to a diversified, operational, and yield-generating infrastructure portfolio seeded with cornerstone assets from the 𝐌𝐞𝐫𝐢𝐝𝐢𝐚𝐦 𝐈𝐧𝐟𝐫𝐚𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐮𝐜𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞 𝐄𝐮𝐫𝐨𝐩𝐞 𝐂𝐨𝐫𝐞 𝐅𝐮𝐧𝐝.

Learn more from the press release: https://lnkd.in/eaU9jRSQ

authorHeadline:
Mirova is a global asset management company dedicated to sustainable investing and an affiliate of Natixis Investment Managers. At the forefront of sustainable finance for over a decade, Mirova has been developing innovative investment solutions across all asset classes, aiming to combine long term value creation with positive environmental and social impact. Headquartered in Paris, Mirova offers a broad range of equity, fixed income, multi-asset, energy transition infrastructure, natural capital and private equity solutions designed for institutional investors, distribution platforms and retail investors in Europe, North America, and Asia-Pacific. Mirova and its affiliates had €31.1 billion in assets under management as of June 30, 2024. Mirova is a mission-driven company, labeled B Corp*. 
*References to a ranking, award or label have no bearing on the future performance of any fund or manager.
