text:
👇 Live from New Delhi at the Indian Impact Summit with Guillaume Abel, Deputy CEO, and Arnaud Grapin, Head of IT, Data and CISO, building on Mirova’s strategic AI programme and exchanging with global AI & impact stakeholders. Their takeaways:

1️⃣ #AI is entering a new phase of maturity
• Generative AI is now widely adopted across organizations.
• Agentic AI is scaling fast, moving from content generation to autonomous execution of complex tasks.
• Physical AI is emerging, with real‑world applications on the horizon (robotics, surgery, autonomous mobility).
This evolution signals growing specialization, with different #LLMs dedicated to training, simulation and control, and real‑world deployment.

2️⃣ #Energy: both a decisive constraint and a solution space
The expected rise in #datacenters puts energy at the center of strategic decisions. At the same time, AI can support solutions: renewable forecasting, real‑time grid optimization, predictive maintenance, and #infrastructure #cybersecurity.

3️⃣ Value ultimately depends on trust, grounded in #governance, data and cybersecurity
Without robust governance, there can be no sustainable AI. Concerns around hallucinations, bias, auditability and cybersecurity are now central.
There is also growing convergence on the need for internationally aligned regulation, to avoid fragmented frameworks, and for global coalitions to support the development of inclusive, responsible and trustworthy AI.

#ArtificialIntelligence #SustainableFinance

#MarketOutlook 🔭 Stay updated with our Monthly Market Review & Outlook 👉 https://lnkd.in/eic_XqYh
📊 February 2026 presents a nuanced investment landscape with a dual dynamic: offensive and defensive asset flows supported by #AI-driven productivity as a key earnings growth engine. Europe leans into strategic autonomy and diversification away from the US, while the US benefits from solid macro fundamentals and AI-enabled productivity gains. Somes regions show notable dispersion in growth and inflation trajectories.
🔍 Sectors to watch include AI, #Technology, and #Sustainability. Europe’s energy transition, grid networks, and storage are central themes, with governance and #Sovereignty considerations shaping opportunities.

text:
We are pleased to share major milestones reached by Mirova and Evecon’s Baltic Renewable Energy Platform (BREP) in #Estonia 🔗 https://lnkd.in/ejECiGuv 
🔹the hybridization of the 77.5 MWp Kirikmäe solar PV plant with a 55 MW / 250 MWh battery energy storage system (BESS), currently under construction 
🔹BREP has also signed the first Flexibility & Power Purchase Agreement (FPPA) of its kind in the Baltics with Pure Energy, integrating renewable electricity sales and flexibility optimization under a long-term structure.
🔹To support this next phase of growth, Swedbank has committed a c.€85 million long-term financing package covering the refinancing of the existing solar PV portfolio and the construction of the Kirikmäe BESS.
Together, these developments illustrate how combining renewables, storage and long-term optimization tools can enhance grid stability and contribute to Europe’s #EnergyAutonomy.

#EnergyTransition #BatteryStorage #RenewableEnergy #Infrastructure #PrivateAssets

text:
The global acceleration of #Renewables is reshaping energy systems, creating new challenges and opportunities. Check out our latest paper 👉https://lnkd.in/e7A2Aaqx
🔹 A Paradigm Shift: Rising electricity demand from #AI and #Data centers accelerates the transition while stressing power grids.
🔹 Europe's Integration Challenge: A surge in negative electricity prices (up 12x in France) highlights the urgent need for grid modernization and storage to manage overproduction. 📉
🔹 The US Context: Favorable policies are driving massive grid investments, with operators raising over $22billion in 2025 for modernization.
🔹 Storage is Booming: Falling battery costs fuel exponential storage growth; California's capacity reached 15 GW by 2025, a model now accelerating in Europe. 
🔹 A New Investment Cycle: Investment focus is shifting from generation to enabling infrastructure like power grids, storage, and critical equipment.

#EnergyTransition #Electrification #EnergyStorage #ListedAssets #PrivateAssets #Equities #FixedIncome #Infrastructure

text:
🎤 How can European #savings become a genuine lever for #impact and #sovereignty in a world defined by disruption?

At the World Impact Summit, supported by Groupe BPCE, our CEO Philippe Zaouati reminded us of a simple but compelling idea: in times of uncertainty, clarity and honesty must guide Europe’s long‑term vision:
→ Europe must reaffirm a clear direction: transition, innovation, sovereignty.
→ Its most powerful tool already exists: a vast pool of under‑mobilized savings.
→ #SustainableFinance has a critical role to play in rebuilding trust and funding a resilient economic model.

Restoring confidence means ensuring stability: economical, technological and democratic. Protecting Europe also means protecting the framework that allows sustainable finance to thrive.

Money is never neutral. Every euro either destroys or transforms. Remaining still would be the real risk. Our mission: to help finance a world that endures, within a sovereign, stable, ambitious European framework.

*References to a ranking, award or label have no bearing on the future performance of any fund or manager.

authorTitle:
Mirova is a global asset management company dedicated to sustainable investing and an affiliate of Natixis Investment Managers. At the forefront of sustainable finance for over a decade, Mirova has been developing innovative investment solutions across all asset classes, aiming to combine long term value creation with positive environmental and social impact. Headquartered in Paris, Mirova offers a broad range of equity, fixed income, multi-asset, energy transition infrastructure, natural capital and private equity solutions designed for institutional investors, distribution platforms and retail investors in Europe, North America, and Asia-Pacific. Mirova and its affiliates had €31.1 billion in assets under management as of June 30, 2024. Mirova is a mission-driven company, labeled B Corp*. 
*References to a ranking, award or label have no bearing on the future performance of any fund or manager.

text:
Congratulations to the champions of the 12th edition of the Rio Open. Following a week of world-class tennis at the Mubadala-sponsored tournament, Tomas Martin Etcheverry from Argentina secured his first ATP title in the Singles, while Brazil's João Fonseca and Marcelo Melo successfully claimed the Doubles title.
 
Our partnership with the Rio Open underscores Mubadala's dedication to fostering talent and promoting excellence within global sport.
authorHeadline:
Mubadala Investment Company is a global, sovereign investor with an entrepreneurial mindset and a mandate to create sustainable returns for Abu Dhabi. Together, we build upon the legacy of the late Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan, who envisioned a free, stable and dignified life for the people of this country. 

We create value by leveraging our talent, our shareholder, our diverse portfolio, and our global presence and partnerships. As a responsible investor, we serve a wider social and economic development purpose through a principled work culture and by inspiring our businesses to make a positive and lasting impact on the communities in which they operate. 

Mubadala is a $330 billion (AED 1.2 trillion) business that spans six continents with interests across multiple sectors and asset classes. Headquartered in Abu Dhabi, Mubadala also has offices in London, Rio de Janeiro, New York, San Francisco and Beijing. At Mubadala we continue to look towards the future, with aspirations to double the size of our portfolio in the coming decade.

Our environment thrives on inspiration, partnerships, and continuous evolution and is tailored for those who aspire to not only reach new heights in their careers but also contribute to sustainable, long-term investments that shape future industries. Here, you’ll find opportunities to lead, innovate, and drive meaningful change in a variety of sectors.

text:
Discipline is the foundation of long-term investing.
 
Khaled Al Shamlan Al Marri, our CEO of Real Assets, shares why returns must begin with clarity on risk and a deliberate focus on the downside.
 
He describes building what he calls a “risk castle,” structuring investments to safeguard capital across cycles. For Khaled, investing is not about chasing momentum, but about resilience, preparation, and protecting value with a long-term lens.
 
Watch Episode 2 of ‘The Exchange’:

🔗 YouTube: https://bit.ly/46AUuw7
🔗 Spotify: https://bit.ly/4aGwKrt
🔗 Apple Podcast: https://apple.co/4kJKpm1

Hosted by Mustafa Alrawi

text:
We are supporting Stake’s continued growth through our participation in its oversubscribed Series B funding round, which was led by Emirates NBD, and had additional participation from Middle East Venture Partners (MEVP), Property Finder, STV NICE, Wa’ed Ventures, GFH Partners, and Ellington Properties, raising US$31 million.
 
This investment reflects our confidence in Stake’s role in modernizing how real estate is accessed, and supports the UAE’s long-term economic diversification.

text:
Our Managing Director and Group CEO, Khaldoon Khalifa Al Mubarak, joined Brad Smith, Microsoft's Vice Chair and President, on his 'Tools and Weapons' podcast to discuss how sovereign capital is shaping the AI era and reinforcing the UAE’s leadership in adoption.

Amid rapid technological acceleration, he underscored the distinctive role investors play in enabling emerging technologies, investing in foundational infrastructure, and taking the long view required to translate innovation into sustainable, lasting impact.

text:
We have announced a landmark partnership with H&H Development, a Dubai-based developer renowned for design excellence and a portfolio of best-in-class developments, to bring the esteemed Eden House brand to Abu Dhabi.
 
The new development, strategically located on Al Maryah Island, Abu Dhabi's premier business and lifestyle destination, solidifies a shared vision to deliver a new benchmark for sophisticated, high-end residential living while strengthening the UAE's luxury residential ecosystem.

Together, we build upon the legacy of the late Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan, who envisioned a free, stable and dignified life for the people of this country. 

We create value by leveraging our talent, our shareholder, our diverse portfolio, and our global presence and partnerships. As a responsible investor, we serve a wider social and economic development purpose through a principled work culture and by inspiring our businesses to make a positive and lasting impact on the communities in which they operate. 

Mubadala is a $330 billion (AED 1.2 trillion) business that spans six continents with interests across multiple sectors and asset classes. Headquartered in Abu Dhabi, Mubadala also has offices in London, Rio de Janeiro, New York, San Francisco and Beijing. At Mubadala we continue to look towards the future, with aspirations to double the size of our portfolio in the coming decade.

Our environment thrives on inspiration, partnerships, and continuous evolution and is tailored for those who aspire to not only reach new heights in their careers but also contribute to sustainable, long-term investments that shape future industries. Here, you’ll find opportunities to lead, innovate, and drive meaningful change in a variety of sectors.

authorHeadline:
Northleaf is a global private markets investment firm focused on mid-market companies and assets. With more than US$31 billion in capital commitments raised, Northleaf has an established, long-term track record as an investor in private equity, private credit and infrastructure globally. Northleaf serves some of the world’s leading institutional investors and family offices.

Northleaf’s 300-person team, located in Toronto, Chicago, London, Los Angeles, Melbourne, Menlo Park, Montreal, New York, Seoul and Tokyo, is focused exclusively on sourcing, evaluating and managing private markets investments globally. Northleaf currently manages eight global private equity funds, three specialist private equity secondaries funds, one direct private equity fund, three global private credit funds, three OECD-focused infrastructure funds and a series of customized investment mandates tailored to meet the specific needs of institutional investors and family offices.

text:
We continue to view private equity as a core driver of long‑term returns and portfolio diversification, even as macroeconomic uncertainty persists across global markets.

In our Private Equity Market Update: Q4‑2025, we share our perspective on the themes shaping the private equity landscape, with a focus on how the mid-market provides opportunities for operational value creation, active ownership, and disciplined investing. We believe these fundamentals remain central to building resilient portfolios and supporting durable performance through periods of disruption.

The update also highlights the continued evolution of the private equity secondary market, which has become an increasingly important tool for portfolio construction and liquidity management for institutional investors.

Read the full commentary here: https://lnkd.in/g98NpaJi

text:
The fourth quarter closed the year against a more stable economic outlook relative to the first half of 2025. Although tariff policies continued to have an impact on certain segments of the economy and market volatility persisted, overall economic growth proved resilient in the latter part of the year, helping to bolster investor confidence heading into 2026. 
 
Key highlights from our Q4-2025 Private Credit Market Update include:
 
• Easing base rates and stabilized but tightened credit spreads
 
 • Improving momentum in deal volume, though the recovery in LBO/M&A activity has been gradual
 
 • Stronger relative value across core mid-market direct lending, Europe and asset-based specialty finance
 
 • Continued sector bifurcation with higher expected losses in select sub-segments, including consumer and healthcare services, though overall borrower performance remains relatively resilient

Read more: https://lnkd.in/ganwhqSp
 
#PrivateCredit #MidMarket #PrivateMarkets

We are pleased to announce that Matthew Altman will be joining NOVA Infrastructure as Vice President. His strong infrastructure and energy investing experience makes him the perfect addition to the growing NOVA team as we enter a new chapter of growth. Welcome, Matt!
 
We are also excited to share the promotions of Noah Frick and Michael Troy to Vice President and Jeremy Feinstein to Senior Associate. Noah, Michael, and Jeremy have made significant contributions to the firm and its portfolio companies in their time at NOVA, and these promotions recognize their hard work and dedication. 
 
Read more about these exciting updates: https://lnkd.in/eimuTvp7

Nuveen is a global investment leader, managing public and private assets for clients around the world and on behalf of TIAA, our parent company and one of the world’s largest institutional investors. We invest in the growth of businesses, real estate, infrastructure and natural capital, providing clients with the reliability, access and foresight unique to our heritage. Our prevailing perspective on the future drives our ambition to innovate and adapt our business to the changing needs of investors — all to pursue lasting performance for our clients, our communities and our global economy.

✅ AI & Digital Infrastructure

Commercial real estate has repriced and opportunity is emerging.

Get the global perspective in the latest Q&A with Private Debt Investor: https://ow.ly/hN9H50YceTI

text:
In his latest memo – occasioned by massive changes he cites in just the 11 weeks since the last one – Howard Marks revisits the subject of AI, providing his observations on its essence and how it differs from previous technological innovations. He highlights AI’s immense power, speed, and autonomy; its limitations; and its potential implications for investing. He expresses conviction that AI is far from a fad, but also that investors must view its transformational potential against an assessment of the appropriateness of the prices of AI assets: https://lnkd.in/gvMZfPFH

Click here to read and subscribe to memos from Howard Marks: https://lnkd.in/gppRhcA
authorHeadline:
Oaktree is a leader among global investment managers specializing in alternative investments, with $218 billion in assets under management as of September 30, 2025. The firm emphasizes an opportunistic, value-oriented, and risk-controlled approach to investments in credit, equity, and real estate. The firm has more than 1,400 employees and offices in 26 cities worldwide.

For additional information, please visit Oaktree’s website at http://www.oaktreecapital.com/.

text:
Oaktree is pleased to be named the ‘Best International Asset Manager’ for the second edition of the Outliers InfoMoney Awards. 

This award recognizes the international asset manager that stood out in the Brazilian market by bringing excellence and innovation to the country and by contributing to Brazilian investors’ access to global markets.

For additional information: https://lnkd.in/gkaqmQ8w #AlternativeInvesting

Important Information: The evaluation considers (i) Successful integration into the Brazilian market (including local structures, products, and client base), as well as a track record of growing relevance in the country; (ii) Distinction in fundraising, performance, or innovation across its investment strategies; and (iii) Positive impact on the local industry, through partnerships or the development of new practices and products. For this purpose, global asset managers that distribute active management strategies in Brazil through feeder structures were analyzed, identifying those that showed the strongest growth, the largest number of investors, and recognizing those that have been pioneers in the Brazilian market. Oaktree neither submitted nor provided compensation for this award series.

text:
We are pleased to announce the successful close of €770 million in new debt facilities at Borealis Spain Parent B.V., the holding company for OMERS ~25% stake in Exolum, a Spanish-headquartered global energy logistics infrastructure company providing specialised solutions to support the energy transition in which OMERS has been directly invested since 2016. 
 
Michael Hill, Executive Vice President and Global Head of OMERS Infrastructure, said: “The scale, pricing, and the engagement of both bank and private placement lenders in this process demonstrate the strong fundamentals and quality of the Exolum investment, as well as the expertise of our team. The offering was oversubscribed, with lenders recognizing the company’s robust growth, effective energy transition diversification strategy, and the strength of its leadership. We extend our appreciation to everyone involved and thank Exolum’s management for their valuable support throughout this process.”
 
The company owns the transmission pipeline network spanning 4,000km in Spain and operates a 2,000km pipeline network in the UK. Exolum also owns 68 storage terminals with a total capacity of 11+ million cbm and serves over 48 airports worldwide – including Heathrow, Gatwick, Stansted, Madrid, Barcelona, Lisbon, Lima in Peru, and Charles de Gaulle – making it a global leader in aviation fuel infrastructure.
 
Special shout out to the team – Susannah Lowndes, Timm Degenhardt, Irini Kalamakis, Abigail Farrelly, Sofía Gimeno Pérez de Ascanio, Sara Petrov, Orla Ralston, Dimitri Merza and our Amsterdam colleagues.

text:
How do you build and develop an outstanding investment team?
 
At the recent 2025 Rest Investments Offsite, Stasha Prnjatovic, one of our Managing Directors based in Sydney, participated in a panel to share her perspective on what it takes to be part of a successful team. The discussion explored the foundations of investment success, including the importance of embracing new technologies such as AI, and of creating an inclusive, high-performing culture. 
 
Stasha explained how incorporating diverse thinking into the investment process, asset management projects and partnership model with management, enables colleagues to bring their authentic selves to work. At OMERS, we’re passionate about fostering a culture of partnership, excellence and positivity, and we’re proud of our amazing team around the world . 
 
Thank you for the opportunity to take part, Rest Investments!

text:
Congratulations, team! Our investment in Grandi Stazioni Retail S.p.a. (GSR) has won the European Transportation Deal of the Year at the IJGlobal Awards in London. The investment marked OMERS first infrastructure investment in Italy and 19th in Europe. GSR holds the concession to manage commercial leasing and advertising spaces at 14 major Italian railway stations, serving nearly 800 million visitors annually – about five times the total traffic of all Italian airports. 
 
Over the past year, we've worked with partners and management to modernize key stations and enhance passenger experience by adding new escalators at Milano Centrale, re-developing Ala Mazzoniana at Roma Termini, and opening Bar Centrale by Cova at Milano Centrale. Proud of what we’ve achieved together! Congratulations to everyone involved! 👏

Further details about the awards can be found here: https://lnkd.in/etzAvMiH

text:
Over the past 10 years, OMERS has deployed over AUD 4 billion into energy, transport and digital assets in Australia.  
 
Our Head of Asia-Pacific, Christopher Curtain, has played a vital role in establishing this dynamic presence in the region and, last week, he spoke on a panel at Infrastructure Investor’s Australia Forum about how we have grown our presence in this vibrant market. Recent developments include our new partnership with Future Fund on Transgrid, and Waveconn’s completed acquisition of 340 mobile tower and rooftop sites from Optus.
 
As we progress through our second decade on the ground, Chris explained how Australia would remain one of OMERS priority markets notwithstanding the increasing competition from other countries seeking investment from global pools of capital. We look forward to seeing what the next decade holds Down Under! Thank you for the opportunity to take part, Infrastructure Investor.

text:
We’re proud to announce a marquee Canadian financing transaction by our team in the Bruce Power investment!

We are pleased to share that BPC Generation Infrastructure Trust (BGIT), the holding company for OMERS investment in Bruce Power, has completed its inaugural senior unsecured bond issuance totaling C$1.5 billion across 5, 7, and 10-year tranches of notes. This transaction represents the largest domestic holding company bond issuance to date within the Canadian power and utilities sector.

Michael Hill, Executive Vice President and Global Head of OMERS Infrastructure, said: “This transaction marks a milestone in Canadian infrastructure financing. The scale, pricing, and investor engagement in this process reflects the high quality and underlying strength of the Bruce Power investment and the caliber of our outstanding team. Through this process, we have now built a scalable platform to access, and further collaborate with, Canadian debt capital markets. Congratulations to everyone involved and thank you to Bruce Power’s leadership team for support throughout this process.”

OMERS has proudly been directly invested in Bruce Power, located in OMERS home province of Ontario, since 2003. Over more than two decades, the OMERS team has played an active role in advising and collaborating with the company and its management team to deliver the company’s vision and support its growth. Bruce Power currently produces approximately 6,550 megawatts of peak clean energy, representing about 30% of Ontario’s electricity.

Special shout out to the team involved – Peter Gray, CFA, Steven Zucchet, Blair McKechnie, Sara Petrov, Matt Liddle, Ryan Wang, Kate Kocur, Matthew Mulholland.

text:
Exciting announcement about Waveconn, our wholly-owned digital asset in Australia! With our support, Waveconn has entered into an agreement to acquire 340 mobile tower and rooftop sites from Optus. This will be an excellent bolt-on to the existing Waveconn platform and it offers many important strategic benefits, including meaningfully increasing the scale of Waveconn’s footprint by up to 20%.
 
This is another proud moment for OMERS in Oz. Now in our second decade of investing in the country, OMERS has deployed over AUD 4 billion into energy, transport and digital assets. Huge congratulations to everyone involved!

text:
Exciting leadership announcement! We’re thrilled to share that Jenine Krause has been appointed Senior Managing Director, Portfolio Management and Operations, effective July 1. Jenine will continue to be based in Toronto and will be responsible for leading the development and implementation of the OMERS Infrastructure global operating strategy. She will join the Infrastructure Management Committee and Infrastructure Investment Committee.

Since joining us in 2022, Jenine has made significant contributions as a board member across key assets, including Puget Sound Energy, Beanfield and Teranet. She has overseen the implementation of key strategic initiatives across a variety of investments and strengthened value creation frameworks across the platform leading the global Asset Management Committee.

Michael Hill, our Global Head of Infrastructure, said: “Jenine has made a tremendous impact since joining our team almost three years ago and we’re thrilled to announce her promotion. As we look for new ways to drive value in our investment portfolio across the energy, transportation and digital sectors, Jenine’s accomplished leadership and asset management background will be invaluable.”

Jenine Krause said: “I’ve been hugely impressed by the investment capabilities and dedication of the team since joining OMERS. We have a special culture, a purpose that drives what we do and how we work, and a global Infrastructure portfolio with whom we effectively partner to generate value for the Fund and drive meaningful impact in the communities in which we invest. We have a clear Infrastructure strategy, an ambitious operating plan, and an outstanding team in place to realize our vision. I look forward to taking on this role.”

Congratulations, Jenine!

Ontario Teachers’ is a fully funded defined benefit pension plan, and it invests in a broad array of asset classes to deliver retirement security for 343,000 working members and pensioners.

For information on our legal Terms of Use, visit https://www.otpp.com/en-ca/terms/linkedin-account/

text:
2025 marked 35 years since Ontario Teachers’ was founded in 1990. It was built on a bold, first-of-its-kind model that helped shape the global “Maple 8” standard for pension excellence.

To mark this milestone, we sat down with past and present leaders to share the story of Ontario Teachers’ in their own words.

From our beginnings as a newly independent plan to becoming one of the world’s leading institutional investors, our journey reflects 35 years of innovation, leadership, and a steadfast commitment to delivering retirement security for Ontario’s teachers.

text:
It was great to welcome so many investors to the second edition of our Private Markets and Evergreen Solutions event held in Madrid this week with the support of Embarcadero Private Equity.

Our audience – made up of banks, insurance companies, family and multi-family offices, wealth managers, and pension funds – heard presentations from Pantheon partners Petra Bukovec, Richard Sem, and Toni Vainio on the opportunity in evergreen solutions across private equity, infrastructure, and private credit, and an update on Pantheon’s innovations in this sector.

Pantheon has been at the forefront of evergreen investing since launching its first vehicle more than a decade ago. We offer a range of evergreen solutions, with a combined $7.8bn in assets under management across our global platform.

You can read more on our evergreen offering here: https://lnkd.in/emZE4QHf. Or reach out to our local relationship managers Gonzalo Bonhome Peinado and Carlos I. Arias.

For more information on Pantheon click here: https://lnkd.in/eahSMD-A

authorHeadline:
Pantheon has been at the forefront of private markets investing for more than 40 years, earning a reputation for providing innovative solutions covering the full lifecycle of investments, from primary fund commitments to co-investments, secondary purchases and direct customized solutions, across private equity, real assets and private credit.

We have partnered with more than 1,000 clients, including institutional investors of all sizes as well as a growing number of private wealth advisers and investors, with approximately $62bn in discretionary assets under management and a combined $94.6bn in assets managed or advised (as of June 30, 2023) through a wide range of pooled funds and bespoke, customized accounts.

Using creative approaches informed by our specialized experience and delivered by a global team of professionals based in offices across Europe, the Americas and Asia, we invest with purpose and lead with expertise to build secure financial futures.

text:
Pantheon's Chief Investment Officer, Jeffrey Miller, explores how the volatility of 2025 has diminished, but not disappeared. Against a macro backdrop of ongoing geopolitical risk and the uncertain impact of AI on labor, there is still some uncertainty in the system, and fund managers have to separate this noise from real investment signals.

But even in this more complex world, opportunities can be found with a sophisticated, focused, and intelligent approach to investing. This is where experience and expertise comes into play, and where the importance of bottom-up analysis of underlying assets in secondaries investments is emphasized. 

To read the full paper on Private Markets in 2026, including exclusive insights from our asset class heads, click here: https://lnkd.in/e7p9TPZM. 

For more information on Pantheon, click here: https://lnkd.in/eahSMD-A.

text:
Pantheon's Chief Investment Officer, Jeffrey Miller, discusses how valuations have been improving across private markets, but greater dispersion means there are still plenty of pockets where deals are more expensive. This means having access to the whole deal flow is hugely valuable. That allows investors to set the bar high, only selecting the deals with the best risk reward, and to maintain underwriting discipline.

To read the full paper on Private Markets in 2026, including exclusive insights from our asset class heads, click here: https://lnkd.in/e7p9TPZM.

For more information on Pantheon click here: https://lnkd.in/eahSMD-A.

text:
Open-ended funds, commonly known as “semi-liquids”, are reshaping private markets due, in part, to their liquidity features. 
 
According to Campbell Lutyens’ Secondary Market Report, roughly 60% of semi-liquid fundraising is earmarked for secondaries, spotlighting the benefits secondaries play within evergreen structures; potentially smoothing durations, generating nearer-term cash flow, and enabling redemption flexibility.
 
As we explore in Pantheon’s latest piece “Just how liquid is your semi-liquid fund?”, including more secondary exposure can meaningfully influence liquidity in evergreen vehicles. 
 
Read more: https://lnkd.in/e3tTzBTf
 
​About the series: Decoding Private Markets equips private wealth investors and private market allocators with clarity on private markets investing. From fund performance metrics to due diligence, liquidity realities to the evolving role of secondaries, we cover all the key concepts with actionable insights that make sophisticated strategies more accessible to private wealth portfolios.​
​
To read more about our private wealth solutions, click here: https://lnkd.in/emZE4QHf

#privatemarkets #evergreens #semiliquid #privatewealth

text:
After a record year for volumes in 2025, secondaries markets globally are set to reach a new level of maturity in 2026. Growth has not only been driven by cyclical liquidity needs, but structural shifts now mean secondaries are a standard part of the playbook for portfolio management.

In the video below, Pantheon's Chief Investment Officer, Jeffrey Miller, discusses how secondaries markets have become thicker and more reliable for both buyers and sellers, while product innovation means the market is continuing to evolve. 

To read Pantheon’s full paper on Private Markets in 2026, including exclusive insights from our asset class heads, click here: https://lnkd.in/e7p9TPZM.

For more information on Pantheon click here: https://lnkd.in/eahSMD-A.

authorHeadline:
Partners Group is a global private markets firm with USD 185 billion in assets under management and c. 2,000 professionals across 24 offices worldwide. 

We seek to generate superior returns through capitalizing on thematic growth trends and transforming attractive businesses and assets into market leaders. As a committed, responsible investor, we aim to deliver lasting, positive impact for all our stakeholders. We provide an innovative range of bespoke client solutions to institutional investors, sovereign wealth funds, family offices and private individuals globally. 

Partners Group has been listed on the SIX Swiss Exchange since 2006 (symbol: PGHN).

text:
Exciting times at Partners Group as we look ahead to our next office opening, this time in Kuwait as we intend to further extend our footprint in the Gulf Cooperation Council region. We have had a presence in the Middle East since the opening of our Dubai office in 2010, before adding our office in Abu Dhabi in 2025. Today, our portfolio companies employ over 2,000 individuals in the region, with many actively looking to increase their footprint. 

The Kuwait office will bring Partners Group’s total number of offices worldwide to 25.

Read the full press release here: https://lnkd.in/ef3Jti32

text:
Last week at our office in Zug, we brought together thought leaders in maritime decarbonisation to move the conversation from ambition to implementation — especially in the context of an uncertain and evolving regulatory landscape.
 
Our portfolio company, North Star, is the UK's leading offshore vessel operator, providing critical support vessels — including service operation vessels (SOVs) that enable safe, efficient access to offshore wind farms. Decarbonising SOV operations is a key lever for reducing emissions across the offshore wind value chain.
 
We explored practical pathways for SOV decarbonization - from hybridization and battery energy storage systems (BESS) to future fuels – along with the real-world implications for operations and business cases, and what it will take to scale solutions: efficiency gains now, optionality and fuel flexibility, and alignment across the wider value chain. 
 
The discussions were a strong example of our industry coming together to solve shared challenges — openly, pragmatically, and with a bias to action.
 
Thank you to RWE and DNV for contributing your perspectives and helping make the session so practical and forward-looking.
 
These efforts reflect North Star's dedication to leading the maritime industry's transition to cleaner and more sustainable operations. Supporting that journey is closely aligned with how we think about value creation and resilience through the energy transition at Partners Group.

text:
We are excited to announce the next milestone in our partnership with BlackRock, with the launch of a multi-alternatives SMA for US wealth advisors.

The first-of-its-kind solution includes three outcome aligned profiles, giving wealth advisors the ability to better match their clients’ objectives to a matching private markets allocation - all in a single account.

Companies are staying private for longer and there is increasing concentration in public markets - but advisors remain under allocated to private markets due to a fragmented client experience and limited scalability—these SMA strategies aim to rectify these barriers to access. 

Read the full press release here: https://lnkd.in/eAezftzM

New investment strategy alert! We are pleased to formally launch our special opportunities strategy, to take advantage of the growth in investment opportunities that is expected to be generated by a transforming economy in the decade ahead. The team is led by Josh Hartz, who has joined us as a Partner from Bain Capital.

The strategy will focus on three types of transactions across corporate solutions, real assets, and other liquidity driven opportunities and will invest on a global basis. Special opportunities is the latest investment strategy that Partners Group has added to its multi-strategy private markets platform since adding royalties in late 2024.

text:
In Latin America, a scarcity of reliable capital and a persistent regional risk premium continue to keep spreads elevated, even when underlying fundamentals are strong.

It’s within this backdrop that Patria Investments is preparing the next vintage of our Latin America private credit strategy, designed to give global institutional investors access to the region’s large, structurally under-levered corporate credit market.

We are building on:
- 25 years in-region experience and a US$12.3bn* AUM regional credit platform
- The close of our first dedicated LatAm private credit fund and related vehicles in May 2025, which raised US$314m in commitments
- A focus on senior-secured, US dollar-denominated loans with robust collateral, covenants, and cash-flow protections - supporting mid-market and family-owned businesses

As Javier Montero, Partner and Head of Private Credit, puts it: “Patria is built to convert this structural inefficiency into repeatable alpha through proprietary origination at scale, structuring-first underwriting, and a long-memory information edge under one risk framework.”

Learn more: https://lnkd.in/eNy-RdGr

#PatriaInvestments #PatriaCredit #PrivateCredit

*Pro-Forma aggregate AUM includes 1 acquisition announced, WP Global Partners, and 2 completed, Solis and RBR by February 3, 2026

text:
Last Monday, we were delighted to announce the acquisition of WP Global Partners, a U.S.-based private equity solutions manager focused on the lower middle market.

This acquisition strengthens Patria’s North American presence, enhancing U.S. investment and fundraising capacity, and complementing our Private Equity Solutions franchise to meet growing global demand for middle-market private equity.
 
Patria’s Global Private Markets Solutions platform now exceeds $13.3 billion in Fee-Earning AUM, with nearly 40% in U.S. assets, marking a meaningful expansion in a key strategic market.
 
Founded in 2005, WP Global Partners brings an experienced team of over 30 employees, including ten investment professionals, and has deployed over $6 billion across diverse sectors.
 
This acquisition further accelerates Patria’s long term diversification strategy — advancing our global platform across private equity, infrastructure, credit, and real estate, and expanding our suite of investment solutions for clients worldwide.
 
“This is an important step forward in bringing to Patria a talented team with a proven track record and strong reputation,” said Marco D'Ippolito, Managing Partner. “This transaction broadens the universe of GPs in our ecosystem and strengthens our product offering across private equity primaries, secondaries and co investments in the U.S. lower middle market.”
 
Additional information is available at: https://lnkd.in/e3pD_cVV

text:
📣 Patria Investments Full Year Results 📣 

We are delighted to publish our 4Q and FY results for the 12-months ending 31 December 2025. 

Key highlights include:

 📈 Fundraising: Record $7.7bn of capital raised FY and $1.7bn in 4Q, excluding M&A. We exceeded our fundraising target by over $1bn 

 🎯 FEAUM: Fee Earning AUM reached $40.8bn, up 24% compared to year-end 2024, and $203mn of Fee Related earnings in 2025, a 19% year-over-year increase

 💡 New Investments and Acquisitions: We are proud to have completed the acquisition of 51% of the Brazilian private credit manager Solis; we also acquired several Brazilian REITS from RBR, making Patria the largest manager of listed REITS in Brazil; and this week we announced that we are bolstering our U.S. presence with the acquisition of WP Global Partners, which will enhance our capabilities in our global private markets business. 

Read the full release at the link below. 👇🏽

Patria Investments is heading to IPEM 2026 in Cannes!
 
Patria Investments is delighted to announce our attendance at IPEM from February 3-5. We bring 37 years of deep expertise and a trusted track record in generating attractive returns as a global alternative asset manager.
 
With $51 billion in assets under management, we specialize in the mid-market segment, offering robust strategies across primaries, secondaries, and co-investment. To arrange a meeting, please contact Eduardo.Pinho@patria.com

text:
❓ 𝐇𝐨𝐰 𝐟𝐚𝐦𝐢𝐥𝐢𝐚𝐫 𝐚𝐫𝐞 𝐲𝐨𝐮 𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐜𝐨𝐨𝐥𝐢𝐧𝐠-𝐚𝐬-𝐚-𝐬𝐞𝐫𝐯𝐢𝐜𝐞?
 
If your answer is 'not familiar', or even if you are familiar with cooling-as-a-service (CaaS), then you might be surprised to learn that CaaS won the Zayed Sustainability Prize in the Energy category this year, recognising global sustainability innovations. The prize is UAE’s award for innovative solutions to global challenges.
 
The winning firm, BASE Foundation, created the SET Alliance in 2022 to empower visionary organisations to accelerate the energy transition through CaaS. One of the partner organisations in the Alliance is Kaer, which is the fastest-growing CaaS provider in Asia and which PATRIZIA, together with Mitsui & Co., Ltd., invested in last year.
 
To learn more about CaaS and PATRIZIA's investment, read our informative article by PATRIZIA Associate Director - Sustainability Infrastructure Jane Baseby and PATRIZIA Operating Partner - Infrastructure Patrick L. here: https://lnkd.in/eGHHF-jj
 
And for more on this year's Zayed Sustainability Prize winners, visit https://lnkd.in/enMNyMak
 
#PATRIZIA #Infrastructure #Investment #Asia #CaaS #Sustainability #Innovation #EnergyTransition #RealAssets

text:
🏘️ 𝐓𝐡𝐞 𝐡𝐨𝐮𝐬𝐢𝐧𝐠 - 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐚𝐟𝐟𝐨𝐫𝐝𝐚𝐛𝐥𝐞 𝐡𝐨𝐮𝐬𝐢𝐧𝐠 - 𝐩𝐢𝐜𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞 𝐢𝐧 𝐒𝐩𝐚𝐢𝐧

Like the rest of Europe, Spain has a large deficit of homes but public housing makes up just 2.5% of its total stock versus an average of 9% elsewhere in the EU. The affordable housing need is acute, with private investment an important lever to increase supply.

As part of its impact investing strategy, PATRIZIA is developing sustainable, affordable housing in Spanish cities. To learn more about the company's affordable housing drive in Spain, and in the UK, read our article which features commentary by PATRIZIA Fund Manager Marleen Bekkers: https://lnkd.in/dnSYm4fh

#PATRIZIA #RealEstate #Investment #Europe #Spain #AffordableHousing #ImpactInvesting #Urbanisation #ModernLiving #RealAssets #RealInsights

European living markets have undergone a significant repricing over the past three years and are now entering a new cycle. With values rebased and a severe undersupply of modern housing, conditions are turning favourable for investors prepared to move early.

This is the view shared by PATRIZIA Head of Investment Management Living Antonio Marin-Bataller in his article on the living sector in our 2026 Outlook publication. To read the full piece, which calls upon insights from our Investment Strategy & Data Intelligence team and refers to sub-sectors such as student housing and affordable housing, as well as highlighting asset highlights from PATRIZIA's portfolio, visit https://lnkd.in/dj9wnU5s

📈 𝐑𝐞𝐧𝐞𝐰𝐞𝐝 𝐦𝐨𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐮𝐦 𝐢𝐧 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐧𝐞𝐰 𝐜𝐲𝐜𝐥𝐞

PATRIZIA CEO Asoka Wöhrmann is one of 43 CEOs and senior executives from European-based firms featured in Real Asset INSIGHT's CEO Outlook 2026. The Q&A with Asoka covers:

👍 How the new cycle will be slower, longer and bumpier - yet underpinned by renewed momentum
🌍 How the cycle is being shaped by the DUEL megatrends - digitalisation, urbanisation, energy transition and living transition - which act as a compass for PATRIZIA's investment strategy
💻 Why Asoka's 2025 milestone highlight was the sale of Aligned Data Centers
🏘️ Why the living sector remains one of the most structurally compelling areas within European real assets and is a strategic priority for PATRIZIA
🏙️ The strategic importance of infrastructure, as well as the growing opportunity at the intersection of infrastructure and living to support urbanisation

To access the magazine and the full article with Asoka, visit https://lnkd.in/dQG2ACJW (log-in required)

PATRIZIA manages more than EUR 46 billion in assets and employs over 800 professionals at 24 locations worldwide. Through its PATRIZIA Foundation, PATRIZIA is committed to social responsibility. The Foundation has helped over 220,000 children in need worldwide over the last 20 years. You can find further information at www.patrizia.ag.

text:
🌍 2026 𝐎𝐮𝐭𝐥𝐨𝐨𝐤: 𝐒𝐭𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐞𝐠𝐢𝐞𝐬 𝐮𝐧𝐝𝐞𝐫𝐩𝐢𝐧𝐧𝐞𝐝 𝐛𝐲 𝐃𝐔𝐄𝐋 𝐦𝐞𝐠𝐚𝐭𝐫𝐞𝐧𝐝𝐬 𝐨𝐟𝐟𝐞𝐫 𝐛𝐞𝐬𝐭 𝐨𝐩𝐩𝐨𝐫𝐭𝐮𝐧𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐞𝐬
 
What can we expect in the year ahead for real assets investment? PATRIZIA Head of Investment Strategy Radu Mircea tackles this broad question, setting the scene in our 2026 Outlook publication, in his article which covers:

📈 Stable interest rates, and fiscal and policy tailwinds in Europe
🌏 The vital role of emerging markets in advancing the energy transition
💻 The AI boom creating a CapEx supercycle
🏢 The renewed attractiveness of the office sector
🏘️ The living sector's leading role in European real estate investment, including emerging segments such as student accommodation and affordable housing
💶 Value creation in sectors underpinned by the DUEL megatrends (digitalisation, urbanisation, energy transition and living transition) and the importance of tactical tilts in portfolios

To read Radu's article, visit https://lnkd.in/ebvb2fB4
 
#PATRIZIA #RealAssets #Investment #Outlook #InvestmentStrategy #AI #Offices #Digitalisation #Urbanisation #EnergyTransition #ModernLiving #Infrastructure #RealEstate #RealExperts #RealInsights

Alexandre’s exceptional talent and leadership have long been instrumental to PSP Investments in delivering value for our beneficiaries and advancing our strategic objectives. As Chief Risk Officer, he will continue to contribute to the long-term success of PSP Investments
  
Congratulations Alexandre!

authorHeadline:
The Public Sector Pension Investment Board (PSP Investments) is one of Canada's largest pension investors with $299.7 billion of net assets as of March 31, 2025. It manages a diversified global portfolio composed of investments in public financial markets, private equity, real estate, infrastructure, natural resources and private debt. Established in 1999, PSP Investments manages net contributions to the pension funds of the federal Public Service, the Canadian Forces, the Royal Canadian Mounted Police and the Reserve Force. Headquartered in Ottawa, PSP Investments has its principal business office in Montréal and offices in New York, London and Hong Kong. For more information, visit investpsp.com or follow us on LinkedIn. 

Social media community terms of use

At PSP Investments, we welcome respectful and constructive exchanges on our social media accounts. 

While we encourage your comments on our posts, we reserve the right to delete, hide or block comments that are in violation of our Social Media Community Terms of Use: https://www.investpsp.com/en/footer/terms-use/

Violations of our Terms of Use may also result in the user being permanently blocked from commenting on our accounts.

text:
It was a pleasure to welcome Queensland Treasurer, Hon. David Janetzki MP to our Brisbane office today, where he spent time with teams from across the business.  

We shared what we’re seeing across markets, what it takes to keep Queensland competitive for investment, and our focus on delivering long-term value for our clients and for Queenslanders.
authorHeadline:
QIC is one of Australia’s largest institutional investment managers, with A$135bn+ in assets under management.

Headquartered in Brisbane, we are a private markets specialist with deep fixed income and overlays expertise, investing across infrastructure, real estate, private equity, private debt and liquid markets.

We partner with approximately 125 institutional investors and government clients globally, applying disciplined judgment to deliver resilient performance over the long term.

With offices also in Sydney, Melbourne, New York, San Francisco, London and Singapore, we combine deep Australian heritage with global capability.

Established in 1991 to manage capital for the Queensland Government, QIC operates with legislated investment independence. Our sovereign ownership provides stability and accountability.

We remain deeply connected to our Queensland heritage and committed to delivering long-term economic benefits for Queenslanders.

Today, close to 1,000 professionals across our corporate and investment functions work together to create shared value by responsibly investing for the prosperity of clients, people and communities.

text:
Clear blue skies are powering innovation at Brussels Airport, with a pilot program underway to share solar power generated onsite with local households.

One hundred households are currently receiving solar power directly from Brussels Airport’s solar panels, improving access to renewable energy and strengthening community ties, with even more households expected to benefit as the airport increases its solar capacity.

Read more below.

text:
North West Queensland’s abundant copper reserves are critical to building the state’s energy future.

That’s why we’re backing the refurbishment of the Rocklands Copper Mine and sulphide processing hub through a A$15 million cornerstone commitment to Austral Resources Australia.

This investment underpins a two-year refurbishment program ahead of operations recommencing in mid-2027, bringing established processing infrastructure back online to strengthen Queensland’s copper supply capacity. https://lnkd.in/gVqsSCVi

text:
The capital is here. The ambition is here. The focus now is scale.

Earlier this week, Queensland’s innovation community gathered for the launch of the 2025 State of Australian Startup Funding Report, authored by Cut Through Venture and Folklore Ventures.

As third-time hosts of this event, we’ve had a front row seat to the growth of Queensland’s startup landscape. The discussion has moved beyond “can we attract capital?” to “how do we build enduring, scalable businesses?"

With strong momentum and an established opportunity set, one thing is clear: Queensland’s startup ecosystem is primed for growth.

Special thanks to Chris Gillings and Alister Coleman for their engaging discussion and insights from the report.

Nicholas Guest, Crystal Russell, Stephen Whatmore, Matiela Baker, Nicola Wightman, Nick Capell, Leo Channon, Patrick Christiansen, Brandon Clews, Sean Ferguson, Aaron Kane Massingham, Matthew Mather, Eden Peterson, Henry Swan, Julien Taylor, Bailey Wallace

Food, fuel and baseload power, all from the same fields.

That’s the future Kalfresh Vegetables is leading with Australia’s first integrated food and energy precinct in the Scenic Rim, transforming local food and farm waste into renewable electricity and natural gas for Queensland homes, industry and transport.

Alongside Wollemi Capital, we are proud to support Kalfresh as they demonstrate a scalable platform for agriculture and clean energy to work hand-in-hand. https://lnkd.in/gyfFyX3r

text:
Australia has the fundamentals, natural advantages and policy leadership to position itself as a global leader in sustainable energy. That’s the perspective shared by QIC Infrastructure’s Ross Israel, Kirsten Whitehead and Patrick Mulholland in Investment & Pensions Europe (IPE).  

We’re continuing to see significant opportunities to deploy capital at scale across the value chain, from off-grid remote energy to storage and connection assets. There are also substantial opportunities in the transport sector, where structural tailwinds and the need for scalable decarbonisation solutions are strongest.  

Find out more in our latest article in IPE Real Assets here: https://lnkd.in/grh8kBsf
authorHeadline:
QIC is one of Australia’s largest institutional investment managers, with A$135bn+ in assets under management.

Headquartered in Brisbane, we are a private markets specialist with deep fixed income and overlays expertise, investing across infrastructure, real estate, private equity, private debt and liquid markets.

We partner with approximately 125 institutional investors and government clients globally, applying disciplined judgment to deliver resilient performance over the long term.

With offices also in Sydney, Melbourne, New York, San Francisco, London and Singapore, we combine deep Australian heritage with global capability.

Established in 1991 to manage capital for the Queensland Government, QIC operates with legislated investment independence. Our sovereign ownership provides stability and accountability.

We remain deeply connected to our Queensland heritage and committed to delivering long-term economic benefits for Queenslanders.

Today, close to 1,000 professionals across our corporate and investment functions work together to create shared value by responsibly investing for the prosperity of clients, people and communities.

text:
The internationally recognised health, research and knowledge institutions at Brisbane's Boggo Road have united under one name: Boggo Road Innovation Junction.

We are proud to have led the development of the precinct identity in close collaboration with the Precinct Council, partners and key stakeholders. 

Home to a ~13,000-strong workforce and student community across 39 hectares of specialised life sciences and advanced manufacturing space, and attracting ~$270m annually in R&D investment, Boggo Road is the southern anchor of the Brisbane Knowledge Corridor. 

As master developer, we are co-creating the long-term renewal and connectivity of this globally relevant innovation cluster, building on 30 years of policy and investment in Queensland. 

Metro South Health, Queensland Health, The University of Queensland, QUT (Queensland University of Technology), Griffith University, CSIRO, Department of the Environment, Tourism, Science and Innovation, Department of Primary Industries (Queensland), Department of State Development, Infrastructure and Planning, Patheon by Thermo Fisher Scientific, Mater Research, Sanofi, Translational Research Institute Australia

text:
When severe storms caused widespread power outages in Silverton and Broken Hill, QIC portfolio company Tilt Renewables moved quickly to provide practical community support.

In this episode of Innovation in Infrastructure, QIC’s Oendrila Roy speaks with Tilt’s Head of Sustainability, Maja Barnett, and Senior Community and Stakeholder Engagement Advisor, Koel Wrigley about how Tilt provided immediate support when it mattered most, and how community-led engagement can build long-term resilience in regional areas.

Listen here: https://lnkd.in/gQKU7Tdm

We’re excited to announce key appointments to Quinbrook’s investment team as we further strengthen our global investment capability to capture the significant opportunities created by the energy transition. 

Tim Horneman joins as Managing Director in the Australian investment team. With more than 20 years’ experience Tim will play a critical role in advancing and executing our growing Australian project pipeline.

In the UK, Tom Wray has been appointed as a Senior Director. Tom brings deep power sector expertise spanning transaction origination, development, construction and asset management.

#EnergyTransition
authorHeadline:
Quinbrook is a specialist investment manager focused exclusively on the infrastructure needed to drive the energy transition. 

Led and managed by a senior team of energy industry professionals, Quinbrook is an accomplished asset creator and business builder that conceives, develops, constructs and operates a diverse array of innovative infrastructure projects and growth platforms. Since its founding in 2015, Quinbrook has invested in over 40 GW of energy infrastructure assets and businesses representing a total transaction value exceeding USD 27 billion spanning large-scale renewable power generation, biofuels production, long duration storage, hyperscale data centre infrastructure, AI powered asset optimisation, grid support and flexibility solutions in North America, the UK/Ireland and Australia.  Quinbrook's investment and asset management team has offices in New York, Houston, London and Brisbane.

text:
Supernode Stage 1 has commenced commercial operations!
 
Supernode Stage 1, a 260MW / 619MWh Battery Energy Storage System located in Brisbane, is now operating at full output to the Queensland electricity grid with the commercial tolling of Stage 1 under the management of Origin Energy.
 
Construction is ongoing for Stages 2 and 3 which are set to increase Supernode to 780MW / 3,074MWh. Once fully operational, the first three stages of Supernode will support the power needs of approximately 188,000 typical Queensland households during evening peak periods. Supernode is a case study in how large-scale storage can deliver long-term infrastructure assets that are purpose-built to support the next phase of power system evolution.
 
Read more: https://lnkd.in/eVX5Fq64

text:
Quinbrook recently attended the Net Zero Investor Annual DC Forum in London, where discussions focused on how policy reform and macroeconomic change are shaping approaches to net zero investing. 

Our Global Head of Sustainability and Impact, Hilkka Komulainen, highlighted the importance of adopting a longer-term perspective beyond short-term cost metrics to support effective net zero delivery, particularly through private markets like infrastructure and other long-duration assets that play a critical role in the energy transition.

text:
Quinbrook today announces the sale of its Flexitricity energy optimisation business to Drax Group. 

Quinbrook first acquired the next-generation flexible energy platform in 2020 and since then has more than doubled Flexitricity’s contracted portfolio of flexible energy assets from 540 MW to c. 1.3 GW of distributed capacity including battery storage, industrial demand response, and flexible generation. 

Flexitricity was and remains today a true pioneer of flexible energy solutions in the UK market and we are proud of our role supporting Flexitricity’s evolution as it expanded its innovative and tailored solutions to support a diverse range of customers in a complex energy landscape. We wish Andy Lowe and the Flex team continued success under Drax’s ownership.

Read more: https://lnkd.in/eDkSg_Ui

Since its founding in 2015, Quinbrook has invested in over 40 GW of energy infrastructure assets and businesses representing a total transaction value exceeding USD 27 billion spanning large-scale renewable power generation, biofuels production, long duration storage, hyperscale data centre infrastructure, AI powered asset optimisation, grid support and flexibility solutions in North America, the UK/Ireland and Australia.  Quinbrook's investment and asset management team has offices in New York, Houston, London and Brisbane.

text:
Quinbrook was pleased to host Hon Chris Bowen MP - Minister for Climate Change and Energy, Ali France MP - Member for Dickson and Mayor Peter Flannery, City of Moreton Bay at our Supernode site in Brendale, Queensland this week.

Supernode is one of Australia’s largest grid-connected battery energy storage projects and highlights the critical role large-scale batteries play in strengthening the electricity network and supporting Australia’s energy transition.

We’re to proud deliver infrastructure that supports local jobs, long-term investment, and energy security. Thank you to all involved for taking the time to visit.

#Infrastructure #BatteryStorage #EnergyTransition

text:
Schroders Greencoat Wins Infrastructure Manager of the Year at the Irish Pension Awards 2025

We’re honoured to be recognised as Infrastructure Manager of the Year at the Irish Pension Awards 2025.
 
This award reflects our long-standing commitment to building and managing energy transition infrastructure on behalf of investors as a dedicated sector specialist.
 
Importantly our work is about more than just renewables projects; it’s about making a positive impact by accelerating the energy transition. It is essential as we move forward in a world where energy security and the cost of energy is having a greater impact on our investors and societies than ever before. By investing in energy transition infrastructure, we are delivering secure, inflation protected returns for our institutional and wealth clients alike, whilst helping to create a future where energy is cheaper, greener and more secure for society as a whole.
 
A huge thank you to everyone who made this achievement possible. As one of the largest renewable infrastructure specialist investors in Europe, we’re proud to play a leading role in shaping Ireland’s energy future through resilient, long-term infrastructure.
 
#IrishPensionAwards #InfrastructureInvestment #EnergyTransition #Sustainability #SchrodersGreencoat
authorHeadline:
Schroders Greencoat is a specialist investment manager dedicated to renewable infrastructure, with over £8.5 billion under management. We aim to provide strong risk-adjusted returns for our investors, while also delivering positive environmental outcomes.
Founded in 2009, Schroders Greencoat has built a reputation for unwavering delivery of our investment propositions, as well as for innovation – our offerings have become the model for an entire industry sector. Our investments span the globe, with local expertise in the Europe, the UK and the US, and are managed across a range of publicly listed vehicles*, private market pooled funds, co-investment vehicles and Separate Managed Accounts. 
Renewable infrastructure is a multi-trillion dollar global sector that will grow by more than $100 billion a year over the next decade. Utilities, energy companies and infrastructure developers will build out the majority of this transition, but the scale of capital involved will require significant private financing. There exists a very large and growing pool of capital which is keen to own and operate these assets, but lacks the inhouse technical skills and sector expertise to manage the investments independently. Our purpose is to bridge this gap, by acting as a solution provider for investors, and providing the partnership, financial, technical and operational expertise to deliver secure incomes for our clients.
Acquired by Schroders plc in 2022, Schroders Greencoat is the fourth pillar of the group's private markets investment business, Schroders Capital.  Schroders Capital through Schroders Greencoat has the ambition to become a global leader in infrastructure and the alternative investment industry more broadly.
 
* Schroders Greencoat is the investment manager for two listed vehicles, Greencoat UK Wind PLC and Greencoat Renewables PLC.

text:
This time last week we were delighted to host our #investors and guest speakers to the 2025 Schroders Greencoat LLP Annual Investor Meeting.

The AIM was a great opportunity to connect with LPs, industry professionals, and stakeholders, while addressing some of the most important developments shaping the energy transition space today.  There were some super discussions ranging from #policy and #regulatorychange, advancements in #energystorage and #grid resilience, to the innovation accelerating the sector and the ability for a wider group of investors to access the market.

We hosted a group of investors at one of our district heating asset operated by Bring Energy at Battersea Power Station.  It was an excellent opportunity to witness the scale and ambition of Bring Energy, but also to see firsthand how investor capital has been put to work and how district heating is supporting the energy transition and regeneration of such an iconic London landmark. 

A sincere thank you to everyone who took part for your engagement and partnership - we are also immensely grateful to all of the home team for their time and dedication in bringing the event to life.  Special thanks to our guest speakers on the day of the AIM and the Bring Energy team for expertly running the site visit and sharing their expertise.

As always, Schroders Greencoat LLP is focused on delivering long-term value for our investors while delivering on the energy transition. We really appreciate your continued partnership and support.

#renewables #energytransition #infrastructure 

Minal Patel | Richard Nourse | Carvell Conduah | Francesca Geoghegan | Emma Finch | Karl Byrne | Gerard Reid | Matt Tingle | Steve Packwood | Mark Williamson | Caroline Still | Daniel Mushin | James Samworth | John Musk | Daniel McKeown | Rebecca Sedler FEI | Rebecca Beresford | Cameron Hepburn | Jamie Milne, CAIA |

Founded in 2009, Schroders Greencoat has built a reputation for unwavering delivery of our investment propositions, as well as for innovation – our offerings have become the model for an entire industry sector. Our investments span the globe, with local expertise in the Europe, the UK and the US, and are managed across a range of publicly listed vehicles*, private market pooled funds, co-investment vehicles and Separate Managed Accounts. 
Renewable infrastructure is a multi-trillion dollar global sector that will grow by more than $100 billion a year over the next decade. Utilities, energy companies and infrastructure developers will build out the majority of this transition, but the scale of capital involved will require significant private financing. There exists a very large and growing pool of capital which is keen to own and operate these assets, but lacks the inhouse technical skills and sector expertise to manage the investments independently. Our purpose is to bridge this gap, by acting as a solution provider for investors, and providing the partnership, financial, technical and operational expertise to deliver secure incomes for our clients.
Acquired by Schroders plc in 2022, Schroders Greencoat is the fourth pillar of the group's private markets investment business, Schroders Capital.  Schroders Capital through Schroders Greencoat has the ambition to become a global leader in infrastructure and the alternative investment industry more broadly.
 
* Schroders Greencoat is the investment manager for two listed vehicles, Greencoat UK Wind PLC and Greencoat Renewables PLC.

text:
Schroders Greencoat LLP has closed on a 50% ownership interest in JERA Nex’s 395MW US solar portfolio. This investment is comprised of two operating solar assets – the 300MW Oxbow Solar Farm, the largest solar farm in the state of Louisiana, and the 95MW Happy Solar Farm in Arkansas.

We are excited about this opportunity to expand across technologies and geographies, while partnering with JERA Nex.

Learn more about the news here: https://okt.to/zEuNDo
#RenewableEnergy #EnergyTransition #Solar #SchrodersGreencoat

text:
Schroders Greencoat welcomes the UK Government’s decision to reform the national market for electricity, and not introduce a Zonal market.
 
Billions of pounds of investment in wind, solar and grid infrastructure is needed to deliver the low-carbon and affordable power system the UK needs. Zonal pricing would have disrupted investment and increased the cost of capital for new projects. By ruling out zonal and focusing on national market reform instead, the negative impacts of zonal can be avoided and consumer costs kept to a minimum.
 
Schroders Greencoat has made the case for retaining and updating the national market throughout the REMA process. We look forward to working alongside other industry stakeholders and the Government to ensure that a reformed national market and a modern transmission charging regime delivers good outcomes for investors and consumers.

text:
Sixth Street is proud to announce a $500 million strategic financing facility with Beam Therapeutics to support the anticipated launch of ristoglogene autogetemcel (risto-cel) for sickle cell disease. 
 
We believe Beam has built a differentiated base editing platform with the potential to deliver precision genetic medicines that could make a meaningful impact for patients. Sixth Street is committed to partnering with innovative companies like Beam that are overcoming healthcare challenges and improving patient care. 
 
Read more about our partnership: http://spr.ly/6047hoGNR

text:
In a new episode of It's Not Magic's San Francisco season, Daniela Amodei, Co-Founder and President of Anthropic, sat down with Sixth Street Co-President David Stiepleman to discuss the rapidly evolving AI landscape at a very interesting moment.
 
As a leader at one of the world's fastest growing tech companies, Daniela shares insights on Anthropic's commitment to AI safety, their responsible scaling policy, and how they now serve over 300,000 business customers. A San Francisco native, she reflects on launching the company in her hometown and why the city's culture of innovation remains the ideal base for their new downtown headquarters.
 
The conversation dives into the "wonder and fear" of the current landscape, covering the pace of model development, the importance of regulation, preserving culture amid rapid growth, and why generalists and specialists need each other to succeed.

Listen to the full episode: https://lnkd.in/eQhssYYe

For the fourth consecutive year, Sixth Street Growth has been recognized as one of GrowthCap's 2025 Top Growth Equity Firms. Sixth Street Growth provides growth equity and bespoke, flexible financing solutions to mid- and late-stage growth companies, pairing deep sector expertise, strategic value creation resources, and an acceleration mindset to help founders and management teams meet company-driven growth objectives.
 
We're proud to partner with innovative leaders across the technology and software sectors and are grateful to our portfolio company leaders for their collaboration and trust. Read more: http://spr.ly/6043hPxac

R. Martin Chavez, Sixth Street Partner and Vice Chairman, joined CNBC Squawk Box Asia live from Singapore to discuss AI’s exponential growth, how it is transforming today’s economy, and how Sixth Street approaches AI’s proliferation with a long-term investment horizon.
 
AI has been around for decades, but as Marty points out, its current trajectory is creating an unprecedented demand for infrastructure and power. To learn more about how Sixth Street is navigating today’s evolving AI landscape, see below and watch Marty’s full interview here: https://lnkd.in/eUSJt4uQ

text:
Brandon Wohl first joined StepStone as a summer intern. Early on he was encouraged to take ownership, contributing to investment memos and models, presenting to committees and engaging with partners. The experience helped him build confidence, judgment, and leadership skills he continues to develop as a full-time Analyst. 

At StepStone, early-career professionals are supported as they step up, collaborate, and lead. Follow along for more early-career stories and updates on future opportunities.
authorHeadline: StepStone Group (Nasdaq: STEP) is a global private markets investment firm focused on providing customized investment solutions and advisory and data services to our clients. StepStone’s clients include some of the world’s largest public and private defined benefit and defined contribution pension funds, sovereign wealth funds, and insurance companies, as well as prominent endowments, foundations, family offices, and private wealth clients, which include high-net-worth and mass affluent individuals. StepStone partners with its clients to develop and build private market portfolios designed to meet their specific objectives across the private equity, infrastructure, private debt, and real estate asset classes.

text:
As we continue to expand access to global private markets, we’re excited to announce a new partnership between StepStone and Utmost Group for UK-based investors.

Through this collaboration, Utmost’s clients will gain access to StepStone’s full suite of evergreen private markets strategies, supporting the flexibility and diversification modern portfolios demand.

With growing interest from UK wealth managers in private markets, this partnership is designed to unlock long-term growth potential for British savers.

authorHeadline: StepStone Group (Nasdaq: STEP) is a global private markets investment firm focused on providing customized investment solutions and advisory and data services to our clients. text: Earlier this month, Stonepeak Senior Managing Director and Credit Partner, Ryan Roberge shared his perspectives on infrastructure credit at CFA Québec’s Le Rendez-Vous de la Finance 2026. Speaking on the Energy Transition panel, he discussed how increasing global investment needs for power generation combined with favorable macro tailwinds like data center expansion are fueling the growth of infrastructure credit geared towards energy security and progressing the energy transition. Sectors such as renewable energy, energy storage, microgrids and traditional gas-fired power generation are all seeing increased capital investment and driving the need for creative and tailored lending solutions. Read more about Stonepeak’s credit capabilities: https://lnkd.in/e_mmBwEh

text: Stonepeak’s Nicolò Zanotto, Managing Director and Head of Digital Infrastructure, Europe, recently joined the keynote panel at TMT Finance EMEA 2026. Joined by fellow industry leaders, he unpacked the evolving digital infrastructure landscape in the region, including which sectors are outperforming, how consolidation and capital rotation are reshaping the sector’s value equation, and how investors and operators are adapting their strategies in today’s environment. Nicolò also discussed what we’re seeing in Europe's tower market, highlighting Stonepeak’s recently announced agreement to acquire TeleTower, a leading tower operator in Lithuania and Latvia.

text:
Stonepeak and CMA CGM have teamed up to launch United Ports LLC, a groundbreaking U.S. joint venture, to acquire 10 major CMA CGM-operated port terminals worldwide, with Stonepeak investing $2.4 billion to acquire a 25% stake in the JV.

This transaction represents an exciting opportunity to invest in a high-quality portfolio of terminals that play a mission-critical role in global trade alongside one of the largest and most respected shipping and logistics groups in the world. We look forward to the beginning of a long-term relationship with CMA CGM to support this essential infrastructure.

📢 Für unseren neuen kotierten Geschäftsimmobilienfonds planen wir eine Erstemission mit einem Volumen von bis zu CHF 500 Millionen. Die Zeichnungsfrist läuft vom 9. bis 20. März 2026.  
 
Der Fonds steht sowohl qualifizierten als auch nicht-qualifizierten Anlegerinnen und Anlegern offen und bietet eine attraktive Möglichkeit, in ein breit diversifiziertes, qualitativ hochstehendes Portfolio aus Schweizer Geschäftsimmobilien zu investieren.

authorHeadline:
We support our clients in achieving their long-term investment objectives. This in turn helps their clients to plan long term and act in a financially self-determined manner.

We think long term and act responsibly. Drawing on our knowledge and experience of over 165 years in risk management, we develop future-oriented investment solutions. That includes the provision and active management of a broad range of investment solutions in the fixed income, real estate, equity, infrastructure and multi-asset investment classes. 


We are a leading European asset manager and institutional real estate asset manager with locations in Switzerland, France, Germany, Luxembourg, the UK, the Nordics and Italy.

text:
Our open-ended infrastructure fund exceeded 𝗘𝗨𝗥 𝟐𝟎𝟎 𝗺𝗶𝗹𝗹𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗶𝗻 𝗶𝗻𝘃𝗲𝘀𝘁𝗼𝗿 𝘀𝘂𝗯𝘀𝗰𝗿𝗶𝗽𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀 at the beginning of 2026. As one of the first ELTIF 2.0 funds, it enables private and institutional investors in certain countries of the EU and Norway, as well as qualified investors in Switzerland to invest in tomorrow’s infrastructure – an asset class that was previously reserved for institutional players. 

Over the past year, we have further enhanced the fund’s strategic diversification through targeted acquisitions that align with our long‑term investment approach and foster sustainable value creation. 

👇 Curious to learn more? Explore our latest investments below.

text:
📢 Die Erstemission und Kotierung unseres neuen Geschäftsimmobilienfonds an der SIX Swiss Exchange ist per 1. April 2026 geplant. Der Fonds steht sowohl qualifizierten als auch nicht-qualifizierten Anlegerinnen und Anlegern offen und bietet eine attraktive Möglichkeit, in ein breit diversifiziertes, qualitativ hochstehendes Portfolio aus Schweizer Geschäftsimmobilien zu investieren.  

Mit dem Erlös aus der Erstemission beabsichtigt der Immobilienfonds, ein bestehendes Liegenschaftsportfolio an attraktiven Lagen in Städten wie Zürich, Genf, Basel, Luzern, St. Gallen oder Lugano zu erwerben. Geplant ist eine Zeichnungsfrist vom 9. März bis 20. März 2026.

🗞️ Lesen Sie mehr zu Anlageziel, ESG-Ansatz und Kotierung des Fonds in unserer Medienmitteilung: https://lnkd.in/e4VbmUiy  
 
#Medienmitteilung #Geschäftsimmobilien

text:
We have been alerted to “Temasek Nexus AI”, an entity which claims to be a trading platform.
 
“Temasek Nexus AI” has various websites, some of which include fake endorsements of the platform by our CEO, Dilhan Pillay, and our former CEO, Ho Ching.
 
Temasek, Dilhan and Ho Ching are not associated with “Temasek Nexus AI” in any way. For avoidance of doubt, Temasek will never ask the public to invest on “Temasek Nexus AI”.

Temasek does not assume or accept responsibility for any transactions conducted with or by this entity.

We regularly publish alerts concerning detected scams, impersonations and misinformation at our newsroom.

Our Purpose “So Every Generation Prospers” guides us to make a difference for today’s and future generations. text:
At the CNA Summit held in Jakarta, our CIO Rohit Sipahimalani said there is a sense of excitement as most major markets are projecting double digit earnings, while noting that heightened uncertainty is likely to persist over the next few years.

He reiterated Temasek's focus on building a resilient portfolio that delivers steady, long‑term returns.

To this end, Temasek has been investing in companies with access to large domestic markets, as well as sectors like core‑plus infrastructure, and other asset classes which are less volatile.

Said Rohit: “It is tough to navigate but if you have the approach of resilience, have the approach that you want to have in things that are more uncertain, you have the flexibility to pivot, I think you can navigate the environment.”

Read more : https://tmsk.sg/70da5c
