text:
Temasek is pleased to be part of Singapore’s first voluntary Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) procurement trial, alongside the Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore (CAAS), the Sustainable Aviation Fuel Company Ltd. (SAFCo), Boston Consulting Group (BCG), Changi Airport Group, DBS Bank, GenZero, Google, OCBC,  Singapore Airlines and Scoot.  

By aggregating demand across the public and private sectors, this trial marks an important step towards building a scalable, integrated SAF ecosystem for Singapore’s air hub. 

Our Chief Financial Officer, Chin-Yee Png, shared: “Aviation remains one of the toughest sectors to decarbonise – and SAF is the clearest lever we have this decade. The challenge is scale and cost, and by catalysing demand for SAF through this trial, we can play a proactive role in driving the system-level change needed to advance real-world decarbonisation. Small actions, taken together, can unlock meaningful scale and accelerate the transition to a more resilient world in a tangible way.” 

Through partnerships like this, we hope to continue supporting solutions that drive systems-level change, contribute to real-world decarbonisation, and build a more resilient future.  

#SustainabilityTemasek #ClimateForwardTemasek #DecarbonisingAsia #SustainableAviation

text:
Here’s how our portfolio has grown through market cycles. 📈  

When we first set out on our T2010 roadmap in 2002, our portfolio stood at S$77b. From 2011 to 2020, it doubled from S$193b to S$381b. 

Fast forward to today – building on our T2020 strategy and advancing under our T2030 roadmap, our portfolio has reached S$434b. 

Each roadmap is shaped by lessons learnt over the decades, helping us navigate through market cycles and bounce back from setbacks and global disruptions.  

Learn how our portfolio has remained resilient and forward-looking, amidst global events and market shifts via https://lnkd.in/gKf6zU82. 🌍

text:
We welcome the next chapter for ST Telemedia Global Data Centres (STT GDC) with KKR and Singtel, whose complementary strengths and shared vision are expected to accelerate STT GDC’s expansion and reinforce its leadership in digital infrastructure. This transition reflects Temasek’s commitment to backing businesses that create enduring value and contribute to resilient, future-ready infrastructure for an AI driven world.

Said Ravi Lambah, our Head of Strategic Initiatives, "Over the past 12 years, ST Telemedia (STT) has been instrumental in shaping STT GDC into a leading global digital infrastructure platform. As a long-term shareholder of STT, Temasek has supported STT’s and STT GDC’s management teams and invested across multiple stages of STT’s growth journey, helping to build a strong foundation for STT GDC’s next wave of opportunities."

Read more in this article by The Straits Times: https://tmsk.sg/gv3

 #TemasekInvests #AI #DataCentres

text:
#Circularity is not just about recycling, it’s also about recovering value from what can’t be recycled. In land-scarce Singapore, Keppel Ltd.'s waste-to-energy plays a critical role: reducing landfill volume, generates electricity, and enabling metals recovery. 

Innovations like ash reuse and carbon capture, could further cut landfill pressure and accelerate climate goals.

Learn how waste-to-energy is shaping a more sustainable future. https://tmsk.sg/5fbe44  
#ClimateForwardTemasek #BurningQuestions #wastetoenergy #circularity

text:
Our Head of Emerging Technologies, Russell Tham, shared his perspectives on how manufacturing companies can scale with rapid #AI adoption at this year’s Manufacturing Day Summit.

Organised by the Singapore Manufacturing Federation, the Summit was attended by more than 1,000 guests from corporates, government agencies, and embassies.

In a world being transformed by rapid technological changes, Russell underscored the urgency for companies to strive for deeper understanding,  move faster than the competition, innovate boldly, and redesign workflows for the future. At Temasek, we do things today with tomorrow in mind. Our commitment to emerging technologies enable us to shape a resilient and forward-looking portfolio, #SoEveryGenerationProspers.

A better brew. A more inclusive future.

text:
Our first issue of The Catalyst for the year is here. This month, we explore how Temasek’s portfolio has transformed from its early days as a Singapore holding company into a global investment company, and how we’re navigating today’s dynamic investment landscape by identifying promising opportunities across sectors and markets.
 
In this issue you’ll find:

 ✅ An interactive chart of our portfolio’s evolution across the decades

 ✅ How we’re building resilience through AI, Core‑plus Infrastructure, and Alternative Assets

✅ A closer look at India and why it remains one of our best-performing markets in the past decade
 
 Read and subscribe to The Catalyst now!

authorHeadline: Tiger Infrastructure Partners is an innovative private equity firm focused on providing transformational growth capital to middle market infrastructure companies. Tiger Infrastructure’s value-add approach targets growth investments across the Digital Infrastructure, Energy Transition and Transportation sectors in North America and Europe, where Tiger Infrastructure believes strong tailwinds are driving demand for new infrastructure.  Tiger Infrastructure maintains offices in New York and London.

text: Congratulations to Tiger Infrastructure Partners’ portfolio company 11:11 Systems on the successful acquisition of Digital Sense, a Brisbane‑based cloud and managed services provider previously owned by Aussie Broadband. The transaction provides Digital Sense customers with a stable, long‑term home on 11:11’s resilient cloud platform while supporting their modernization needs amid changes in the VMware Cloud Service Provider ecosystem. This acquisition further strengthens 11:11 Systems’ presence in Australia and reinforces its continued investment across the broader APAC region.

text: Congratulations to Tiger Infrastructure Partners’ portfolio company ELM Utility Services (“ELM”) on securing a major contract with Charter Communications, marking a significant expansion of ELM’s operations across nine states. Under this new partnership, ELM will support Charter’s growing infrastructure needs in both existing and newly launched service territories, with plans to add approximately 500 employees to strengthen service delivery across the region. This milestone underscores ELM’s continued growth and its commitment to providing safe, reliable, and high-quality utility services nationwide.

text: Congratulations to Tiger Infrastructure Partners’ portfolio company Qwello on securing a financing agreement with Banque des Territoires, the local arm of Groupe Caisse des Dépôts committed to the development of sustainable mobility in France. text:
At the recent Pensions Bridge Private Credit conference, TJ Durkin, Partner and Head of Asset Based Finance, discussed the institutional drivers and shifts currently shaping the asset-based finance landscape.

In evaluating the current opportunity set, TJ emphasized the importance of maintaining pricing discipline and avoiding niche opportunities because they’re not favorable for pricing. He also noted that the market has become increasingly sophisticated as borrowers are now doing diligence on GPs to ensure they have a clear understanding of who their lenders will be.

As the market continues to institutionalize, TPG remains focused on delivering asset-backed solutions grounded in deep fundamental research and long-term partnership.

Vauban Infrastructure Partners a le plaisir de participer à la 1ʳᵉ édition de l’Université de l’Énergie de Demain, organisée par Mouvement Impact France, le 12 février 2026 à La Caserne, Paris, aux côtés des acteurs clés de la transition énergétique en France.
 
🎙️ Olivier Guerrini, Directeur d’Investissement chez Vauban Infrastructure Partners, interviendra lors du grand débat de 16h00 à 17h00, aux côtés de Cristian MONTOYA, Agnès Pannier-Runacher, Claire PEDINI et Thomas Veyrenc, pour décrypter les enjeux, les opportunités et les conditions de succès d’une transformation désormais incontournable.
 
Les échanges porteront notamment sur l’électrification des usages, la décarbonation de l’industrie, la rénovation énergétique et le rôle clé des investissements dans les infrastructures.
 
🎟️Réservez votre pass dès à présent: https://lnkd.in/e-nkyuR9
 
***************
 
Vauban Infrastructure Partners is pleased to take part in the inaugural edition of the Université de l’Énergie de Demain, organised by Mouvement Impact France, on 12 February 2026 at La Caserne, Paris, bringing together key stakeholders of France’s energy transition.
 
🎙️ Olivier Guerrini, Investment Director at Vauban Infrastructure Partners, will speak during the main debate from 16:00 to 17:00, engaging in discussion with Cristian MONTOYA, Agnès Pannier-Runacher, Claire PEDINI and Thomas Veyrenc on the challenges, opportunities and success factors of this increasingly unavoidable transformation.
 
Their discussion will cover electrification of uses, industrial decarbonisation, energy-efficient building renovation, and the central role of infrastructure investment in supporting a lasting transition.
 
🎟️ Secure your pass now: https://lnkd.in/e-nkyuR9

Vauban Infrastructure Partners commits to industry partners, users and public authorities through a long-term approach to investing. Vauban’s team of experts identifies investments that have a strong growth potential and are resilient in the face of uncertainties and risks, and ensure their profitability in the long-term.

text:
📣 We’re pleased to announce that Vauban Infrastructure Partners, through its dedicated digital infrastructure platform Vauban Infra Fibre (VIF), has successfully completed the acquisition of Towerlink France SAS, a leading urban edge data center platform in France, formerly part of Cellnex’s portfolio.
 
A pioneer of the French fibre market for over a decade, Vauban Infrastructure Partners has since established itself as one of the leading investors in digital infrastructure, in France and on the international stage. VIF brings together a diversified portfolio of brownfield assets and plays a central role in supporting the development of resilient, high-quality digital infrastructure nationwide.
 
Founded in 2018, Towerlink (now rebranded as Société D’Exploitation et de Gestion d’Infrastructures Link – Segilink) owns, operates and develops urban edge data centres across multiple sites in France, many of which are already operational. Located close to end users in Tier 1 and Tier 2 cities, these assets support the acceleration of local digital transformation and regional economic development.
 
This transaction further strengthens VIF’s leading position in the French digital infrastructure market and reinforces its long-standing relationship with Bouygues Telecom, Towerlink’s main client and one of France’s leading telecom operators.
 
Steve Ledoux, Maxime BUISSON, Céline Filho, Youcef Barrage, Anne-Sophie Leurent , Maxime Sion, Aida Zahiri

text:
🛤️  Sustainable Investment in Green Transport
 
Green transport infrastructure is central to the transition towards more efficient and sustainable societies. As highlighted in a recent keynote interview with Christoph Stagl, Investment Director and Partner of Vauban Infrastructure Partners, the modernization and electrification of road, rail, and waterways are unlocking a wide range of investment opportunities across Europe.
 
“Many transport networks are still operating with older, less efficient assets, which creates a large potential for electrification and fleet renewal” comments Christoph. 
 
From railway and inland shipping electrification to the gradual adoption of hybrid and electric truck fleets, momentum is clearly building. While government support can play an important role in accelerating early adoption, long-term success ultimately depends on robust business models, reliable contractual frameworks and proven technologies.
 
Vauban Infrastructure Partners’s investment approach combines leasing and operatorship models, enabling efficient fleet management and long-term partnerships with public authorities. In addition, the integration of renewable energy, grid infrastructure and intermodal connectivity is key to strengthening the resilience and sustainability of green transport networks. Looking ahead challenges such as regulatory consistency, permitting timelines and supply chain constraints remain. Yet with a disciplined investment approach and a focus on tangible, long-lived assets, the outlook for green transport infrastructure remains compelling.

#Infrastructure #Sustainability #GreenMobiity

text:
✨  Vauban Infrastructure Partners is pleased to welcome Olivier Guerrini as Investment Director in our Paris Office.
 
Olivier brings a wealth of expertise across the broad range of the sector in energy transition complementing Vauban Infrastructure Partners’ existing capabilities. He will play a key role in the origination process across Europe, leveraging on his network and expertise to identify and secure high-potential investment opportunities within the Vauban's Value-Add Transition Strategy and broader infrastructure sectors through strategic bilateral partnerships. 
 
Olivier is a strong addition to the Vauban Infrastructure Partners’ Investment Team, composed already of around 40 individuals based in Paris, France and Munich, Germany. Olivier is an energy sector leader with +20 years of global experience across major utilities and IPPs. He has a proven track-record in P&L general management, origination, development, financing, O&M and scaling business units. He benefits from a deep expertise in Renewables, Green Gases and PowerFlex-type energy solutions.
 
Gwenola Chambon and Mounir Corm, Founding Partners of Vauban Infrastructure Partners Infrastructure Partner, commented: "We are delighted to welcome Olivier to Vauban Infrastructure Partners. His deep experience across the energy transition sectors and his ability to build and integrate new business lines will strengthen our investment platform. Olivier will support the Vauban’s efforts to originate high quality opportunities and partner with stakeholders to deliver resilient, long- term value for the communities we serve."
 
Olivier Guerrini, Investment Director at Vauban Infrastructure Partners says “I am honored to join Gwenola Chambon and Mounir Corm, as well as the exceptional teams at Vauban Infrastructure Partners, with whom I share strong human values and a deep environmental and social commitment. I am eager to bring my +20 years’ experience in Energy Industry to Vauban and carry out the implementation of the ‘Transition Value-Add’ strategy of Vauban and to actively contribute to accelerating Vauban’s investments in the energy and digital transition.”  

Please find more details in the press releases: 
- English Version: https://lnkd.in/eRKch3Rd
- French Version: https://lnkd.in/e6HVqEfE

text:
👉 Création de Valeur : Refinancement Infra de Proxiserve
 
Merci au magazine Nextstep d'avoir mis en valeur le refinancement de PROXISERVE conduit par les équipes de Vauban Infrastructure Partners et de Proxiserve en début d'année 2025. 
 
En effet, nous avons annoncé en début d'année 2025, le refinancement historique de 606 M€ pour Proxiserve, leader français des compteurs intelligents, moteurs de la transition énergétiques des bâtiments. Ce refinancement renforce la capacité de Proxiserve à investir dans l’efficacité énergétique et à financer son expansion en France et en Europe, expliquent de concert Vincent CASSAGNEs, Directeur d'Investissement et Associé de Vauban Infrastructure Partners, Bruno LARRIEU, Directeur Financier de Proxiserve et Eric Villateau, Associé de DLA Piper.
 
Vauban Infrastructures Partners, via des fonds d'investissement sous sa gestion, co-contrôlait Proxiserve à compter de 2019, et en détient le contrôle exclusif depuis 2022. Par cet investissement dans Proxiserve, Vauban Infrastructures Partners soutient la transition energétique en France et en Europe, à travers une vision à long-terme. 
 
👉 Value Creation: PROXISERVE Infra Refinancing
 
Thank you to Nextstep magazine for highlighting the refinancing of Proxiserve, led by the teams at Vauban Infrastructure Partners and Proxiserve at the beginning of 2025.
 
At the start of 2025, we announced a historic €606 million refinancing for Proxiserve, the French leader in smart meters and a key driver of the energy transition in buildings. This refinancing strengthens Proxiserve’s ability to invest in energy efficiency and fund its expansion in France and Europe, as jointly explained by Vincent CASSAGNE, Investment Director and Partner at Vauban Infrastructure Partners, Bruno LARRIEU, CFO of Proxiserve, and Eric Villateau, Partner at DLA Piper.
 
Since 2019, Vauban Infrastructure Partners (via investment funds under its management) has been a co-controlling shareholder of Proxiserve and has exercised sole control since 2022. Through this investment in Proxiserve, Vauban supports the energy transition in France and Europe with a long-term vision.

#Infrastructure #ValueCreation #Sustainable

text:
Earlier this month, Gwen Colin, Head of ESG and Partner at Vauban Infrastructure Partners, participated in a panel at the PEI Responsible Investment Forum Europe 2025, moderated by Carmela Mondino Borromeo, ESG Advisor at Borromeo Mondino Advisory GmbH, alongside Joost Bergsma (Nuveen Infrastructure), and Catherine Edet (STOA Infra and Energy).
 
The discussion, titled “Transition to Transformation: The Social Impact of Renewables,” examined how renewable energy investments can combine economic performance with social responsibility. Panelists discussed the impact of tariff-based frameworks on procurement and supply chains, the role of sustainable procurement in driving operational discipline, the importance of ensuring assets demonstrate net zero behavior in practice, and the challenge of addressing not in my backyard dynamics in local communities.
 
Gwen emphasized the shift of ESG from a reporting exercise to a strategic and operational tool within infrastructure investment:
“Sustainability is no longer just a matter of data management discussion among committed ESG managers; it has become a tool to empower investment teams and industrial operators to implement meaningful actions that enhance the long-term resilience of their assets. It also serves as a reliable compass to identify those who previously used it as a communication tool during its hype phase.”
 
The panel highlighted that the credibility of the energy transition now depends on execution at asset level, disciplined governance, and the ability to align investors, operators, and communities around shared long-term outcomes.

Vision Ridge Partners, LLC is pleased to support the launch of GSSG Chikuden, a utility-scale battery storage developer dedicated to accelerating the Japanese energy transition, with a US$400M commitment. 
 
Established in partnership with GSSG Solar, LLC, GSSG Chikuden will seek to leverage its management team’s extensive boots on-the-ground experience, broad network, and history of energy project development across Japan’s power markets.
 
“We are pleased to expand our decade-long relationship with GSSG Solar with the launch of GSSG Chikuden. We think battery storage assets form a crucial part of the emerging sustainable energy economy globally, and are confident that GSSG Chikuden’s management team, project development experience, and regional relationships will enable it to deliver much-needed energy storage assets to the grid in Japan,” said Reuben Munger, Managing Partner and Chief Investment Officer of Vision Ridge.

text:
Wafra is pleased to participate in the PTC’2026 conference taking place in Honolulu, Hawaii. Michael Coleman, Managing Director in Real Assets & Infrastructure, will join the panel “Digital Infrastructure at Scale: Financing the AI Revolution”.

Michael will be featured alongside Madonna Park of Switch, Wes Cummins of Applied Digital, Jonathan Mauck of DigitalBridge, and Brandon Amber of Palladium Partners, to discuss the rise of AI mega-campuses, energy partnerships, and evolving capital strategies.

text:
As we celebrate our 40th anniversary, we are honored to mark this milestone in a way that reflects the values that have shaped our firm. We are proud of our longstanding partnership with Make-A-Wish Metro New York and Western New York, and this year we had the privilege of granting our 40th wish for a child facing a critical illness—a milestone that underscores our commitment to creating impact beyond business.

We invite you to watch the short clip below and join us in celebrating our 40th anniversary and the power of a wish.

text:
Wafra is proud to reinforce our commitment to education and community through our annual “Wafra Gives Back” Day of Service.

In partnership with New York Cares and PS1X – The Courtlandt School, over 40 colleagues dedicated their time to supporting students and enhancing the school environment. From painting classrooms and revitalizing outdoor learning spaces to facilitating interactive math and reading activities, our volunteers worked together to help create a more engaging and welcoming setting for the PS1X community.

We appreciate the opportunity to support PS1X and are grateful for our employees’ continued dedication to making a positive and lasting impact.

text: Congratulations to the SeaCube Cold Solutions team and the partnership they have created with the Wonderful Company to expand the reach of SeaCube's portable cold storage business into California's Central Valley, Arizona, and Nevada. Portable cold storage uses refrigerated containers to eliminate the need to transport goods to and from distant cold storage facilities, simplifying operations and reducing handling costs. SeaCube provides easy set up, adaptability to meet seasonal storage needs, and reduced operating expenses for their customers. We look forward to seeing many successes!
authorHeadline: Wren House was established in 2013 as the direct infrastructure investment arm of the Kuwait Investment Authority. Initially set up as a local firm focusing on core infrastructure investments in Europe, we have evolved with a core and core plus global OECD mandate and a strong focus on North America and Europe. Throughout, we have focussed on delivering attractive risk-adjusted returns across private equity and structured solutions through a disciplined investment approach over a long-term horizon, and responsible active asset management.

text:
Last week, we brought our ~40 infrastructure colleagues together for our annual Wren House offsite in Hertfordshire. It was a great opportunity to:

🔭 Reflect on the market opportunity, discuss our evolving investment strategy and specifically discuss where to prioritise our investment efforts.

📈 Hone our value creation plans across the portfolio and share best practices. A special thanks to our external speakers John Beaumont from Bain & Company, Olivier Beau de Loménie from West Monroe, and Michael McKessar CEO of Voyage Care Ltd for taking time out of their day to join us for an insightful discussion on Digital Transformation.

🥇 Spend some time having fun and discovering hidden skills whether around a Karaoke mic or getting way too competitive with Ferret races, Duck herding and Zorb bowling!

Thanks to all for your energy, contributions, good sense of humour and going the extra mile for your team! Way to go!

Velocity FBO Network, our North American network of fixed-base operators, today announced the acquisition of BTR Jet Center, the leading FBO serving Baton Rouge Metropolitan Airport (KBTR) in Louisiana.

With this third acquisition and fifth location, Velocity FBO Network marks another key milestone in building a nationwide footprint of customer-centric FBO locations.

authorHeadline: Tallvine Partners is an independent investment advisor focused on investing in value-add middle-market infrastructure opportunities. Based in Miami, FL, and led by partners Thomas Lefebvre, Chucri Hjeily, Mark Clark and Victor Sosa, Tallvine focuses on middle-market infrastructure opportunities across the energy and utilities, transportation and logistics, and communications sectors in North America. Tallvine seeks to invest in target sectors by leveraging the firm’s strong and cohesive partnership, its experience building, enhancing and divesting platforms, its ability to identify and empower management teams, its wide-reaching network of experts, and the pattern recognition its four partners have developed investing through two decades of business cycles in each of the firm’s target sectors.

text:
Tallvine has announced the launch of its North American critical marine infrastructure platform in conjunction with the acquisition of Donjon Marine (“Donjon Marine”), based in New Jersey with operations across the U.S. The acquisition marks the creation of Tallvine’s third platform.

Donjon Marine is a leader in critical marine infrastructure with a comprehensive suite of essential solutions for the operation and maintenance of North America’s waterways, coastal regions, and harbor infrastructure. With a large, specialized fleet and a skilled team, Donjon Marine has been essential in maintaining navigable waterways and accessibility to key harbor infrastructure, as well as responding to maritime emergencies.

We look forward to working with the Donjon Marine Co., LLC team to expand our geographic reach and service offerings, drive the growth and modernization of our fleet, and execute strategic acquisitions in the sector.

Tallvine has announced the launch of its North American critical marine infrastructure platform in conjunction with the acquisition of Donjon Marine (“Donjon Marine”), based in New Jersey with operations across the U.S. The acquisition marks the creation of Tallvine’s third platform.

Donjon Marine is a leader in critical marine infrastructure with a comprehensive suite of essential solutions for the operation and maintenance of North America’s waterways, coastal regions, and harbor infrastructure. With a large, specialized fleet and a skilled team, Donjon Marine has been essential in maintaining navigable waterways and accessibility to key harbor infrastructure, as well as responding to maritime emergencies.

We look forward to working with the Donjon Marine Co., LLC team to expand our geographic reach and service offerings, drive the growth and modernization of our fleet, and execute strategic acquisitions in the sector.

We are excited to share that Tallvine has acquired TRG Datacenters ("TRG"), a Houston-based data center platform serving enterprise, hosting, cloud, and Al customers.

TRG brings a strong operational foundation and a growing footprint, with significant expansion potential in Houston and other major Texas and U.S. metro markets. We're thrilled to partner with Chris Hinkle and the TRG Datacenters team as they enter their next phase of growth.

Together, we'll continue supporting the platform's retail and wholesale colocation customers-and help power next-generation workloads, including Al inferencing.
